description: Source of truth mapping Numista IDs to coin metadata and local image paths.

68396.name: 2 Euros (Council of Europe)
68396.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
68396.obverse_description: Coat of arms of Andorra in the centre-left with number "20" where the zero is stylised to represent the Council of Europe's flag. Coin’s outer ring bearing the twelve stars of the European Union.
68396.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

68395.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders

96326.obverse_description: Microphone and parabolic antenna (representing radio and television) circled by several circular lines. Coin's outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
96326.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

124386.name: 2 Euros (Anthem of Andorra)
124386.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
124386.obverse_description: Partial reproduction of the anthem of Andorra published in 1914. The central part reproduces the first notes of the anthem flanked by an ornamentation of floral style. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
124386.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

124387.name: 2 Euros (The Pyrenean country)
124387.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
124387.obverse_description: On its upper part a triangle, consisting of three undulating strips representing a simplified version of the map of the country. These three elements make up the brand owned by the Government of Andorra, which is used to provide a uniform, consistent and coordinated image to all graphic communication and, at the same time, achieve an unmistakable and instant identification. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
124387.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

152661.name: 2 Euros (Universal Declaration of Human Rights)
152661.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
152661.obverse_description: Seven staircases (representing the seven parishes or administrative divisions of Andorra) in the shape of mountains that lead to the valley. These staircases are, at the same time, the branches of a tree symbolizing humankind. 30 leaves come out of the branches of this tree representing the 30 articles of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
152661.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

133778.name: 2 Euros (Constitution of Andorra)
133778.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
133778.obverse_description: "Monument to the men and women of Andorra who wanted the Constitution", located in the Plaza of the General Council (Parliament of Andorra). To the right of the monument is the map of Andorra with its motto in the interior. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
133778.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

184042.name: 2 Euros (General Council of the Valleys)
184042.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
184042.obverse_description: Upper part of the entry portal of "Casa de la Vall" with the coat of arms of Andorra. Two compact groups of human faces on both sides of the design. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
184042.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

162097.name: 2 Euros (Alpine Ski World Cup Finals)
162097.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
162097.obverse_description: Skier sliding down a slope. In the background, four curved lines, from the official logo of these Ski World Cup Finals (11-17/03/2019), represent the slopes where the competition will take place. Some snowflakes around and coin’s outer ring depicting the twelve stars of the European flag.
162097.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

232060.name: 2 Euros (Universal Women's Suffrage)
232060.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
232060.obverse_description: Female face inside a wrapper of endless moving lines. These lines are formed by female names in Catalan language close together, making it difficult to read them individually, as a tribute to solidarity of women in the fight for their rights. There is one repeated name only, that of ‘VICTORIA’ and symbolises the victory of gaining the right to vote. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
232060.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

215401.name: 2 Euros (Ibero-American Summit)
215401.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
215401.obverse_description: Tree made up by human silhouettes and tine wheels. The human silhouettes symbolize the integration of society, culture and education for the realization of a sustainable future. The tine wheels symbolize the synergy of movement between the ideas and proposals of participants to this Summit. Next to the tree, there is the logo of this 27th Summit. On its upper side, three triangles represent the colours of the Andorran flag. On its lower side, six triangles represent Andorra’s opening towards Sustainable Development Goals. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
215401.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

302814.name: 2 Euros (Our Lady of Meritxell)
302814.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
302814.obverse_description: Foreground depicts the reproduction of the Romanesque carving of Our Lady of Meritxell (patron saint of the Principality of Andorra), which dates back from the 11th and 12th centuries. The background of the design shows a partial reproduction of the basilica sanctuary where it is located and a graphic element symbolizing a flower. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
302814.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

302815.name: 2 Euros (Old People in Covid-19 Pandemic)
302815.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
302815.obverse_description: The subject of the coin is symbolized by the image of the hand of a young person holding another hand that shows all the signs of aging, with a stethoscope below them. Several reproductions of the SARSCoV-2 virus represents the State’s commitment to preventing its spread and caring for its citizens. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag
302815.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

344732.name: 2 Euros (The Legend of Charlemagne)
344732.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
344732.obverse_description: In the background, a landscape with mountains and a river, representing the rich scenery of Andorra. The foreground depicts a partial reproduction of the well-known portrait of Emperor Charlemagne by the artist Albrecht Dürer. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
344732.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

344733.name: 2 Euros (Currency Agreement between Andorra and EU)
344733.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
344733.obverse_description: The different shapes and sizes of the puzzle pieces depicted in the lower part of the design symbolize the Principality of Andorra and the countries belonging to the European Union. In the upper part of the design, the stars encircling the symbol of the common European currency represent that they are all part of the euro universe. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
344733.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

372758.name: 2 Euros (Admission of Andorra to the UN)
372758.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
372758.obverse_description: Number 30 represented by two symbolic elements: a crown of olive branches and a globe. At the top of the design appears the coat of arms of Andorra. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
372758.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

372756.name: 2 Euros (Summer solstice fire festival)
372756.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
372756.obverse_description: Human figure, called “fallaire”, dressed in a traditional cape and drawing a circle of fire with the “falla” (main element of the festival and consists of tree barks which, once ignited, are energetically spun creating large circles of fire). Graphic composition of lines on the background representing the summer solstice. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
372756.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

427530.name: 2 Euros (Skiing in Andorra)
427530.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
427530.obverse_description: Dynamic scene on the piste featuring a skier making a left turn, skiing toward the right edge of the image, and kicking up snow towards the top left. The skier's leg with skis and right hand with a ski pole are still visible. Coin's outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
427530.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

427536.name: 2 Euros (Mountain Bike World Championships)
427536.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
427536.obverse_description: Mountain biker on a rapid descent. Coin's outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
427536.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

482934.name: 2 Euros (Games of the Small States of Europe)
482934.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
482934.obverse_description: Bouncing balls from various sports (basketball, soccer, tennis, rugby). Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
482934.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

482937.name: 2 Euros (Bearded vulture)
482937.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
482937.obverse_description: Head of Bearded vulture facing right. Coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
482937.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

64.name: 2 Euros (1st map)
64.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
64.obverse_description: A portrait of the radical pacifist Bertha von Suttner, a symbol of Austria's efforts over many decades to support peace, is accompanied with the face value
64.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the fifteen states of the European Union

2193.name: 2 Euros (Austrian State Treaty)
2193.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2193.obverse_description: The centre of the coin shows a reproduction of the signatures and seals in the Austrian State Treaty, which was signed by the foreign ministers and ambassadors of the Soviet Union, the United Kingdom, the United States and France, and by Leopold Figl, Foreign Minister of Austria, in May 1955. The inscription ‘50 JAHRE STAATSVERTRAG' appears above the seals and the year of issue, 2005, below them. The vertical stripes in the background represent Austria's national colours (red-white-red). The outer ring of the coin features the 12 stars of the European Union.
2193.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2163.name: 2 Euros (Treaty of Rome)
2163.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2163.obverse_description: The coin shows the Treaty document signed by the six founding countries on a background evoking the paving (designed by Michelangelo) of the Piazza del Campidoglio in Rome, which took place on 25 March 1957. ‘Treaty of Rome 50 years’, ‘EUROPE’, and the name of the issuing country appear in the respective languages of the euro area or in Latin. Thus, the legend differs from country to country, but the image is the same. The Treaty of Rome established the European Economic Community and ultimately led to the introduction of the euro in 1999 and the euro banknotes and coins in 2002. The anniversary was celebrated on 25 March 2007. The euro area countries have marked the occasion by jointly issuing this commemorative coin. Its design was selected following a competition organized by the European mints. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
2163.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

9761.name: 2 Euros (2nd map)
9761.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
9761.obverse_description: A portrait of the radical pacifist Bertha von Suttner, a symbol of Austria's efforts over many decades to support peace, is accompanied with the face value and heraldic hatchings representing the Austrian flag and is encircled by the twelve stars of Europe
9761.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the gathering of the fifteen states of the European Union

5055.name: 2 Euros (Economic and Monetary Union)
5055.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5055.obverse_description: Stick figure which merges into the € symbol. Twelve stars of the European Union on outer ring.
5055.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28191.name: 2 Euros (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28191.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28191.obverse_description: Coin features the world in the form of a euro symbol in the centre, showing how the euro has become a true global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolise the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28191.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

77180.name: 2 Euros (European Union Flag)
77180.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
77180.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
77180.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

80926.name: 2 Euros (Anniversary of the National Bank)
80926.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
80926.obverse_description:
The Österreichische Nationalbank was started in 1816 by Johann Philipp Stadion, Count von Warthausen in the reign of Francis I (Franz I.), the first Emperor of Austria. The foundation of this bank followed a period of high currency devaluation in Austria during the war times when paper money was issued by the Habsburg state.

The design depicts two gods of Roman mythology featured in the carved relief above the entrance to the main building of the Oesterreichische Nationalbank.

To the left, Mercury, the messenger of the gods and the god of merchants and commerce; to the right, Fortuna, the goddess of fate and prosperity, who is portrayed with a horn of plenty. The background picture, running from the left to the right edges of the core, shows the OeNB’s main premises. An ornamental band hugging the bottom of the coin centre evokes the red-white-red pattern of the Austrian national flag, with hatched vertical lines symbolizing the colour red, as laid down in the rules of heraldic design.

The years ‘1816’ and ‘2016’ to the left of Mercury refer to the OeNB’s founding year and its bicentennial. The inscriptions are running along the edges of the gold-coloured centre.
80926.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

131199.name: 2 Euros (Republic of Austria)
131199.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
131199.obverse_description: In its brass core, the coin shows a detail of the statue of Pallas Athena that stands in front of the neoclassical Parliament building in Vienna. The goddess of wisdom wears an Attic helmet decorated with a sphinx. Both the Parliament (completed in 1883) and the Pallas Athena fountain (1902) were based on designs by Theophil von Hansen. Pallas Athena serves as a symbol of Austrian parliamentarianism and signifies knowledge, reason and strategic ability.
131199.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

331112.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
331112.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
331112.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
331112.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

80.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (1st map, 1st type, 1st portrait)
80.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
80.obverse_description: The portrait of King Albert II facing left encircled by the 12 stars of Europe in the outer ring, among which is situated his monogram (a capital "A" with the Roman numeral "II" underneath a crown)
80.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the fifteen states of the European Union

2165.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Belgium-Luxembourg Economic Union)
2165.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2165.obverse_description: The effigies of Grand Duke Henri of Luxembourg and King Albert II of Belgium are depicted in profile (from left to right) in the center of the coin, above the year of issue, 2005. The engraver's initials, ‘LL', appear to the lower right. The two effigies and the date are surrounded by the outer ring bearing the 12 stars of the European Union and the monograms of Grand Duke Henri on the left and of King Albert II on the right. The mintmarks appear between two stars near the bottom of the coin.
2165.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2166.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Brussels Atomium)
2166.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2166.obverse_description: An image of the Atomium in the center of the coin and the engraver’s initials to right with two mintmarks near the base. The outer ring of the coin features the 12 stars of the European Union, the letter ‘B’ and the year of mintage ‘2006’.
2166.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

6292.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (2nd map, 1st type, 1st portrait)
6292.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
6292.obverse_description: Portrait of King Albert II facing left
6292.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the 15 states of the European Union

2167.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Treaty of Rome)
2167.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2167.obverse_description: The coin shows the Treaty document signed by the six founding countries on a background evoking the paving (designed by Michelangelo) of the Piazza del Campidoglio in Rome, where the signing took place on 25 March 1957. ‘Treaty of Rome 50 years’, ‘EUROPE’, and the name of the issuing country appear in the respective languages of the euro area or in Latin. Thus, the legend differs from country to country, but the image is the same. The Treaty of Rome established the European Economic Community and ultimately led to the introduction of the euro in 1999 and the euro banknotes and coins in 2002. The anniversary was celebrated on 25 March 2007. The euro area countries have marked the occasion by jointly issuing this commemorative coin. Its design was selected following a competition organized by the European mints. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
2167.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the 15 states of the European Union

3196.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Human Rights)
3196.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
3196.obverse_description: The coin shows curved lines around a rectangle marked with the figure 60. The year ‘2008’ is inscribed above the rectangle and the words ‘UNIVERSAL DECLARATION OF HUMAN RIGHTS’ underneath it. The name of the country appears below the design in its three official languages: ‘BELGIE – BELGIQUE – BELGIEN’. The mintmark and the signature mark of the Master of the Mint are shown to the left and right of the design respectively. The outer ring of the coin features the twelve stars of the European Union.
3196.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

6293.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (2nd map, 2nd type, 2nd portrait)
6293.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
6293.obverse_description: The bust of Albert II facing left with the date below flanked by privy marks, his monogram to the right, and the 12 stars of Europe in the outer ring
6293.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the 15 states of the European Union

5056.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Economic and Monetary Union)
5056.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5056.obverse_description: Stick figure which merges into the € symbol. Twelve stars of the European Union on outer ring.
5056.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

8070.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Louis Braille)
8070.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
8070.obverse_description: The coin features a portrait of Louis Braille with his initials, L and B, in the alphabet that he designed. His name appears above the portrait and, below it, the code of the issuing country, ‘BE’ and the dates ‘1809’ and ‘2009’. To the left and right respectively are the mint mark and the signature mark of the Master of the Mint. The coin’s outer ring shows the 12 stars of the European Union.
8070.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

6311.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (2nd map, 2nd type, 1st portrait)
6311.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
6311.obverse_description: The bust of Albert II facing left with the date below flanked by privy marks, his monogram to the right, and the 12 stars of Europe in the outer ring
6311.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the 15 states of the European Union

11550.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Belgian Presidency of the European Union)
11550.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
11550.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin features the commemorative logo: the stylised letters “EU” and “trio.be”. The central design is encircled by the words “BELGIAN PRESIDENCY OF THE COUNCIL OF THE EU 2010” and the trilingual country designation in Dutch, French and German ‘BELGIE BELGIQUE BELGIEN’. Beneath the logo and above the country’s name, the year of issue, “2010”, is centred between the mint mark on the left and the mint master’s mark on the right. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
11550.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

19734.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (100th Women's Day)
19734.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
19734.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin commemorating the 100th anniversary of International Women’s Day, which is celebrated on 8 March, features effigies of Isala Van Diest, the first female Belgian medical doctor, and Marie Popelin, the first female Belgian lawyer above a semicircle formed by their names and the symbols of their professions on either side of the year 2011. Centered above the effigies are the letters “BE” in reference to their nationality, flanked by the Mint master’s mark on the left and the mintmark on the right. The coin’s outer ring shows the 12 stars of the European Union.
19734.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28325.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28325.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28325.obverse_description: Coin features the world in the form of a euro symbol in the centre, showing how the euro has become a true global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolise the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28325.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

32526.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Queen Elisabeth Music Competition)
32526.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
32526.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows the logo of the Queen Elisabeth Competition superimposed on a left profile of Queen Elisabeth, who founded the music competition in 1937. On the left is the mintmaster mark (a quill) and on the right the mintmark (the head of the Archangel Michael). In a semi-circle along the bottom edge of the inner part is the inscription “QUEEN ELISABETH COMPETITION”, while the years “1937 – 2012”, marking the 75th anniversary of the competition, are inscribed along the top edge. The letters “BE” to the right of the image refer to the country of issue. The outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
32526.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

49339.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Royal Meteorological Institute)
49339.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
49339.obverse_description: The obverse of the coin displays in its centre the number 100 with the first zero encircling the abbreviations ‘KMI’ and ‘IRM’ and the second zero representing a sun. Isobars, raindrops and snowflakes are depicted to the left of the sun. The year ‘2013’ is displayed in the upper rays of the sun and the nationality ‘BE’ is indicated in the lower rays. The mark of the mint master and the mark of the Brussels mint, a helmeted profile of the Archangel Michael, are displayed under the ‘1’ of the number ‘100’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
49339.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

59828.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (WW I Anniversary)
59828.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
59828.obverse_description: The coin’s central field depicts a poppy above the years 2014-18. Below these years appears the inscription ‘The Great War Centenary’, under which are the signature mark of the Master of the Mint and the mark of the Brussels mint, a helmeted profile of the archangel Michael. The top of the central field features the trilingual inscription ‘BELGIE — BELGIQUE — BELGIEN’ - the name of the country in Dutch, French and German. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
59828.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

64978.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Belgian Red Cross)
64978.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
64978.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin features a cross with the number ‘150’ in its centre. The vertical and horizontal bars of the cross bear the words ‘Rode Kruis’ and ‘Croix-Rouge’ respectively. The cross is surrounded by the mark of the Brussels mint (a helmeted profile of the archangel Michael), the signature mark of the Master of the Mint, the year 2014, and the country code ‘BE’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
64978.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

56039.name: 2 Euros - Philippe
56039.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
56039.obverse_description: Coin features the effigy of the new Head of State His Majesty Philippe, King of the Belgians, in profile to the right. To the left of the effigy, the indication of the issuing country ‘BE’ and on top of it the royal monogram. Under the effigy, the mint master mark at the left and the mint mark at the right flank the year of issuance.
56039.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

76630.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (European Year for Development)
76630.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76630.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin depicts a hand holding a globe of the Earth with a plant in the foreground. The inscription ‘2015 EUROPEAN YEAR FOR DEVELOPMENT’ features in an arc above the globe. The country code ‘BE’ appears beneath the hand while the signature mark of the Master of the Mint and the mark of the Brussels mint, a helmeted profile of the archangel Michael, is to its left. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
76630.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

77181.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (30 Years of European Union Flag)
77181.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
77181.obverse_description: The European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in a semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
77181.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

87173.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Child Focus)
87173.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
87173.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows the face of a child surrounded by the inscription "MISSING-DISPARU-VERMIST", "WWW.CHILDFOCUS.BE" and the trilingual indication of nationality "BELGIQUE-BELGIE-BELGIEN. The mintmark of Brussels, a helmeted head of St. Michael the Archangel, and the mark the designer are also depicted to the left and right of the face respectively.
87173.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

84609.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Summer Olympics 2016 in Rio de Janeiro)
84609.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
84609.obverse_description: The design is based on the Olympic team logo of Belgium symbolizing an athlete who wears the flag of the country, celebrating victory and expressing pride in representing her country. The inner part of the coin depicts, from top to bottom, a stylized character, the five Olympic rings, and the inscription "BELGIUM TEAM." To the left is the year 2016. On the right, the indication of nationality 'BE' appears between the monetary brand of Brussels (helmeted head of the Archangel Michael) and the brand of "currency commissioner".
84609.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

124813.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (200 years of the University of Ghent)
124813.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
124813.obverse_description: The logo of the Ghent University, with the dates 1817 - 2017 below, surrounded by the inscription "200 JAAR UNIVERSITEIT GENT – 200 YEARS GHENT UNIVERSITY" and the country indication BE. The mintmark of Brussels, a helmeted head of the Archangel Michael and the mint directors’ mintmark, the coat of arms of the municipality Herzele, are located respectively on the right and left of the country indication. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
124813.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

108778.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (200 years of the University of Liège)
108778.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
108778.obverse_description: The inner part of the piece represents the logo of the University of Liege, with the dates 1817 — 2017 below, surrounded by the inscription and the country indication BE. The mintmark of Brussels, a helmeted head of the Archangel Michael and the mint directors’ mintmark, the coat of arms of the municipality Herzele, are located respectively on the right and left of the country indication.
108778.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

150281.name: 2 Euros (ESRO-2B)
150281.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
150281.obverse_description:
The design shows the ESRO 2B satellite, the first successful satellite of the European Space
Research Organization and launched in May 1968, circling around planet Earth. The ESRO 2B, also renamed as ‘IRIS’
 (International Radiation Investigation Satellite) once in orbit, was concentrated on solar X-rays, cosmic radiation and
Earth’s radiation belts.
The mintmark of Utrecht (the Mercury’s wand) is located on the bottom together with the Belgian mint master mark,
the coat of arms of the municipality Herzele, the country code BE and the initials LL referring to the designer of the
coin, Mr Luc Luycx.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
150281.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

140149.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Events of May 1968)
140149.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
140149.obverse_description: The design represents students with a pamphlet and flag in their hands and refers to student demonstrations that took place in Belgium in connection with the socio-cultural changes of May 1968. It was an important national event that shaped Belgium’s national politics and influenced subsequent Government reforms, now 50 years ago. The mintmark of Utrecht (the Mercury’s wand) is located on the left together with the Belgian mint master mark (the coat of arms of the municipality Herzele). The year mark 2018 and the country code BE are located at the left as well, just like the initials LL of the designer of the coin, Luc Luycx. An auditorium marks the back, which is a reference to students. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
140149.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

155935.name: 2 Euros (European Monetary Institute (EMI))
155935.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
155935.obverse_description: The portrait of Alexandre Lamfalussy, the first president of the EMI, on the right with his name below. On the left-hand side, the abbreviation EMI stands central with the year 1994 above, referring to the date of the establishment of the Institute and the designation of Lamfalussy as its first president. Below ‘EMI’, several coins are depicted falling on each other with the inscription €, ‘ECU’ and ‘BEF’ from top to bottom. Given the fact that this is a Belgian issuance, we have chosen for ‘BEF’, the abbreviation of our former national currency. The purpose of this representation is to symbolise the transition of the national currencies to European single currency, the euro as the EMI’s main focus was on establishing the European System of Central Banks, including the ECB and the new currency. On the upper part of the left side of the coin is an inscription ‘European Monetary Institute’. The mintmark (a mercury staff is located on the left with the Belgian mint director mintmark, the coat of arms of the municipality Herzele). The country code BE and the year mark 2019 is located on the bottom. The initials LL referring to the designer of the coin, Mr Luc Luycx, are inscribed on the right. The coins outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
155935.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

155931.name: 2 Euros (Pieter Bruegel the Elder)
155931.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
155931.obverse_description:
The inner part of the piece depicts the portrait of the famous Belgian artist Pieter Bruegel the Elder, together with a painting on an easel. Above this, you can find the name P. Bruegel, the years 1569, with a subtle obelisk referring to the year of death, and 2019, the year of issuance.
As the Royal Dutch Mint will strike the coins, the mintmark of Utrecht, a mercury staff is located on the left together
with the Belgian mint director mintmark, the coat of arms of the municipality Herzele, the country code BE. The initials LL referring to the designer of the coin, Mr Luc Luycx, are located on the right. Finally, around the edge of the inner part small dots form a circle.
The coins outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
155931.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

176636.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (International Year of Plant Health)
176636.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass centre in copper-nickel ring
176636.obverse_description: The piece is dedicated to the International Year of Plant Health and shows in the middle the official logo of the International Year of Plant Health (IYPH 2020). The leaves, arranged like a globe, symbolize healthy plants as a source of oxygen that we breathe, the food we eat and all life on planet Earth. Above is the edition event in English and on the left the mint sign of Ingrid van Herzele and on the right the coin sign of the Dutch Mint in Utrecht. The country abbreviation "BE" and the year "2020" are arranged at the bottom of the image. The twelve stars of the European flag are represented on the outer ring of the coin.
176636.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

176637.name: 2 Euros (Jan van Eyck)
176637.composition: Bimetallic: nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
176637.obverse_description: The motif of the piece shows the portrait of the famous Belgian artist Jan van Eyck on the right. On the left, the edition "J van Eyck", his signature and a palette of painter with the initials of the creator Luc Luycx. At the bottom of the picture you can see the coin signs of the Dutch Mint in Utrecht and the mint sign by Ingrid van Herzele. On the right edge of the image are the country abbreviation "BE" and the typing year "2020". The twelve stars of the European flag are represented on the outer ring of the coin.
176637.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

309301.name: 2 Euros - (Carolus V Coins)
309301.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
309301.obverse_description:
The most famous coin is the Golden Carolus Guilder. Charles V’s pursuit of uniformity and centralization was also expressed in this series of coins. Almost 500 years after Charles V introduced his silver Carolus guilder, he was depicted on some ECU coins. The ECU, standing for European Currency Unit, was the forerunner of the euro.
The national side of the coin depicts in the inner part of the piece the portrait of the Charles V, together with the text Carolus V. At the left you can find the 500 year old golden Carolus guilder, issued after the ordinance of the second period of coin issuance during the reign of Charles V. As the Royal Dutch Mint will strike the coins, the mintmark of Utrecht, a mercury staff is located on the bottom together with the Belgian mint director mintmark, the coat of arms of the municipality Herzele. It also contains the initials LL, referring to the designer of the coin, Mr. Luc Luycx. The country code BE and the year mark 2021 are located on the left. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag
309301.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

299176.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Economic Union)
299176.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
299176.obverse_description: Double portrait of King Philippe of Belgium and Grand Duke Henri of Luxembourg. Outline of the countries of Belgium and Luxembourg.
299176.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

328080.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Healthcare in COVID)
328080.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
328080.obverse_description:
The design depicts in the inner part of the coin the health personnel. At the left you can find the inscription ‘Danke – Merci – Dank u’ together with various pictograms referring to healthcare sector. From top to bottom, a cross, a stethoscope, a heart, a syringe, a wheelchair and a chemical mixture are shown. At the far right are the initials of the designer Luc Luycx located. As the Royal Dutch Mint will strike the coins, the mintmark of Utrecht, a mercury staff is located on the bottom together with the Belgian mint master mark, the coat of arms of the municipality Herzele, the country code BE and the year mark 2022.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
328080.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

331208.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Erasmus Programme)
331208.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
331208.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
331208.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. Twelve stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

368766.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Art Nouveau)
368766.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
368766.obverse_description:
The design shows a decorative detail from the facade of the ‘Hotel Van Eetvelde’, an iconic town house in Brussels which has been designed by the Belgian art nouveau architect Victor Horta and has been recognized as Unesco World Heritage. The curved, asymmetrical lines of this decorative detail reflect the characteristic nature-inspired style of the art nouveau movement. This detail fills up the bottom half of the piece, leaving a vacant space on the upper half, which is filled with the description ‘ART NOUVEAU’. At the far right are the initials of the designer Iris Bruijns located. As the Royal Dutch Mint will strike the coins, the mintmark of Utrecht, a mercury staff is located on the far left together with the Belgian mint director mintmark, an aster flower in front of an Erlenmeyer flask, the country code BE and the year mark 2023.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
368766.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

383352.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Women's suffrage)
383352.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
383352.obverse_description: On core, a drawing commemorating the seventy-five years of the Belgian Women’s suffrage, featuring the Venus symbol which loop is filled thanks to a pencil, the signature IB of the artist engraver Iris Bruijns, the ISO code of Belgium, BE, the year of issue of the coin, 2023, and the Dutch marks (including the caduceus of the Utrecht Mint); on outer ring, the twelve emblematic European five-pointed stars.
383352.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

395552.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Presidency of the Council of the EU)
395552.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
395552.obverse_description: At the bottom left, the text 'BELGIAN PRESIDENCY OF THE COUNCIL OF THE EU 2024'. Centrally across the coin, 27 swallows (27 member states) swarm around the country code BE. The swallow moves in unison and flies in one group after the trigger. Swallows are helpful, industrious and loyal. Left: 'IB' the initials of designer Iris Bruijns, above the mintmaster's mark (an erlenmeyer flask with aster) and the mintmark of the Royal Dutch Mint (the Mercury staff).
395552.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments

415044.name: 2 Euros - Philippe (Cancer)
415044.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
415044.obverse_description: The obverse shows a stylised rainbow as a symbol of hope for a cancer-free world, with the country and year BE 2024. At the centre is the representation of a heartbeat in the form of an awareness ribbon often pinned to express our solidarity with the patient. At the bottom is the bilingual inscription Lutte contre le cancer - Fight against cancer, surrounded by the initials IB of designer Iris Bruijns, the mintmaster's mark (Erlenmeyer flask with aster) and the mintmaster's mark of the Royal Dutch Mint (the Mercury staff).
415044.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

451966.name: 2 Euro (Lottery)
451966.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
451966.obverse_description:
The design depicts in the centre the classical allegorical representation of good fortune, the goddess Fortuna, carrying the horn of plenty. On the right and below are 4 depictions of a four-leaf clover, another universal symbol of luck. Behind the image of Fortuna, the current National Lottery logo is visible.

On the left side of the coin the text “loterie nationale loterij” is displayed. On the bottom left the country code BE and the year of issue 2025 are shown. At the bottom right, the initials of designer Iris Bruijns are visible. As the Royal Dutch Mint will strike the coins, the mintmark of Utrecht, a mercury staff, is located underneath the year mark, together with the Belgian mint director mintmark, an aster flower in front of an Erlenmeyer flask.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
451966.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

477994.name: 2 Euros - (Spa-Francorchamps Circuit)
477994.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
477994.obverse_description: Line drawing of the current circuit, surmounted by the inscription "CIRCUIT DE SPA FRANCORCHAMPS". On the left are the length of 7.004 meters and the date of creation of the circuit in 1921. At the very bottom, we find the mark which is an Erlenmeyer flask with an aster, followed by the inscription "BE" for Belgium, the year of issuance 2025 and the mint mark, a Mercury rod. The outer ring of the coin is decorated with the twelve stars of the European flag.
477994.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

537216.name: 2 Euros
537216.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
537216.obverse_description: Paisius of Hilendar (Paisiy Hilendarski), the Bulgarian Revival historian, facing left in detailed relief. His facial features and clothing are finely sculpted, reflecting 18th-century attire. Marked with „БЪЛГАРИЯ“, 2026, and the EU stars along the outer ring, symbolizing Bulgaria’s cultural heritage and place within the European Union.
537216.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

373926.name: 2 Euros (Member of the Euro Area)
373926.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
373926.obverse_description:
The design features the inscriptions of the issuing country ‘HRVATSKA’ (Croatia) and the year of issuance ‘2023.’, depicted horizontally, as well as the words ‘ČLANICA EUROPODRUČJA’ (member of the euro area), which are inscribed along the outer edge of the coin’s core. These inscriptions symbolically form a stylised symbol of the euro ‘€’. The other central motive of the coin is Croatia's distinctive and recognisable symbol, the Croatian checkerboard, which represents part of the coat of arms of the Republic of Croatia.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
373926.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

338339.name: 2 Euros (2nd map)
338339.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
338339.obverse_description: Silhouette map of Croatia on the Croatian checkerboard
338339.reverse_description: Denomination and a map of Europe

400974.name: 2 Euros (Old Town of Varaždin)
400974.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
400974.obverse_description: The design features the keep of the Varaždin Castle (12th-16th century), along with the name of the issuing country ‘HRVATSKA’ (Croatia), the year of issuance ‘2024’, and the words ‘STARI GRAD VARAŽDIN’ (Old Town of Varaždin), which are all inscribed circularly at the top and the bottom of the design. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
400974.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

437781.name: 2 Euros (Marko Marulić)
437781.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
437781.obverse_description:
On the obverse is the portrait of Marko Marulić. In addition to the main motif, the coin also features the inscriptions: the symbol for the Republic of Croatia “HR”, the year of issuance “2024.” and the name and surname of Marko Marulić “MARKO MARULIĆ”, which are inscribed along the coin’s core.
The coin’s national side outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag
437781.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

474230.name: 2 Euros (King Tomislav)
474230.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
474230.obverse_description: Features the main motif of King Tomislav on a horse. The background of the main motif features Croatia's distinctive symbol, the Croatian checkerboard. The coin also features the following inscriptions: "KRALJ TOMISLAV” (King Tomislav), the year of coronation of King Tomislav "925.", the issuing country "REPUBLIKA HRVATSKA" (Republic of Croatia) and the year of issuance "2025.", which are inscribed circularly along the edge of the coin’s core. The outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
474230.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

514512.name: 2 Euros (City of Pula)
514512.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
514512.obverse_description: A depiction of the main motif of the Pula Arena (amphitheatre) from the perspective of the Adriatic Sea. To the right of the main motif is a depiction of the Church of St. Anthony. The inscription "CITY OF PULA - ARENA", the country of issue "CROATIA" and the year of issue "2025" are inscribed in a circle along the edge of the coin's core. The outer ring features the 12 stars of the European flag.
514512.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

2180.name: 2 Euros
2180.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2180.obverse_description: A cross-shaped idol dating back to the chalcolithic period (3.000 B.C.), found in Pomos, a village in the district of Paphos. It is a characteristic example of prehistoric art in Cyprus, reflecting the island's antiquity and civilization.
2180.reverse_description: The reverse of the coin shows a geographical image of Europe; it also features the numeral 2 and the inscription EURO.

5060.name: 2 Euros (Economic and Monetary Union)
5060.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5060.obverse_description: Stick figure which merges into the € symbol. Twelve stars of the European Union on outer ring.
5060.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28326.name: 2 Euros (Euro Cash)
28326.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28326.obverse_description: Coin features the world in the form of a euro symbol in the centre, showing how the euro has become a true global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolise the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28326.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

76633.name: 2 Euros (European Union Flag)
76633.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76633.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in a semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
76633.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

112816.name: 2 Euros (Paphos)
112816.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
112816.obverse_description:
The design shows the ‘Paphos Ancient Odeon’, a small amphiteatre dating back to the 2nd century AD. Nowadays it is used in the summer for musical theatrical performances and it will be the venue of a number of events to be held within the program of ‘Paphos 2017 – European Capital of Culture’. The issuing country’s name ‘ΚΥΠΡΟΣ KIBRIS’ and the phrase ‘Πάφος 2017 – Πολιτιστική Πρωτεύουσα της Ευρώπης’ (i.e. Paphos 2017 – European Capital of Culture) are inscribed on the inner part of the national side of the coin.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
112816.reverse_description: A map of Europe, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

225688.name: 2 Euros (Institute of Neurology and Genetics)
225688.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
225688.obverse_description: The design depicts a ‘neuron with its synapses’ about the activities performed by the Cyprus Institute of Neurology and Genetics, which celebrates 30 years of existence. The Cyprus Institute of Neurology and Genetics receives international recognition and plays an active and essential role as a national, regional, and international center of excellence for the provision of high-quality services, innovative research, and postgraduate education. The issuing country’s name ‘KYΠΡΟΣ - KIBRIS’ and the phrase ‘ΙΝΣΤΙTOYΤΟ ΝΕΥΡΟΛΟΓΊΑΣ & ΓΕΝΕΤΙKHΣ KYΠΡΟY 1990-2020’ (i.e. Cyprus Institute of Neurology and Genetics 1990-2020) are inscribed in a circle around the design. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
225688.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

319134.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
319134.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
319134.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
319134.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

431867.name: 2 Euros (EU Membership)
431867.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
431867.obverse_description:
A composition depicting the island of Cyprus, the words “ΚΥΠΡΟΣ”, “KIBRIS”, the Earth’s globe, the building of the European Commission in Brussels, the 12 stars of the European Union flag and the period «2004-2024». The phrase «20 ΧΡΟΝΙΑ ΑΠΟ ΤΗΝ ΕΝΤΑΞΗ ΤΗΣ ΚΥΠΡΟΥ ΣΤΗΝ ΕΥΡΩΠΑΪΚΗ ΕΝΩΣΗ» is circularly inscribed. 
The twelve stars are also depicted in the composition’s outer ring.
431867.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

16593.name: 2 Euros (2nd map)
16593.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
16593.obverse_description: A silhouette map of Estonia with the year above and the country name below and twelve five-pointed stars around the rim representing the European Union
16593.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the gathering of the fifteen states of the European Union

28192.name: 2 Euros (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28192.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28192.obverse_description: Coin features the world in the form of a euro symbol in the centre, showing how the euro has become a true global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolise the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28192.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

77306.name: 2 Euros (European Union Flag)
77306.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
77306.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
77306.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

80919.name: 2 Euros (Paul Keres)
80919.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
80919.obverse_description: Paul Keres, an Estonian chess grandmaster, and a renowned chess writer, who was among the world's top players from the mid-1930s to the mid-1960s, facing left.
80919.reverse_description: The face value, next to a map showing the European continent without borders.

115286.name: 2 Euros (Road to Independence)
115286.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
115286.obverse_description: The sinuous trunk of an oak tree in the center with branches, which symbolize the time of revolutions and hardships from which the road to Estonia's Independence was paved, to the left and leaves, which symbolize the strength, achievements, and longevity of Estonia, to the right. At the bottom left side of the trunk there is the word ‘MAAPÄEV’ (Provisional Assembly of Estonia) and above that the year ‘1917’. At the bottom right side, there is the name of the issuing country ‘EESTI’ and below that the year of issuance ‘2017’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
115286.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

133760.name: 2 Euros (Independent Baltic States)
133760.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
133760.obverse_description: The three Baltic sisters (Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania) with braided hair. Above the coats of arms of the three Baltic countries, the date to the right and the country name to the left. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
133760.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

134284.name: 2 Euros (Independence of Estonia)
134284.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
134284.obverse_description: The official national logo for the centenary of Estonia in the center which can be read as the numbers 18 and 100, linking the year the state was founded with today.
134284.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

168218.name: 2 Euros (Festival of the Song of Estonia)
168218.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
168218.obverse_description:
The design is inspired by the Song Celebration procession, which moves like the waves on the sea with cheers of pride and joy and a panoply of national dress. The design unites music, national costumes, and the various sites of the great and powerful Song Celebration of the nation by the sea. The design also features the first notes of the Estonian national anthem and, at the bottom, the words ‘Laulupidu 150’ (Song Celebration 150). At the top is the year of issuance ‘2019’ and underneath the name of the issuing country ‘EESTI’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
168218.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

183116.name: 2 Euros (University of Tartu)
183116.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
183116.obverse_description: A motif of the main building of Tartu University in the center and also bears the inscriptions ‘RAHVUSÜLIKOOL 100’ (National University 100) and ‘UNIVERSITAS TARTUENSIS’, the year ‘1632’, the issuing country ‘EESTI’ and the date at the bottom. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
183116.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

193396.name: 2 Euros (Discovery of Antarctica)
193396.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
193396.obverse_description: Bellingshausen's sloop-of-war, Vostok, surrounded by the inscriptions ‘Fabian Gottlieb von Bellingshausen’ and ‘ANTARKTIKA 200’, the country name and the date. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
193396.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

287622.name: 2 Euros (Finno-Ugric people)
287622.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
287622.obverse_description:
The national side of the coin depicts a design based on the cave drawings of Lake Äänisjärv. The symbols for the hunter, the elk, the water bird and the sun form the symbolic circle of life for the Finno-Ugric peoples. The central figure is a water bird, as they are represented in the art and folklore of all Finno-Ugric peoples. On the top right, in semi-circle, are the name of the country ‘EESTI’ followed by the year ‘2021’. At the bottom left is the inscription ‘FENNOUGRIA’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
287622.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

275664.name: 2 Euros (Wolf)
275664.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
275664.obverse_description:
The design depicts the silhouette of the wolf and a forest. Close to the edge of the inner ring the country name ‘EESTI’ at the left, the year of issuance ‘2021’ at the right and the text ‘CANIS LUPUS’ (the wolf in Latin) at the top are depicted.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
275664.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

331415.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
331415.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
331415.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus.
