The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
331415.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. Twelve stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

332623.name: 2 Euros (For Ukraine)
332623.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni. Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni)
332623.obverse_description: The two-euro coin dedicated to Ukraine and freedom features a girl as a symbol of tenderness, protecting a bird in her hand. The design also features an ear of wheat.
332623.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

321860.name: 2 Euros (Society of Estonian Literaty)
321860.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
321860.obverse_description: The new commemorative coin shows an open book with the words of the first president of the Society of Estonian Literati Jacob Hurt written on it: “Kui me ei saa suureks rahvaarvult, peame saama suureks vaimult”, meaning ‘If we cannot be a great nation in number, we must be great in spirit’, with the year 1872 that the Society of Estonian Literati was founded. The society was founded in Viljandi and then operated in Tartu, and it helped promote literature in Estonian and so indirectly raised the cultural level of the Estonian people.
321860.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

364450.name: 2 Euros (Barn Swallow)
364450.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
364450.obverse_description: A barn swallow flying and two others sitting on an electricity line. Country name in a semi-circle at the bottom right. Latin name of the barn swallow at the top of the inner ring. Twelve stars of the European flag in the outer ring.
364450.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

415214.name: 2 Euros (Cornflower)
415214.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
415214.obverse_description: The coin dedicated to the cornflower shows a stylised flower leaning towards the viewer in its natural environment of a windblown field of cereal. The flowers of the whole cornflower have an eight-pointed motif at the centre that is an Estonian folk design and symbol that is commonly found in traditional patterns as a sign of luck and a symbol of love.
415214.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

451746.name: 2 Euros (Book)
451746.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
451746.obverse_description:
Several books surrounded by the inscriptions "EESTI RAAMAT 500" and anniversary dates "1525 - 2025".
The outer ring of the coin represents the twelve stars of the European flag.
451746.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

96.name: 2 Euros (1st type, 1st map)
96.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
96.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the fifteen states of the European Union

2189.name: 2 Euros (Enlargement of the European Union)
2189.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2189.obverse_description: A stylised pillar from which the sprouts grow upwards with the sprouts representing the enlargement of the European Union and the pillar representing the foundation for growth with the letters "EU" to the left of the pillar, and the date at the top in the outer ring along with the twelve stars of Europe
2189.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2194.name: 2 Euros (60th Anniversary - Finland in UN)
2194.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2194.obverse_description: Depicted in the centre of the coin are parts of a jigsaw puzzle showing a dove of peace. Below the image of the dove, inscribed along the edge of the bottom half of the central part of the coin, the inscription ‘FINLAND — UN’ appears on the left and the year of issue on the right. Above the last figure of the year of issue, set close to the edge of a piece of the jigsaw puzzle, is the letter ‘K’, the engraver's initial. The mintmark ‘M’ is inscribed immediately below the image of the dove of peace. The outer ring features the 12 stars of the European Union.
2194.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2198.name: 2 Euros (Universal Suffrage)
2198.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2198.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows male and female faces separated by a line. To their left is ‘1.10.1906’ and to their right is ‘20 FI 06’, the year of issue with the abbreviation for Finland, FI. The mint mark ‘M’ appears next to each of the two faces. The 12 stars of the European Union are depicted on the outer ring of the coin.
2198.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

259433.name: 2 Euros (1st Type, 2nd Map; Mule)
259433.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
259433.obverse_description: Cloudberry berries centre, surrounded by 12 stars.

2205.name: 2 Euros (Treaty of Rome)
2205.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2205.obverse_description: The coin shows the Treaty document signed by the six founding countries on a background evoking the paving (designed by Michelangelo) of the Piazza del Campidoglio in Rome, where the signing took place on 25 March 1957. ‘Treaty of Rome 50 years’, ‘EUROPE’ and the name of the issuing country appear in the respective languages of the euro area or in Latin. Thus, the legend differs from country to country, but the image is the same. The Treaty of Rome established the European Economic Community and ultimately led to the introduction of the euro in 1999 and the euro banknotes and coins in 2002. The anniversary was celebrated on 25 March 2007. The euro area countries have marked the occasion by jointly issuing this commemorative coin. Its design was selected following a competition organised by the European mints. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
2205.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2203.name: 2 Euros (Independence)
2203.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2203.obverse_description: The coin shows a nine-oar boat with rowers, symbolizing collaboration. The year of issue, 2007, and the year of independence, 1917, appear above and below the image, respectively. The mint mark appears to the left of the oars and ‘FI’ for Finland to the right. The outer ring of the coin features the 12 stars of the European Union.
2203.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

8714.name: 2 Euros (2nd type, 2nd map)
8714.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
8714.obverse_description: Cloudberries and cloudberry flowers, the emblem of Finland, are encircled by the twelve stars of Europe

4292.name: 2 Euros (Declaration of Human Rights)
4292.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
4292.obverse_description: Designed by sculptor Tapio Kettunen, the coin depicts a human being inside a heart and, underneath it, the inscription ‘HUMAN RIGHTS’. ‘FI’ for Finland, ‘K’ for the sculptor as well as the mint mark appear at the bottom of the inner part of the coin, and the year of issue, 2008, at the top. The 12 stars of the European Union are shown on the outer ring.
4292.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

5062.name: 2 Euros (Economic and Monetary Union)
5062.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5062.obverse_description: Stick figure which merges into the € symbol. Twelve stars of the European Union on outer ring.
5062.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

9174.name: 2 Euros (Autonomy)
9174.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
9174.obverse_description: The first Diet of Finland met in 1809 in Porvoo, so the coin shows a profile of Porvoo cathedral, where the opening ceremony took place, and ‘1809’ written in stylised numbering above. To its left is the country code, ‘FI’, and to its right is the year of issue, ‘2009’. The 12 stars of the European Union appear on the outer ring.
9174.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

16111.name: 2 Euros (Rahapaja - Mark)
16111.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
16111.obverse_description: A stylised figure of a lion taken from the Finnish coat of arms, the date on the left, the mintmark and a set of numbers symbolising coin values to the right thereof in the centre. Centred at the bottom edge is a reference to the issuing country, namely the letters ‘FI’. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
16111.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

26165.name: 2 Euros (Finland National Bank)
26165.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
26165.obverse_description: The commemorative coin to mark the Bank of Finland’s 200th anniversary, which was designed by the artist Hannu Veijalainen, depicts Finland’s national bird, the swan. Also, the inner part of the coin bears the dates “2011”, the year of issue, and “1811”, the year in which Emperor Alexander I of Russia decreed that an “Exchange, Lending and Deposit Office” was to be established in Turku, Finland. It was Finland’s first bank and eventually became the Bank of Finland, which moved to Helsinki in 1819. The Bank began to operate as a central bank in the latter part of the 19th century after Finland obtained its monetary unit and commercial banks had been established in the country. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
26165.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28328.name: 2 Euros (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28328.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28328.obverse_description: Coin features the world in the form of a euro symbol in the centre, showing how the euro has become a true global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolise the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28328.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

38356.name: 2 Euros (Helene Schjerfbeck)
38356.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
38356.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows a stylised self-portrait of the artist, with her name, “HELENE SCHJERFBECK”, and the years of birth and death, “1862-1946”, inscribed vertically to the left and right, respectively. At the bottom to the left of the portrait are the letters “FI” to denote the issuing country and “2012”, the year of issue. The mintmark is located on the edge of the coin’s inner part immediately below the dates of birth and death. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
38356.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

48329.name: 2 Euros (Parliament)
48329.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
48329.obverse_description: The year 1863 and a shoot growing out of it symbolising the start of democracy and Finland’s development. In semicircle, at the bottom, the name of the issuing country ‘SUOMI FINLAND’ with the two words separated by the mint mark, and the year ‘2013’. The Diet of 1863 is the start of a period of illumination. In Finland, the year 1863 marked a start for genuine democracy, transparent press operations and the right to use the Finnish language. With the beginning of regular meetings of the Diet, the opportunities of Finns to influence affairs in their own country improved in an unprecedented way. The year marked the beginning of a period of illumination for Finnish democracy. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
48329.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

51670.name: 2 Euros (Frans Eemil Sillanpää)
51670.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
51670.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin depicts Sillanpää's portrait. At the top, in a semi-circle, the words ‘F. E. SILLANPÄÄ’. At the left, the year ‘1888’ and the mintmark. At the right, the year ‘1964’, the indication of the issuing country ‘FI’ and the year of issuance ‘2013’. This is a special coin to commemorate the grandfather of a whole nation. F. E. SILLANPÄÄ was a master of style who brought deep insights about the relationship between people and nature into Finnish literature. He was awarded the Nobel prize in 1939 based in particular on the works Meek Heritage and The Maid Silja. This masterful author gained even more national renown with his popular radio appearances and memorable Christmas columns. After the war years, the master writer evolved into the long-bearded ‘Taata’, the unofficial grandfather to the country. From recollections of his childhood Christmases emerged a well-loved tradition: Grandpa gathered the whole nation devoutly around their radios with his easy-going Christmas ‘talks’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
51670.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

61715.name: 2 Euros (Tove Jansson)
61715.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
61715.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin depicts Tove Jansson’s portrait. Under the portrait the signature ‘Tove Jansson’ and the years ‘1914-2001’. At the left the indication of the issuing country ‘FI’. At the right the year of issuance ‘2014’ and the mint mark. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
61715.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

66921.name: 2 Euros (Ilmari Tapiovaara)
66921.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
66921.obverse_description: The left inner part of the design shows Ilmari Tapiovaara’s name and years. The right inner part of the design depicts a close-up from the furniture fitting characteristic for Ilmari Tapiovaara. At the right the indication of the issuing country ‘FI’, the mint mark and the year of issuance ‘2014’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
66921.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

77165.name: 2 Euros (Akseli Gallen-Kallela)
77165.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
77165.obverse_description: A swimming swan of Tuonela with the water below waving from the descending spirit. The indication of the issuing country ‘FI’ is at the right end of the horizon line. At the bottom of the design there is the artist’s palette with Gallen-Kallela’s year of birth ‘1865’ and the year of issuance ‘2015’. Next to the pallet is the mint mark. The artist’s name ‘AKSELI GALLEN KALLELA’ is on the ring of the coin, below the horizon line. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
77165.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

69852.name: 2 Euros (Jean Sibelius)
69852.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
69852.obverse_description: At the inner part of the coin is sky with stars, tree tops at the right. The text ‘JEAN SIBELIUS’ and the year of issuance ‘2015’ are placed at the left inner part of the coin. At the right is the indication of the issuing country ‘FI’ and the mint mark. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
69852.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

74920.name: 2 Euros (European Union Flag)
74920.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
74920.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
74920.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

86837.name: 2 Euros (Eino Leino)
86837.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
86837.obverse_description: At the inner part of the coin is a flame, and a poker pointing upwards to the right. The inscription ‘EINO LEINO’, in semi-circle and the year of issuance ‘2016’ are placed at the right. At the bottom left is the indication of the issuing country ‘FI’ and the mint mark. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
86837.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

93999.name: 2 Euros (Georg Henrik von Wright)
93999.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
93999.obverse_description:
At the inner part of the coin is an ancient Doric pillar and an oak branch at the left. The inscription 'GEORG HENRIK VON WRIGHT' is placed in a semi-circle from left to right and the year of issuance '2016' is placed on the top at the right. On the top at the left is the indication of the issuing country 'FI' and the mintmark.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
93999.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

124447.name: 2 Euros (Finnish Nature)
124447.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
124447.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin has a design forming a cartographic likeness of the moon seen from Harmaja, Finland. On top of the moon there is a crow sitting on a branch. The date is at the centre bottom. At the centre-left is the indication of the issuing country ‘FI’. The mint mark is placed centre-right. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
124447.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

113429.name: 2 Euros (Independence)
113429.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
113429.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin has a mosaic design, with the more sparsely set pieces on the right forming a cartographic likeness of Finland. The text ‘SUOMI FINLAND’, the year of independence ‘1917’ and the year of issuance ‘2017’ are placed vertically one below another at the left. In the middle right is the indication of the issuing country ‘FI’ and the mintmark. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
113429.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

143884.name: 2 Euros (Finnish Sauna Culture)
143884.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
143884.obverse_description:
The design shows a Finnish landscape with a typical Finnish lakeside sauna placed in the centre. The date is at the centre bottom. The indication of the issuing country ‘FI’ is at the
centre-left and the mint mark at the centre-right.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
143884.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

141243.name: 2 Euros (Landscape Koli)
141243.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
141243.obverse_description: The design shows a drawing from the top of the Finnish national landscape Koli. The date is at the centre bottom. The indication of the issuing country ‘FI’ is at the centre-left and the mintmark at the centre-right. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
141243.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

182638.name: 2 Euros (Constitution Act of Finland)
182638.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
182638.obverse_description:
The design shows three circular fields that are joined together at the centre. The three fields represent the trias politica, separation of powers into three different branches: a legislative, an executive, and a judiciary
power. The year of issuance ‘2019’ is placed centre-left. The indication of the issuing country ‘FI’ and the mint mark is
placed bottom right. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
182638.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

220248.name: 2 Euros (University of Turku)
220248.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
220248.obverse_description:
The grid-like design represents the interaction between universities and society. At the top of the design, there is the year of issuance ‘2020’, at the right side the mintmark and underneath the mintmark is the indication of the issuing country ‘FI’. 
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
220248.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

241918.name: 2 Euros (Väinö Linna)
241918.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
241918.obverse_description:
The coin celebrates the well-known late Finnish writer Väinö Linna. This year marks the centenary of his birth in 1920. The design reflects Linna’s work in a textile mill and depicts, in addition to his face, letters as weaved fabric and threads that run through his books. The inscription VÄINÖ LINNA is at the left side. At the bottom are the inscriptions of the year of issuance ‘2020’, of the issuing country ‘FI’ and the mintmark.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
241918.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

280491.name: 2 Euros (Journalism)
280491.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
280491.obverse_description:
The figures depicted in the inner part of the coin can be interpreted as stylized human figures made up from and entwined in a ribbon-like net. The outer sides of the inner part of the coin bear the lettering ‘JOURNALISMI JOURNALISTIK’. The bottom of the inner part bears the lettering ‘2021 FI’ preceded by the Finnish mint mark.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
280491.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

284816.name: 2 Euros (Autonomy of the Åland Islands)
284816.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass centre in copper-nickel ring
284816.obverse_description: Depicts the archipelago landscape of the Åland Islands, a shipping route with nautical signs, the bow of a boat and a hand holding a compass, clouds above. Surrounded by the inscription '100th Anniversary of Autonomy of Åland Islands' above in Swedish and below in Finnish separated by two diamond-shaped sea buoys.
284816.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

318763.name: 2 Euros (Ballet)
318763.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
318763.obverse_description:
The design depicts the powerful and free movements of a dancer covered in a light flowing textile that accentuates the beauty and fluidity of the dancer's pose. It also bears the year of issuance ‘2022’ and at the top the indication of the issuing country ‘FI’ and the mintmark.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
318763.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

341111.name: 2 Euros (Climate Research)
341111.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
341111.obverse_description: Nature's own air quality meter – beard lichen – is depicted on the obverse side of this commemorative euro-currency coin. The relief is composed of a root-like branching network. The insightful motif can also be interpreted as representing the lungs – reminding us of the importance of air quality and its study. The pictorial theme has been designed by artist duo Tuominen Patel (Terhi Tuominen and Jitan V. Patel).
341111.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

331204.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
331204.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
331204.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
331204.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

353329.name: 2 Euros (First Finnish Nature Conservation Act)
353329.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
353329.obverse_description: Depicts a stylized beetle, surrounded by the inscription "Nature Conservation" in Finnish above and in Swedish below. The logo of the Mint of Finland (lion) on the middle left, the country abbreviation (FI for Finland) on the middle right, and the date below. The outer ring contains the twelve stars of the European Union.
353329.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.

12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

371497.name: 2 Euros (Social and Health Services)
371497.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
371497.obverse_description: Depicts a stylized map of Finland, surrounded by the inscription "Well being" in Finnish and Swedish. The date in the centre, the logo of the Mint of Finland (lion) below, the country abbreviation (FI for Finland) on the right. The outer ring contains the twelve stars of the European Union.
371497.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

401377.name: 2 Euros (Elections and Democracy)
401377.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
401377.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

432150.name: 2 Euros (Finnish architecture)
432150.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
432150.obverse_description: The buildings stretching towards the centre (the National Museum, Hvitträsk villa, Tallberg House and the Finnish Pavilion at the Paris World Exhibition 1900), as well as the names of the architects.
432150.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

481105.name: 2 Euro (Finland-Sweden Athletics International 100 years)
481105.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
481105.obverse_description: On the obverse, two legs are seen in movement, showcasing the element of competition. In the background, lines are moving in two directions. These lines can be interpreted as the two competing countries, Finland and Sweden.
481105.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

467198.name: 2 Euros (Diplomacy and Foreign Policy)
467198.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
467198.obverse_description:
The design features an abstract door symbolizing state visits, with a mirror-finish rectangular shape. The mintmark, the year 2025, and the lettering “FI” are positioned vertically along the left edge of the rectangle. The rectangle has a rounded top that follows the edge of the coin. Below and to the right, the surface is grooved with horizontal lines, while a circular shape intersects the curve of the image area.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
467198.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

104.name: 2 Euros (1st map)
104.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
104.obverse_description: A tree, symbolising life, continuity and growth, is contained in a hexagon and encircled by the French motto "LIBERTÉ ÉGALITÉ FRATERNITÉ" (liberty, equality, fraternity) and the twelve stars of Europe
104.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the fifteen states of the European Union

2161.name: 2 Euros (Treaty of Rome)
2161.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2161.obverse_description: The coin shows the Treaty document signed by the six founding countries on a background evoking the paving (designed by Michelangelo) of the Piazza del Campidoglio in Rome, where the signing took place on 25 March 1957. ‘Treaty of Rome 50 years’, ‘EUROPE’ and the name of the issuing country appear in the respective languages of the euro area or in Latin. Thus, the legend differs from country to country, but the image is the same. The Treaty of Rome established the European Economic Community and ultimately led to the introduction of the euro in 1999 and the euro banknotes and coins in 2002. The anniversary was celebrated on 25 March 2007. The euro area countries have marked the occasion by jointly issuing this commemorative coin. Its design was selected following a competition organised by the European mints. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
2161.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

6376.name: 2 Euros (2nd map)
6376.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
6376.obverse_description: A stylized tree in a hexagon symbolizes life, durability and growth. It is surrounded by the motto: "LIBERTÉ ÉGALITÉ FRATERNITÉ" and the twelve stars of the European Union linked by numerous lines.
6376.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

183033.name: 2 Euros (French Presidency of the EU; coloured)
183033.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
183033.obverse_description: He coin is inscribed as follows: ‘2008 PRÉSIDENCE FRANÇAISE UNION EUROPÉENNE RF’. The mint mark and the mintmaster's mark are located below, to the left and the right respectively. The twelve stars of the European Union are shown on the outer ring.
183033.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

3561.name: 2 Euros (French Presidency of the European Union)
3561.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
3561.obverse_description: The coin is inscribed as follows: ‘2008 PRÉSIDENCE FRANÇAISE UNION EUROPÉENNE RF’. The mint mark and the mintmaster's mark are located below, to the left and the right respectively. The twelve stars of the European Union are shown on the outer ring.
3561.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

5063.name: 2 Euros (Economic and Monetary Union)
5063.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5063.obverse_description: Stick figure which merges into the € symbol. Twelve stars of the European Union on outer ring.
5063.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

11526.name: 2 Euros (Speech of June 18th 1940)
11526.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
11526.obverse_description: The coin commemorates the 70th anniversary of the appeal (“Appel du 18 juin”) made by General de Gaulle – the leader of the Free French Forces – on the BBC in London on 18 June 1940, after the fall of France. Addressing the French people, de Gaulle declared that the war was not yet over. The speech is considered to mark the birth of the French Resistance and the beginning of its fight against the occupation of France during World War II. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
11526.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

22431.name: 2 Euros (Music Day)
22431.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
22431.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows a cheerful crowd, with a stylized image of a musical instrument and notes floating in the air, denoting the atmosphere of celebration on the Day of Music, which has been celebrated every summer solstice in France since 1981. The words "Fête de la MUSIQUE" and the date "21 JUIN 2011" appear at the center of the drawing. Along the upper edge, towards the right, are the words "30 e ANNIVERSAIRE", while the letters "RF" designate the country of issue at the bottom. The 12 stars of the European Union are depicted on the coin’s outer ring.
22431.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28330.name: 2 Euros (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28330.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28330.obverse_description: The world in the form of a euro symbol in the center, showing how the euro has become a truly global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolize the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28330.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

35856.name: 2 Euros (Birth of Abbé Pierre)
35856.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
35856.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows a portrait of Abbé Pierre wearing his well-known beret, positioned slightly off-centre to the left, and the logo of the foundation that bears his name to the right, including micro-lettering with the question “Et les autres?”, his favourite words to remind us that we should never forget to help others. The inscription “Centenaire de la naissance de l’abbé Pierre” forms an arch around the portrait along the edge of the coin’s inner part, flanked by the horn of plenty (mintmark of Monnaie de Paris) and the letters “RF” to denote the issuing country on the left and the “fleurette” (hallmark of the engraving workshop) on the right. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
35856.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

41904.name: 2 Euros (Élysée Treaty)
41904.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
41904.obverse_description: The coin, which was designed by Yves Sampo (Monnaie de Paris), Stefanie Lindner (Berlin State Mint), Alina Hoyer (Berlin) and Sneschana Russewa-Hoyer (Berlin), depicts stylised portraits of the signatories to the Élysée Treaty (Konrad Adenauer, then Chancellor of the Federal Republic of Germany, and Charles de Gaulle, former President of the French Republic) above their respective signatures on either side of the words “50 ANS JAHRE” and the year “2013” in the centre. The words “TRAITÉ DE L’ÉLYSÉE” and “ÉLYSÉE-VERTRAG” are centred along the top and bottom edge, respectively, of the inner part of the coin. On the right-hand inner edge below the portrait of Adenauer, the inner part also features the “fleurette” (hallmark of the engraving workshop) and the letters “RF” to denote the issuing country, while the mint mark is located on the coin’s left-hand inner edge below the portrait of de Gaulle. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
41904.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

45827.name: 2 Euros (Pierre de Coubertin)
45827.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
45827.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows a portrait of Pierre de Coubertin at a young age, set slightly off-centre to the right, with stylised Olympic rings in the background. They provide the framework for silhouettes symbolising the Olympic sports. To the left of the portrait, the letters “RF”, denoting the issuing country, are given above the year of issue “2013”. The name “PIERRE DE COUBERTIN” is inscribed along the top edge of the inner part of the coin. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
45827.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

60800.name: 2 Euros (D-Day)
60800.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
60800.obverse_description: In the coin’s central field the word D-DAY is written in such a way as to depict a landing craft and a tank gun barrel. The years 1944-2014 appear above the tank gun, with the inscription ‘70e anniversaire du débarquement’ (70th anniversary of the landings) further down. The distinctive footprints left by the boots worn by American, British and Canadian troops are gradually disappearing in the sand, washed away by a wave. The words of a poem by Verlaine used as a code for the start of the landings are engraved on the wave: ‘Les sanglots longs des violons de l’automne blessent mon coeur d’une langueur monotone’ (the long sobs of the violins of autumn wound my heart with a monotonous languor). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
60800.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

67264.name: 2 Euros (World AIDS Day)
67264.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
67264.obverse_description: The red ribbon, symbolising the fight against AIDS, is featured in the coin’s central field. The ribbon was created by the Visual AIDS Artists Caucus and the US painter Frank Moore. It should be worn close to the heart to symbolise solidarity with AIDS sufferers and is in the shape of an upside-down V. The idea is for it to be worn as a V one day to signify victory over the disease. There are three ribbons in the central field. One is in the conventional position and will be coloured red for the brilliant uncirculated (BU) and proof (PRF) versions. The other two ribbons are inverted and associated with two Vs to strengthen the symbolism of the much-hoped-for victory over the disease. The date 1 December, Unesco World AIDS Day, appears at the top of the central field. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
67264.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

67424.name: 2 Euros (World AIDS Day; Coloured)
67424.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
67424.obverse_description: The red ribbon, symbolising the fight against AIDS, is featured in the coin’s central field. The ribbon was created by the Visual AIDS Artists Caucus and the US painter Frank Moore. It should be worn close to the heart to symbolise solidarity with AIDS sufferers and is in the shape of an upside-down V. The idea is for it to be worn as a V one day to signify victory over the disease. There are three ribbons in the central field. One is in the conventional position and will be coloured red for the brilliant uncirculated (BU) and proof (PRF) versions. The other two ribbons are inverted and associated with two Vs to strengthen the symbolism of the much-hoped-for victory over the disease. The date 1 December, Unesco World AIDS Day, appears at the top of the central field. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
67424.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

76635.name: 2 Euros (30 Years of European Union Flag)
76635.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76635.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
76635.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

78890.name: 2 Euros (30 Years of European Union Flag; Coloured)
78890.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
78890.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag colored in blue as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
78890.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

74223.name: 2 Euros (Festival of the Federation)
74223.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
74223.obverse_description: The French national holiday brings the people of France together in celebration of the Republic. The design shows Marianne, the symbol of the Republic, depicted in profile in a graphic, contemporary style. She is wearing the Phrygian cap. On the right the tricolour cockade appears in outline above the letters RF. The year is written in the centre of the coin. The left side features a stanza from the poem ‘Liberté’ by the French poet Paul Eluard, an allusion to the motto of the French Republic. The mint marks appear alongside this extract. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
74223.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

77126.name: 2 Euros (Festival of the Federation; Coloured)
77126.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
77126.obverse_description: The French national holiday brings the people of France together in celebration of the Republic. The design shows Marianne, the symbol of the Republic, depicted in profile in a graphic, contemporary style. She is wearing the Phrygian cap. On the right the colored tricolour cockade appears in outline above the letters RF. The year is written in the centre of the coin. The left side features a stanza from the poem ‘Liberté’ by the French poet Paul Eluard, an allusion to the motto of the French Republic. The mint marks appear alongside this extract. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
77126.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

69366.name: 2 Euros (Peace)
69366.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
69366.obverse_description: In the coin’s central field is a modern graphic representation of a dove. It is carrying an olive branch, the symbol of peace, whose branches have been replaced by the 12 stars of the European flag. The 28 countries of the European Union are represented by the ISO code for each country. The letters RF (République Française) are at the bottom of the coin. The mint marks are on the left-hand side and the year 2015 on the right. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
69366.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

91431.name: 2 Euros (François Mitterrand)
91431.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
91431.obverse_description: The design shows a profile of a pensive François Mitterrand. Adjacent is his personal emblem of a half oak/half olive tree. Above are the dates marking the centenary of his birth ('1916' and '2016') and his name. At the bottom, the indication of the issuing country 'RF'.
91431.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

82237.name: 2 Euros (EURO 2016 Football Championship)
82237.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
82237.obverse_description: The European Football Championship 2016 will be held in France between 10 June and 10 July. It will be the fifteenth European tournament, where 24 national teams will be competing. The design shows, among other figures, a football and the trophy of the European Cup in relief.
82237.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

102697.name: 2 Euros (Auguste Rodin)
102697.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
102697.obverse_description: The letters RF, standing for République Française is drawn as sculpted on the top of the coin and the name of the artist, ‘A. Rodin’ and the relevant dates ‘1917-2017’ are inscribed in his beard with the 12 stars of the European Union in the outer ring
102697.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

122799.name: 2 Euros (Pink ribbon)
122799.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
122799.obverse_description: A woman's bust symbolically protected by a hand and the ribbon surrounded, at the right side, by the inscription '25e ANNIVERSAIRE DU RUBAN ROSE' and the years'1992 - 2017' to the right along with the indication of the issuing country 'RF' and the mintmark. The coin's outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
122799.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

124498.name: 2 Euros (Pink Ribbon; Coloured)
124498.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
124498.obverse_description:
Since the 1990s, the fight against breast cancer has been a major cause around the world. On the occasion of the 25th anniversary of the pink ribbon, iconic symbol of this fight, Monnaie de Paris has decided to support this cause. For nearly 15 years, a pink ribbon prize has been presented every year to support research efforts and innovation.
The design represents a woman's bust symbolically protected by a hand and the coloured ribbon. It is surrounded, at the right side, by the inscription '25e ANNIVERSAIRE DU RUBAN ROSE' (25th anniversary of the pink ribbon). The right side also features the years '1992 - 2017', the indication of the issuing country 'RF' and the mintmarks.
The coin's outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
124498.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

134685.name: 2 Euros (Bleuet de France)
134685.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
134685.obverse_description: The design features the cornflower in the center of the coin. It is surrounded by the words ‘Le Bleuet de France, fleur de mémoire et de solidarité’ (Bleuet of France, flower of remembrance and solidarity). The dates ‘1918-2018’ as well as the letters RF for République Française (French Republic) and the mint marks are also included in the design.
134685.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

134283.name: 2 Euros (Bleuet de France; Coloured)
134283.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
134283.obverse_description: The design features the coloured cornflower in the center of the coin. It is surrounded by the words ‘Le Bleuet de France, fleur de mémoire et de solidarité’ (Bleuet of France, flower of remembrance and solidarity). The dates ‘1918-2018’ as well as the letters RF for République Française (French Republic) and the mint marks are also included in the design.
134283.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

141382.name: 2 Euros (Simone Veil)
141382.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
141382.obverse_description:
The design displays a portrait of Simone Veil, an icon in the combat for women’s rights.
Simone Veil, born Jacob, left us in June 2017 at the age of 89. After surviving deportation to Auschwitz, she went on
to become one of the most important actors in Europe’s building. She was the first elected president of the European
Parliament from 1979 to 1982. She symbolizes the depenalization of abortion she fought for with the law bearing her
name voted in 1975. In 2008, she is elected at the French Academy.
Her deportation registration number appears on her collar. The background depicts the European Parliament on which
her name, key dates, indication of the country ‘RF’, year of issuance ‘2018’ and the mintmarks are inscribed.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
141382.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

160246.name: 2 Euros (Berlin Wall)
160246.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
160246.obverse_description:
Thirty years ago, the Berlin Wall fell. Beyond the impact on German society, it was an event with global consequences. On November 9th, 1989, this occasion marks the end of the cold war and announces an appeasement after several decades of international tensions. At the European level, it is the first step towards the reunification of one of the leading countries of the European Union. This major change was after materialized by the ‘Two Plus Four’ Treaty (Moscow, 12 September 1990) of which France was a signatory. The European Union welcomed then 16 million new citizens in just one year; almost the structural and economic effect of welcoming a new country!

The design represents the Berlin Wall, opened in its centre, letting the doves (allegories of the international appeasement), and the jubilant crowd pass. In the background, the Brandenburg Gate, Berlin’s symbol par excellence, is represented. The mention ‘30 years of the fall of the Berlin Wall/30 Jahre Mauerfall’ is inscribed on one side of the wall. At the bottom of the design is the year of issuance ‘2019’ and at its right the indication of the issuing country ‘RF’ (standing for République Française). The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
160246.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

167978.name: 2 Euros (Astérix)
167978.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
167978.obverse_description: Asterix surrounded by laurels and Roman inscriptions referring to 60 years of the series. The inscription "ASTERIX" is above along with the monogram is the issuing country below. The date is at the bottom along with the mintmark and mintmaster mark. The outer ring has the 12 stars of the European flag.
167978.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

192990.name: 2 Euros (General Charles de Gaulle)
192990.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
192990.obverse_description:
The design represents two profiles of Charles de Gaulle at two different times. The profile in the background shows Charles de Gaulle in army general is uniform during the call to arms of the 18th June 1940 or during the liberation of Paris. The profile in the foreground represents Charles de Gaulle during his second presidential term of office. Finally, the mention RF is semi-integrated in a Lorraine cross, symbol of free France chosen by General de Gaulle in 1940. His dates of birth and death as well as the year date are also inscribed in the Lorraine cross.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
192990.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

239405.name: 2 Euros (Medical Research)
239405.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
239405.obverse_description: The obverse of the coin represents the figuration of the human in union, represented by a face inscribed in a sphere. It faces the infinitely small, represented by fragments of DNA inscribed in a circle. By its oblique axis, this allegory represents the research and its intelligence dominating the infinitely small; studying it and triumphing over the diseases.
239405.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

275891.name: 2 Euros (UNICEF)
275891.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
275891.obverse_description:
The design shows an allegory of the work of ‘UNICEF’ where the hands are a key element. One after the other they support the globe and are the link between the different areas of the Earth. The mention of the 75th anniversary as well as the laurels crown the ensemble accompanied by the acronym RF. On the upper rim, the name ‘UNICEF’ is inserted along with the relevant dates as well as the UNICEF motto ‘For each child’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
275891.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

304119.name: 2 Euros (Olympic Games, Paris 2024 - France 2021)
304119.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
304119.obverse_description:
The design represents Marianne, national figure and icon of French numismatics, running in an ‘old’ way in reference to the Olympic Games of the Ancient Era. Its silhouette blends with the Eiffel Tower, a key element of Parisian heritage, to form a common axis with the Iron Lady. In the background, an athletics track in which the emblem of Paris 2024 is inserted on the left side, is depicted. The year date, the mention RF and the mintmarks are inserted under the arch of the tower.
The coins outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
304119.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

318748.name: 2 Euros (Jacques Chirac)
318748.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
318748.obverse_description:
The design shows a solemn profile of President Jacques Chirac looking towards the future. He is surrounded by several symbols representing his actions: a euro symbol, as sign of his involvement in the introduction of the Euro and his European spirit, and a French flag represented in heraldic colours, itself embellished with the RF as a reference to his presidency. His dates and name are inserted in the Euro’s logo. The mintmarks as well as the year date fit into the design.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
318748.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

331022.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
331022.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
331022.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
331022.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

341320.name: 2 Euros (Olympic Games, Paris 2024 - France 2022)
341320.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
341320.obverse_description:
The design represents the genius, a national figure and icon of French numismatics. It is depicted practicing discus throwing in an ‘ancient’ way in reference to the Olympic Games of the Antique era. His silhouette blends with the one of the Arc de Triomphe, a key element of Parisian heritage. It forms a common axis with this emblematic monument of victory. In the background, an athletics track in which the emblem of Paris 2024 is inserted on the right side, is represented. The year date, the mention RF and the mintmarks are inserted under the arch and around the foot of the Arc.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
341320.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

314962.name: 2 Euros (2nd type)
314962.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
314962.obverse_description: Depicts a tree with leaves and fruits of the oak and the olive tree. In the background is the hexagon, which symbolizes the outline of the map of France, in the upper three leaves the republican motto "Freedom, Equality, Fraternity". Below the monogram "RF" of the French Republic, on the left the year of issue "2022" and on the lower edge of the hexagon next to the trunk the mint mark of the mint on the left, the mint mark of the engraver general on the right and this name above. The twelve stars of the European flag are depicted on the outer ring.
314962.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders

352565.name: 2 Euros (Olympic Games, Paris 2024 - France 2023)
352565.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
352565.obverse_description:
The design of the coin depicts the Sower, a national figure and icon of French numismatics, practicing pugilism, the forerunner of boxing, in reference to the Antic Olympic Games. Her silhouette is in the foreground in front of the PontNeuf and its surroundings, typical of the Ile de la Cité area, a key element of the Parisian landscape. In the background, an athletic track, into which the emblem of Paris 2024 is inserted on the right-hand side, is represented. The year date, the RF mention and the mintmarks are inserted under the arch, on the bridge railing and in the Seine.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
352565.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

369820.name: 2 Euros (Rugby World Cup 2023)
369820.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
369820.obverse_description: Depicts a stylized portrayal of a rugby player sprinting with the rugby ball amidst the goalposts positioned at both ends of the field. The field itself is represented by a stylized globe, serving as a symbol for the global nature of the event. Positioned above the image is the caption "2023 Rugby World Cup in France". In the lower left corner, the official Rugby Union logo, while the lower right corner features the monogram "RF" (French Republic) along with the mintmarks of the mint and engraver. The twelve stars of the European flag are shown on the outer ring of the coin.
369820.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

394012.name: 2 Euros (Olympic Games, Paris 2024 - France 2024)
394012.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
394012.obverse_description:
The design represents Hercules, a national figure and icon of French numismatics, practicing antique wrestling with Notre-Dame’s Chimera, in reference to the Olympic Games of the Ancient era. Both wrestlers are depicted in the foreground in front of Notre-Dame Cathedral. In the background, an athletics track in which the emblem of Paris 2024 is inserted on the left side, is represented. The yeardate, the RF indication, as well as the mintmarks are inserted on the athletics track.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
394012.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

403198.name: 2 Euros (JO 2024 Paris)
403198.composition: Bimetallic: nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
403198.obverse_description: The Eiffel Tower in motion as an athlete in a race. To the left a Tiare flower, to the right the Notre Dame de la Garde Basilica. Surrounded by the 12 stars of the European Flag.
403198.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

419643.name: 2 Euros (Olympic Games, Paris; Coloured)
419643.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
419643.obverse_description: Stylistic design of the Eiffel Tower made to look like a running athlete.
419643.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

455096.name: 2 Euros (Louvre Museum)
455096.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
455096.obverse_description: Perrault's Colonnade, the eastern façade of the building overlooking the church of Saint-Germain-l'Auxerrois. This exterior façade is integrated into the museum's plan, in which the "RF", the vintage and the different are placed. In the background, the Louvre pyramid stands out with its structure, which squares the background of the coin. The outer ring of the coin represents the twelve stars of the European flag.
455096.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

521871.name: 2 Euros (Notre Dame de Paris)
521871.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
521871.obverse_description:
West facade of Notre-Dame Cathedral in Paris, with its rose window emerging between the two towers.
The coin's outer ring represents the twelve stars of the European flag.
521871.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

556437.name: 2 Euros (The Little Prince)
556437.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
556437.obverse_description: The face of this coin features the logo celebrating the 80th anniversary of the release of The Little Prince in France. The Little Prince is shown pensive. Behind him is "80", a reference to the anniversary. Above him is "Le Petit Prince", along with "RF" for République Française and the two hallmarks. Below the Little Prince is the year 2026.
556437.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

284509.name: 2 Euro (Pattern)
284509.obverse_description: The eagle, the traditional symbol of German sovereignty, is surrounded with the twelve stars of Europe.
284509.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the gathering of the fifteen states of the European Union

112.name: 2 Euros (1st map)
112.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
112.obverse_description: The eagle, the traditional symbol of German sovereignty, is surrounded with the twelve stars of Europe.
112.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the gathering of the fifteen states of the European Union.

2169.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Schleswig-Holstein")
2169.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2169.obverse_description: The Holstentor, the gate symbolising of the town of Lübeck, is shown in the centre of the coin and below it the word ‘Schleswig-Holstein’, the first of Germany’s 16 federal states to be commemorated in a euro coin. The engraver’s initials ‘HH’ appear to the right of the image and the mintmark ‘A’, ‘D’, ‘F’, ‘G’ or ‘J’, depending on the mint, to the left. The year of mintage, 2006, the 12 stars of the European Union and the words ‘Bundesrepublik Deutschland’ are shown in the outer ring.
2169.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2162.name: 2 Euros (Treaty of Rome)
2162.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
