2162.obverse_description: The coin shows the Treaty document signed by the six founding countries on a background evoking the paving (designed by Michelangelo) of the Piazza del Campidoglio in Rome, where the signing took place on 25 March 1957. ‘Treaty of Rome 50 years’, ‘EUROPE’ and the name of the issuing country appear in the respective languages of the euro area or in Latin. Thus, the legend differs from country to country, but the image is the same. The Treaty of Rome established the European Economic Community and ultimately led to the introduction of the euro in 1999 and the euro banknotes and coins in 2002. The anniversary was celebrated on 25 March 2007. The euro area countries have marked the occasion by jointly issuing this commemorative coin. Its design was selected following a competition organised by the European mints. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
2162.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2201.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Mecklenburg-Vorpommern")
2201.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2201.obverse_description: The coin shows Schwerin Castle and bears the inscription ‘Mecklenburg-Vorpommern’ - the federal state of Mecklenburg-West Pomerania where the castle is located. The mintmark, ‘A’, ‘D’, ‘F’, ‘G’ or ‘J’, appears above the image, and the engraver’s initials ‘HH’ below it. The year of mintage, 2007, the 12 stars of the European Union and the words ‘Bundesrepublik Deutschland’ appear in the outer ring.
2201.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2994.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Hamburg")
2994.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2994.obverse_description:
The coin depicts the baroque church of St. Michaelis, or “Michel” as it is popularly known. It is a symbol of the city and federal state of Hamburg, as the inscription below the image indicates. The year of issue, 2008, the 12 stars of the European Union and the words ‘BUNDESREPUBLIK DEUTSCHLAND’ appear on the outer ring. The mintmark is shown in the upper right part of the coin.

Church also known as Sankt Michaelis Kirche / St. Michaelis Kirche / Hamburger Michel
2994.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

327881.name: 2 Euros Bundesländer - "Hamburg" (Mule)
327881.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
327881.obverse_description: The coin depicts the baroque church of St. Michaelis, or “Michel” as it is popularly known. It is a symbol of the city and federal state of Hamburg, as the inscription below the image indicates. The year of issue, 2008, the 12 stars of the European Union and the words ‘BUNDESREPUBLIK DEUTSCHLAND’ appear on the outer ring. The mintmark is shown in the upper right part of the coin.
327881.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the fifteen states of the European Union

6323.name: 2 Euros (2nd map)
6323.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
6323.obverse_description: The eagle, the traditional symbol of German sovereignty, is surrounded with the twelve stars of Europe.
6323.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the gathering of the fifteen states of the European Union

5053.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Saarland")
5053.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5053.obverse_description: The coin features the Ludwigskirche, a symbol of Saarbrücken, the capital of the Saarland. It shows the distinctive east facade and bell tower of the church, with the word ‘SAARLAND’ and ‘J’, a mint mark, underneath. It was designed by Friedrich Brenner, whose initials appear to the right of the image. The year of mintage, 2009, the 12 stars of the European Union and the words ‘BUNDESREPUBLIK DEUTSCHLAND’ are depicted on the outer ring.
5053.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

5054.name: 2 Euros (Economic and Monetary Union)
5054.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5054.obverse_description: Stick figure which merges into the € symbol. Twelve stars of the European Union on outer ring.
5054.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

10069.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Bremen")
10069.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
10069.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin features the Town Hall of Bremen, with the Roland statue in the foreground. The word “BREMEN” is inscribed beneath the Town Hall to the right of the statue. The mint mark, represented by the letter “A”, “D”, “F”, “G” or “J”, appears at the left above the Town Hall. The initials of the artist, Bodo Broschat, are given at the very bottom, immediately below the statue. The name of the issuing country, “D”, and the year, “2010”, are inserted at the top and bottom of the outer ring of the coin respectively, interspersed between the twelve stars of the European Union.
10069.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

18666.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Nordrhein-Westfalen")
18666.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
18666.obverse_description: The Köln (Cologne) Cathedral in its entirety, a masterpiece of the German Gothic architectural style, emphasizing the beauty of the south portal. The name NORDRHEIN-WESTFALEN, just below the building, links the pictured building with the state. The mint mark is located in the right upper core region, the initials of the artist, Heinz Hoyer, in the right central core area. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union flag. Between the stars, at the bottom, the year ‘2011’ and at the top the indication of the issuing country reference ‘D’.
18666.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

29694.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Bayern")
29694.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
29694.obverse_description:
The commemorative ‘Bavaria’ was designed by Erich Ott and shows the most famous view of Neuschwanstein Castle. The design shows the view from the east as visitors see it during their ascent to the castle, with the gatehouse in the foreground and the medieval-style towers and turrets. The panoramic mountain situated behind the castle is well captured, as the site of the castle on a cliff overlooking the romantic Pöllatschlucht. The name ‘BAYERN’ links the depicted construction ‘Neuschwanstein Castle’ with the state. The mintmark of the respective mint (A, D, F, G or J) is located in the right central core area and the initials of the artist in the left central core area.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union flag. Between the stars, at the bottom, the year ‘2012’ and at the top the indication of the issuing country ‘D’.
29694.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28321.name: 2 Euros (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28321.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28321.obverse_description: Coin features the world in the form of a euro symbol in the centre, showing how the euro has become a true global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolise the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28321.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

42149.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Baden-Württemberg")
42149.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
42149.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin depicts the Maulbronn Monastery which is home to the most perfectly preserved medieval monastery complex north of the Alps. Founded in 1147, this complex has been a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1993. On the top, the year of issuance ‘2013’. At the bottom, the inscription BADEN-WÜRTTEMBERG and underneath the indication of the issuing country ‘D’. The mint mark, represented by the letter A, D, F, G or J, is located at the right hand side. At the bottom left the initials of the artist. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
42149.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

41786.name: 2 Euros (Élysée Treaty)
41786.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
41786.obverse_description: The coin, which was designed by Yves Sampo (Monnaie de Paris), Stefanie Lindner (Berlin State Mint), Alina Hoyer (Berlin) and Sneschana Russewa-Hoyer (Berlin), depicts stylised portraits of the signatories to the Élysée Treaty (Konrad Adenauer, then Chancellor of the Federal Republic of Germany, and Charles de Gaulle, former President of the French Republic) above their respective signatures on either side of the words “50 ANS JAHRE” and the year “2013” in the centre. The words “TRAITÉ DE L’ÉLYSÉE” and “ÉLYSÉE-VERTRAG” are centred along the top and bottom edge, respectively, of the inner part of the coin. On the right-hand inner edge below the portrait of Adenauer, the inner part also features the mint mark (“A”, “D”, “F”, “G” or “J”, depending on the mint concerned), as well as the letter “D” to denote the issuing country. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
41786.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

53570.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Niedersachsen")
53570.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
53570.obverse_description: The design shows the Church of St. Michael in Hildesheim, Lower Saxony which has been on the Unesco World Cultural Heritage list since 1985. At the top of the year of issuance ‘2014’ and at the left the mintmark (A, D, F, G, J). At the bottom, the inscription ‘NIEDERSACHSEN’ and underneath the indication of the issuing country ‘D’. At the top right, the initials of the engraver OE (Ott Erich). The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
53570.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

69263.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Hessen")
69263.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
69263.obverse_description: A classic perspective on St. Paul’s Church in Frankfurt (the ‘Paulskirche', the seat of Germany’s first freely elected legislative body in 1849 and is regarded as the cradle of German democracy) and is the perspective possessing the greatest recognition value. The design incisively brings out the tension between the dominant tower and the elliptical structure of the church. The slightly exaggerated flight of steps has an inviting quality while simultaneously offering support to the subjacent inscription ‘HESSEN’ (the Federal State of Hessen in which St. Paul’s Church is situated). The inner part also features the date and mintmark to the left and the engraver’s mark (the initials ‘HH’ — Heinz Hoyer) to the right. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
69263.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

69264.name: 2 Euros (25 Years of German Unity)
69264.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
69264.obverse_description: The people in the foreground, who embody a new beginning and the advance towards a better future, are standing in front of the Brandenburg Gate, a symbol of German unity. The rendering of the statement Wir sind ein Volk (‘we are one people’) — a collective expression of will by German citizens – represents the path forwards towards German reunification. The inner part also features the mint mark of the respective mint (‘A’, ‘D’, ‘F’, ‘G’ or ‘J’) as well as the issuing country’s country code ‘D’ and the engraver’s mark (the initials ‘BW’ — Bernd Wendhut). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
69264.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

76632.name: 2 Euros (30 Years of European Union Flag)
76632.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76632.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
76632.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

284594.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Hessen" - Pattern)
284594.obverse_description: A classic perspective on St. Paul’s Church in Frankfurt (the ‘Paulskirche', the seat of Germany’s first freely elected legislative body in 1849 and is regarded as the cradle of German democracy) and is the perspective possessing the greatest recognition value. The design incisively brings out the tension between the dominant tower and the elliptical structure of the church. The slightly exaggerated flight of steps has an inviting quality while simultaneously offering support to the subjacent inscription ‘HESSEN’ (the Federal State of Hessen in which St. Paul’s Church is situated). The inner part also features the date and mintmark to the left and the engraver’s mark (the initials ‘HH’ — Heinz Hoyer) to the right. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
284594.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

80927.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Sachsen")
80927.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
80927.obverse_description: The design shows a view from the inner yard of the world-famous Dresden Zwinger to the Crown Gate. The inner part also features the name ‘SACHSEN’ and the issuing country’s country code ‘D’ at the bottom, the mint mark of the respective mint (‘A’, ‘D’, ‘F’, ‘G’ or ‘J’) as well as the engraver’s mark (the initials ‘JT’ — Jordi Truxa) right at the top and the year ‘2016’ left at the top.
80927.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

102445.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Rheinland-Pfalz")
102445.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
102445.obverse_description: The Porta Nigra in Trier which is probably the best-preserved Roman city gate north of the Alps. The inner part also features the name 'RHEINLAND-PFALZ' and the mintmark to the left and the date at the top along with the initials of the designer 'CH' (Chocola Frantisek) to the right and the coin's outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
102445.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

132936.name: 2 Euros (Helmut Schmidt)
132936.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
132936.obverse_description: The coin represents Helmut Schmidt in a characteristic pose, engaged in dialogue with his interlocutor. At the top right, in a semicircle, the words "HELMUT SCHMIDT"; on the right the years "1918-2015" and below one of the 5 mint signs "A", "D", "F", "G" or "J"; on the left the "D" sign, which identifies Germany, and below the year of issue "2018"; below are the initials of the artist "BB" (Bodo Broschat).
132936.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

132935.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Berlin")
132935.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
132935.obverse_description: The coin shows Charlottenburg Palace. On the left, the year of minting, "2018"; on the right, the "D" sign for Germany; in the lower center, the word "BERLIN" and one of the 5 mint signs "A", "D", "F", "G" or "J"; in the lower left, the initials "BB" of the engraver Bodo Broschat.
132935.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

139058.name: 2 Euros (Bundesrat)
139058.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
139058.obverse_description:
The design shows a highly detailed and finely sculpted rendering of the Bundesrat building. The upper half of the coin’s inner section includes the mint mark of the respective mint (‘A’, ‘D’, ‘F’, ‘G’ or ‘J’), the artist’s initials and the year ‘2019’. The lower half of the coin’s inner section contains the inscription ‘BUNDESRAT’ and Germany’s issuing country code ‘D’.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
139058.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

180626.name: 2 Euros (Berlin Wall)
180626.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
180626.obverse_description:
The design represents the Berlin Wall, opened in its centre, letting the doves (allegories of the international appeasement), and the jubilant crowd pass. In the background, the Brandenburg Gate, Berlin’s symbol par excellence, is represented. The
mention ‘30 Jahre Mauerfall’ (30 years of the fall of the Berlin Wall) is inscribed on one side of the wall. At the bottom of
the design is the year of issuance ‘2019’ and at its right the indication of the issuing country ‘D’. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
180626.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

193506.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Brandenburg")
193506.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
193506.obverse_description:
The design shows the Sanssouci Palace. The upper half of the coin’s inner section includes the mint mark of the respective mint (‘A’, ‘D’, ‘F’, ‘G’ or ‘J’), the artist’s initials and the year ‘2020’. The lower half of the coin’s
inner section contains the inscription ‘BRANDENBURG’ and Germany’s issuing country code ‘D’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
193506.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

267621.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Sachsen-Anhalt")
267621.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
267621.obverse_description:
The design shows the Magdeburg Cathedral, the first Gothic-style cathedral to be constructed on German soil. The inner part also features the name ‘SACHSEN-ANHALT’ and the issuing country’s country code ‘D’ at the bottom, the mint mark of the respective mint (‘A’, ‘D’, ‘F’, ‘G’ or ‘J’) as well as the the year ‘2021’ on the left and the engraver’s mark on the right.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
267621.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

314709.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer - "Thüringen")
314709.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
314709.obverse_description: Angled side view of Castle Wartburg with keep.
314709.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

324867.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
324867.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
324867.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
324867.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

356441.name: 2 Euros (Charlemagne)
356441.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
356441.obverse_description: In the center figures Charlemagne's monogram over the octagon of Aachen Cathedral. The upper part shows the lettering "KARL DER GROẞE" (Charlemagne), the lower part the year of issue "2023", the right side the "D" for the issuing country followed by the mintmark of the respective mint ("A", "D", "F", "G" or "J") and the left side the initials of the artist Tobias Winnen ("TW"). The whole design is surronded by the twelve stars of the European Union on the outer ring.
356441.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

352712.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer II - "Hamburg")
352712.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
352712.obverse_description:
The design shows the Elbphilharmonie concert hall, Hamburg’s newest landmark. The impressive and detailed representation of the concert building against the background of the maritime urban landscape represents the Land of Hamburg in an exceptionally convincing manner. The left half of the coin’s inner section includes Germany’s issuing country code, ‘D’, the year ‘2023’ and the mintmark of the respective mint (‘A’, ‘D’, ‘F’, ‘G’ or ‘J’). The right half features the artist’s initials, and the lower part features the inscription ‘HAMBURG’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
352712.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

394629.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer II - "Mecklenburg-Vorpommern")
394629.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
394629.obverse_description: The Königsstuhl, an emblematic chalk cliff of this region of the north-east German region
394629.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

396761.name: 2 Euros (Constitution of St. Paul's Church)
396761.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
396761.obverse_description: In the foreground, a perspective representation of the Paulskirche memorial site, behind it the paper constitutional document and quill. In the back three female allegories of unity, justice and freedom and the tricolor of black, red and gold.
396761.reverse_description: Large inscription of the value superimposed over the map of Western Europe. Twelve stars on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map and six stars below the map with six vertical stripes cutting across the inner core of the coin connecting the upper and lower star segments.

439766.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer II - "Saarland")
439766.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
439766.obverse_description: Meandering river motif of the Saarschleife (Saar loop) in bird's eye view with wooded hilly landscape in the background. Artist's mark on the upper left, mint mark on the right. Nationality mark next to the year at the bottom.
439766.reverse_description: Geographical map of Western Europe spanning outer ring and inner core on the right side. Denomination is superimposed over map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located on the left of it. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

448790.name: 2 Euros (German Reunification)
448790.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
448790.obverse_description: Text "35 Jahre Deutsche Einheit Wir Sind Ein Volk" split into two parts on the right and left side of the coin, connected by a big letter D in the middle. Two lines representing the Berlin Wall underneath and above the text on each side. Artist's mark on the upper middle, nationality mark on the lower middle. Issuing year underneath of nationality, mint mark is underneath of artist's mark.
448790.reverse_description: Geographical map of Western Europe spanning outer ring and inner core on the right side. Denomination is superimposed over map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located on the left of it. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

544530.name: 2 Euros (Konrad Adenauer)
544530.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
544530.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

474550.name: 2 Euros (Bundesländer II - "Bremen")
474550.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
474550.obverse_description: Klimahaus Bremerhaven, seen from the harbour.
474550.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

120.name: 2 Euros (1st map)
120.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
120.obverse_description: A representation of the myth of the Abduction of Europa, the daughter of the Phoenician King Agenor, by Zeus, who had taken the form of a bull, is accompanied with the legend "Ευρώπη" (Europa) and the face value and encircled by the twelve stars of Europe.
120.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the fifteen states of the European Union.

2190.name: 2 Euros (Olympic Games)
2190.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2190.obverse_description: The twelve stars of the European Union positioned around the outer circle surround the design of an ancient statue depicting a discobolus in his attempt to throw the discus. The base of the statue covers a small part of the coin's external ring (outer part). To the left is the logo of the Olympic Games 'ATHENS 2004' and the five Olympic circles, and to the right, one above the other, are the figure '2' and the word 'ΕΥΡΩ'. The yearmark is written in split form around the star positioned bottom centre, as follows: 20*04 and the mintmark is above the athlete's head to the left.
2190.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2213.name: 2 Euros (Treaty of Rome)
2213.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2213.obverse_description: The coin shows the Treaty document signed by the six founding countries on a background evoking the paving (designed by Michelangelo) of the Piazza del Campidoglio in Rome, where the signing took place on 25 March 1957. ‘Treaty of Rome 50 years’, ‘EUROPE’ and the name of the issuing country appear in the respective languages of the euro area or in Latin. Thus, the legend differs from country to country, but the image is the same. The Treaty of Rome established the European Economic Community and ultimately led to the introduction of the euro in 1999 and the euro banknotes and coins in 2002. The anniversary was celebrated on 25 March 2007. The euro area countries have marked the occasion by jointly issuing this commemorative coin. Its design was selected following a competition organised by the European mints. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
2213.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

6353.name: 2 Euros (2nd map)
6353.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
6353.obverse_description: A representation of the myth of the Abduction of Europa, the daughter of the Phoenician King Agenor, by Zeus, who had taken the form of a bull, is accompanied with the legend "Ευρώπη" (Europa) and the face value and encircled by the twelve stars of Europe
6353.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the states of the European Union

5068.name: 2 Euros (Economic and Monetary Union)
5068.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5068.obverse_description: Stick figure which merges into the € symbol. Twelve stars of the European Union on outer ring.
5068.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

16063.name: 2 Euros (Battle of Marathon)
16063.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
16063.obverse_description: The coin commemorates the 2500th anniversary of the Battle of Marathon. The centre of the coin shows a synthesis of a shield and a runner/warrior representing the struggle for freedom and the noble ideals that the Battle of Marathon stands for. The bird on the shield symbolizes the birth of Western civilization in its present form. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
16063.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

22333.name: 2 Euros (Special Olympics)
22333.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
22333.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows the emblem of the Games, a radiant sun, the source of life that underlines the excellence and power of the athletes who take part in the Games, with excellence represented by the olive branch and power by the spiral starting from the center of the sun. Inscribed around the outer right-hand edge of the inner part are the caption “XIII SPECIAL OLYMPICS W.S.G. ATHENS 2011” and the name of the issuing country “ΕΛΛΗΝΙΚΗ ΔΗΜΟΚΡΑΤΙΑ”, separated from one another by the mintmark. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
22333.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28102.name: 2 Euros (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28102.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28102.obverse_description: Coin features the world in the form of a euro symbol in the centre, showing how the euro has become a true global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolise the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28102.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

49903.name: 2 Euros (Platonic Academy)
49903.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
49903.obverse_description: Portrait of Plato (in profile). On the left: the words ‘2 400 years since the founding of Plato’s Academy’ and ‘The Hellenic Republic’ in ancient Greek lettering. On the right: ‘2013’ and the monogram of the Greek Mint. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
49903.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

49944.name: 2 Euros (Crete)
49944.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
49944.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin depicts Cretan rebels raising the Greek flag, a symbolic representation of Crete’s struggle for Union with Greece. On the upper side, in circular sense and with capital letters the name of the issuing country ‘Hellenic Republic’ in Greek. Underneath the words: ‘100 years of the union of Crete with Greece’ in Greek. On the right: ‘1913-2013’ and the monogram of the Greek Mint. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
49944.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

65017.name: 2 Euros (Domenikos Theotokopoulos)
65017.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
65017.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows a portrait of Domenikos Theotokopoulos. The background features a typical figure from his work, illustrative of his technique. On the left: the year 2014 and the characteristic signature of the artist (‘Domenikos Theotokopoulos Epoiei’). On the right: the mint mark of the Greek State Mint. The entire design is encircled in an inscription reading in Greek ‘DOMENIKOS THEOTOKOPOULOS 1541-1614’ and the name of the issuing country ‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
65017.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

65018.name: 2 Euros (Ionian Islands)
65018.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
65018.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin depicts a seven-pointed star inscribed in Greek with the words: ‘150 YEARS SINCE THE UNION OF THE IONIAN ISLANDS (EPTÁNISA) WITH GREECE 1864-2014’, the name of the issuing country ‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’ and the mint mark of the Greek State Mint. The spaces between the points of the star feature the symbols of the Ionian Islands. The entire design is encircled in a decorative stylised wave motif. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
65018.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

76539.name: 2 Euros (Spyridon Louis)
76539.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76539.obverse_description: The design shows Spyros Louis and the cup that he was awarded, against the background with the Panathenaic Stadium. Inscribed along the inner edge is the country of issuance ‘ΕΛΛΗΝΙΚΗ ΔΗΜΟΚΡΑΤΙΑ’ and ‘75 ΧΡΟΝΙΑ ΜΝΗΜΗΣ ΣΠΥΡΟΥ ΛΟΥΗ’. Above the cup appears the year of issuance ‘2015’ and to the right a palmette (the mint mark of the Greek Mint). Visible at the bottom of the design is the monogram of the artist (George Stamatopoulos).
76539.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

76636.name: 2 Euros (European Union Flag)
76636.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76636.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
76636.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

86465.name: 2 Euros (Dimitris Mitropoulos)
86465.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
86465.obverse_description:
The design features the portrait of Dimitri Mitropoulos against the background of music notes. Inscribed along the inner edge at left is the wording ‘120 YEARS OF THE BIRTH OF DIMITRI MITROPOULOS’ and ‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’, in Greek. At upper left apears the year of issuance ‘2016’ and a palmette (the mintmark of the Greek Mint). Visible at lower right is the monogram of the artist (George Stamatopoulos).
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
86465.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

86464.name: 2 Euros (Arkadi Monastery)
86464.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
86464.obverse_description: The Arkadi Monastery with its name below and the country name to the left along with the date to the upper right and a palmette, the mintmark of the Greek Mint, below the centre inscription. The monogram of the artist is at the lower right and the 12 stars of the European flag in the outer ring
86464.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

117572.name: 2 Euros (Nikos Kazantzakis)
117572.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
117572.obverse_description: The design features a profile portrait of Nikos Kazantzakis, one of Greece’s greatest 20th-century writers. Inscribed along the inner circle, at the left, is the wording ‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’ and the name ‘NIKOS KAZANTZAKIS’ (in Greek). At the top is the year of issuance ‘2017’ and at the center-left a palmette (the mint mark of the Greek Mint). Also visible at lower right is the monogram of the artist (George Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
117572.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

118557.name: 2 Euros (Archeological Site of Philippi)
118557.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
118557.obverse_description: The design features part of Basilica B and linear motifs inspired by a border pattern from an ancient Greek mosaic discovered at the site. Inscribed along the inner circle is the wording ‘ARCHAEOLOGICAL SITE OF PHILIPPI’ and ‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’ (in Greek). Also inscribed in the background is the year of issuance ‘2017’ and to the right a palmette (the mint mark of the Greek Mint). Visible at the lower left is the monogram of the artist (George Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
118557.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

141389.name: 2 Euros (Union of the Dodecanese with Greece)
141389.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
141389.obverse_description: A rose, the canting badge of Rhodes, inspired from a coin minted by the ancient city of Rhodes, one of the most characteristic coins of the Dodecanese, with stylised waves fanning out from the centre. Inscribed along the inner edge is the wording ‘1948-2018 THE UNION OF THE DODECANESE WITH GREECE’ and the country name. Also visible at left is a palmette (the mintmark of the Greek mint) and, at right, the monogram of the artist (George Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
141389.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

141403.name: 2 Euros (Kostis Palamas)
141403.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
141403.obverse_description: The design features a portrait of the Greek poet Kostis Palamas (1859-1943).
141403.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

169092.name: 2 Euros (Manolis Andronicos)
169092.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
169092.obverse_description:
Manolis Andronicos (1919-1992) was one of Greece’s greatest archaeologists. His discovery of the royal tombs at Vergina in 1977 brought to light exquisite finds that attest to the splendour of ancient Macedonian civilisation.
The design features a portrait of Manolis Andronicos. Inscribed along the inner edge at left is the name ‘MANOLIS ANDRONICOS 1919-1992’, along with the minting year and a palmette (mintmark of the Greek Mint); inscribed along the inner edge at right is the wording ‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’. Also visible at the right is the monogram of the artist (George Stamatopoulos).
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
169092.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

169094.name: 2 Euros (Andreas Kalvos)
169094.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
169094.obverse_description:
Born in Zante, Andreas Kalvos (1792-1869) is one the most important modern Greek poets. Combining a solid neo-classicist education with the high ideals of Romanticism and archaicising with demotic Greek, he expressed both the revolutionary ideas of his time and his personal vision.
The national side of the coin features a portrait of Andreas Kalvos. Inscribed along the inner edge at the left is the name ‘ANDREAS KALVOS 1792-1869’, along with the minting year and a palmette (mintmark of the Greek Mint); inscribed along the inner edge at the right is the wording ‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’. Also visible at the right is the monogram of the artist (George Stamatopoulos).
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
169094.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

198590.name: 2 Euros (Battle of Thermopylae)
198590.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass centre in copper-nickel ring
198590.obverse_description: Helmet of ancient Hellenic warrior, encircled by meander motif and lettering. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
198590.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

214727.name: 2 Euros (Union of Thrace with Greece)
214727.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass centre in copper-nickel ring
214727.obverse_description: An ancient Thracian drachma coin from Avdira, with a griffin, surrounded by the inscription in Greek (100th Anniversary of the Union of Thrace), a palmette (the mint mark of the Greek Mint) on the bottom left, the date on the bottom right, the state name below. The twelve stars of the European Union surround the design on the outer ring.
214727.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

261607.name: 2 Euros (1821 Greek Revolution)
261607.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
261607.obverse_description:
The design features the Greek flag at the centre, encircled by laurel branches. Inscribed along the inner edge are the wordings ‘1821 – 2021 200 YEARS SINCE THE GREEK REVOLUTION’ and ‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’. Visible, at the bottom, between two laurel branches, are a palmette (the minmark of the Greek Mint) and the signature of the artist (George Stamatopoulos).
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
261607.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

332105.name: 2 Euros (Greek Constitution)
332105.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass centre in copper-nickel ring
332105.obverse_description:
The design features the temple of Asclepius at Epidaurus with the statue of the god at centre. The theme replicates the reverse of a commemorative medal on the First National Assembly held by the revolted Greeks at Epidaurus, which was awarded to the Assembly members during the reign of king Otho. Inscribed along the inner edge is the wording ‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’ and ‘THE FIRST GREEK CONSTITUTION’, as well as the years ‘1822’ and ‘2022’, a palmette (the mintmark of the Greek mint) and the monogram of the artist (George Stamatopoulos).

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
332105.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

331432.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
331432.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
331432.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
331432.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

365351.name: 2 Euros (Maria Callas)
365351.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
365351.obverse_description:
The design features a portrait of the legendary Greek soprano Maria Callas. Inscribed along the inner edge is the wording ‘100 YEARS FROM THE BIRTH OF MARIA CALLAS’, and at the right the minting year ‘2023’ and a palmette (mintmark of the Greek Mint); below the portrait runs the inscription ‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
365351.reverse_description: Map of Western Europe on outer ring and inner core at right side, value superimposed. Above and below map, six stars with vertical bars.

365354.name: 2 Euros (Constantin Carathéodory)
365354.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
365354.obverse_description:
The design features a portrait of the Greek mathematician Constantin Carathéodory. Inscribed along the inner edge at the left is the wording ‘CONSTANTIN CARATHÉODORY 1873-1950’, and at right the minting year ‘2023’ and a palmette (mintmark of the Greek Mint); below the portrait runs the inscription ‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
365354.reverse_description: Map of Western Europe on outer ring and inner core at right side, value superimposed. Above and below map, six stars with vertical bars.

421060.name: 2 Euros (Democracy in Greece)
421060.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
421060.obverse_description:
The design features the Hellenic Parliament building, surrounded by the wording ‘50 ΧΡΟΝΙΑ ΑΠΟ ΤΗ ΜΕΤΑΠΟΛΙΤΕΥΣΗ’ (‘50 YEARS FROM THE RESTORATION OF DEMOCRACY IN GREECE’) and ‘ΕΛΛΗΝΙΚΗ ΔΗΜΟΚΡΑΤΙΑ’ (‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’). Below is inscribed a palmette (mintmark of the Greek Mint). The fall of the colonels’ junta on 24 July 1974 marked the restoration of democracy in Greece following a seven-year dictatorship.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
421060.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

421064.name: 2 Euros (Penelope Delta)
421064.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
421064.obverse_description:
The design features a portrait of Penelope Delta. The theme is bordered by the wording ‘ΠΗΝΕΛΟΠΗ ΔΕΛΤΑ 1874-1941’ (‘PENELOPE DELTA 1874-1941’) and ‘ΕΛΛΗΝΙΚΗ ΔΗΜΟΚΡΑΤΙΑ’ (‘HELLENIC REPUBLIC’). To the left is a palmette (mintmark of the Greek Mint) and to the right the minting year. Penelope Delta was a pioneer of children’s literature in Greece. Generations of Greek children have been raised with her books, which remain popular.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
421064.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

481973.name: 2 Euros (Mikis Theodorakis)
481973.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
481973.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

481971.name: 2 Euros (Laskarina Bouboulina)
481971.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
481971.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

128.name: 2 Euros (1st map)
128.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
128.obverse_description: The Celtic harp, a traditional symbol of Ireland, with the country name to the left and the date to the right, and the 12 stars of Europe in the outer ring
128.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the fifteen states of the European Union

2211.name: 2 Euros (Treaty of Rome)
2211.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2211.obverse_description: The coin shows the Treaty document signed by the six founding countries on a background evoking the paving (designed by Michelangelo) of the Piazza del Campidoglio in Rome, where the signing took place on 25 March 1957. ‘Treaty of Rome 50 years’, ‘EUROPE’ and the name of the issuing country appear in the respective languages of the euro area or in Latin. Thus, the legend differs from country to country, but the image is the same. The Treaty of Rome established the European Economic Community and ultimately led to the introduction of the euro in 1999 and the euro banknotes and coins in 2002. The anniversary was celebrated on 25 March 2007. The euro area countries have marked the occasion by jointly issuing this commemorative coin. Its design was selected following a competition organised by the European mints. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
2211.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

6334.name: 2 Euros (2nd map)
6334.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
6334.obverse_description: An Irish harp in the center with the country name in Irish to the left, the date to the right, and the 12 stars of Europe in the outer ring
6334.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders

5069.name: 2 Euros (10 Years of EMU)
5069.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5069.obverse_description: The coin shows a stick figure which merges into the € symbol. It seeks to convey the idea of the single currency and, by extension, the Economic and Monetary Union (EMU) being the latest step in Europe’s long history of trade and economic integration. The coin is issued by each euro area country and shows the name of the country as well as the legend ‘EMU 1999-2009’ in the respective languag (s). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
5069.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28194.name: 2 Euros (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28194.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28194.obverse_description: The world in the form of a euro symbol in the center, showing how the euro has become a global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolize the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28194.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

77182.name: 2 Euros (30 Years of European Union Flag)
77182.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
77182.obverse_description: The design shows the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
77182.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

80921.name: 2 Euros (Easter Rising)
80921.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
80921.obverse_description: The design shows the statue of Hibernia perched at the top of the General Post Office building – the seizure of which marked the beginning of the Easter rising back in 1916. She is a symbol of Ireland. Her name was taken from the ancient Greek reference for Ireland and her form was realised by the sculptor John Smyth. She is viewed as a witness to the events of 1916 and, in the 100 years since, she has seen the nation blossom before her based on the ideals set out in the Proclamation. She continues to look forward and is a symbol of the past, present and future of the country. At the top of the inner circle, the inscription ‘HIBERNIA’ appears in hand‑rendered lettering that takes influence from the historic Book of Kells. The sunrays reflect well the underlying concept of the Rising and the Proclamation, e.g. the dawning of the new nation/republic. The image of the statue is flanked by the years ‘1916’ and ‘2016’. At the bottom of the inner circle appears the name of the issuing country ‘éire’ and, underneath it, the year of issuance ‘2016’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
80921.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

151940.name: 2 Euros (First meeting of Dáil Éireann)
151940.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
151940.obverse_description:
The design depicts the first sitting of Dáil Éireann in the Round Room. The Governance of
the Irish nation began on the 21st of January and the sitting was conducted entirely in the Irish language to mark the
symbolic nature of the meeting. The large Round Room of the Mansion House arcs above the vast congregation of the
nation’s first elected Dáil. The words ‘An Chéad Dáil’, at the centre of the design, together with the year ‘1919’ at the
top, are inscribed in a traditional uncial font. At the bottom are inscribed the name of the country and the year of
issuance ‘ÉIRE 2019’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
151940.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

319135.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
319135.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
319135.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
319135.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

368762.name: 2 Euros (EU Membership)
368762.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% N)
368762.obverse_description:
The design depicts a modification to the ‘EU50’ programme logo used by Government Departments to promote events marking the 50th anniversary of Ireland’s EU membership. The name of the issuing country ‘ÉIRE’ is inserted above the logo. The year ‘1973’ is referenced to recognise the year Ireland joined the EU as well as ‘2023’ to mark the coin issue year.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
368762.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

136.name: 2 Euros (1st map)
136.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
136.obverse_description: A portrait drawn by Raffaello of Dante Alighieri, housed in the Pope Julius II Wing of the Vatican City Palace, is encircled by the twelve stars of Europe. Date, monogram of the Republic and mintmark are at left of the portrait. Author's initials are below the neck.
136.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the 15 states of the European Union

2191.name: 2 Euros (World Food Program)
2191.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2191.obverse_description: A globe tilted to the right bearing the inscription 'WORLD FOOD PROGRAMME' with an ear of wheat, an ear of maize and an ear of rice coming out from its border. The three grains representing the world's basic sources of nourishment. The monogram of the Italian Republic (and R superimposed over and I) appears to the right of the globe with a smaller combination of the letters U and P below, as initials of the engraver. To the upper left of the globe are the mint mark and the date with the twelve five-pointed stars in the outer rim representing the European Union.
2191.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2195.name: 2 Euros (European Constitution)
2195.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2195.obverse_description: The center of the coin features Europa and the bull, with Europa holding a pen signing the text of the European Constitution and the mintmark to the upper-left with the initials of the engraver to the lower-left. The date is in the top-right above the head of the bull and the monogram of the Italian Republic slightly off-center at the bottom with the words "COSTITUZIONE EUROPEA" forming a semicircle underneath the central image, with the twelve five-pointed stars around the outer ring representing the European Union.
2195.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2199.name: 2 Euros (2006 Winter Olympics - Turin)
2199.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2199.obverse_description: To the right the image of a skier during a downhill competition while to the left is a representation of one of the most famous Turin’s landmark: the Mole Antonelliana building. The location is marked also by the name of the city below the Mole. The skier and the image of the city are separated by a dynamic, curvilinear figure and more are in the background below the skier. These are the trails left by the skies. The monogram of the Italian Republic ‘RI’ and the mintmark ‘R’ are also to the skier’s left. The date, initials of the designer are to the right and the 12 stars of the European Union are around the rim, on the ring.
2199.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2210.name: 2 Euros (Treaty of Rome)
2210.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2210.obverse_description: The coin shows the Treaty of Rome being signed by the six founding countries of the European Union a background evoking the paving (designed by Michelangelo) of the Piazza del Campidoglio in Rome, where the signing took place on March 25, 1957. The words ‘Treaty of Rome 50 years’, ‘EUROPE’, and 'ITALIAN REPUBLIC appear in Italian. The coin’s outer ring bears the twelve five-pointed stars of the European Union.
2210.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

3731.name: 2 Euros (Human Rights)
3731.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
3731.obverse_description: The coin depicts a man and a woman with an olive branch, an ear of corn, a cogwheel and some barbed wire – symbols of the right to peace, food, work and freedom respectively. The monogram of the Italian Republic ‘RI’ is placed between the two figures, as is the year of issue ‘2008’. At the bottom of the image are the links of a chain forming the number ‘60’ and the inscription ‘DIRITTI UMANI’ (human rights). The initials ‘MCC’ of the artist Maria Carmela Colaneri and the mint mark ‘R’ are shown on the right. The outer ring of the coin depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
3731.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

6384.name: 2 Euros (2nd map)
6384.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
6384.obverse_description: A portrait drawn by Raphael of Dante Alighieri, housed in the Pope Julius II Wing of the Vatican City Palace, is encircled by the twelve stars of Europe.
6384.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the 15 states of the European Union.

8108.name: 2 Euros (Louis Braille)
8108.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
8108.obverse_description:
An hand upon an open book as is the act of reading. Above the monogram of the Italian Republic and two seagulls in flight, symbols of free and universal knowledge. Below the book the name Louis Braille written in his relief writing system and the initials of the author, with mintmark at right.
In the outer ring the twelve stars of European Union.
8108.reverse_description: Left the number 2 indicating the value. At right six straight lines run vertically between the lower and upper right part of the coin. Twelve stars are superimposed on the lines, one just before the ends of each line. Below these lines there is a representation of the European continent. Above all is superimposed horizontally the word 'EURO'. Under the letter O, right, near the edge of the coin appears the monogram of the engraver (LL).

5070.name: 2 Euros (Economic and Monetary Union)
5070.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5070.obverse_description: Stick figure which merges into the € symbol. Twelve stars of the European Union on outer ring.
5070.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

14445.name: 2 Euros (Count of Cavour)
14445.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
14445.obverse_description: The coin commemorates the 200th anniversary of the birth of Camillo Benso, Count of Cavour (1810-1861), a key figure in Italian unification and early Italian liberalism, and the founder of the political newspaper "Il Risorgimento". It features a portrait of Cavour based on a painting by Francesco Hayez from 1864. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
14445.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

20892.name: 2 Euros (Unification of Italy)
20892.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
20892.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows, in the centre, three Italian flags fluttering in the wind, to mark three past periods of 50 years each – the periods ending in 1911, 1961 and 2011 respectively – in illustration of a perfect link between generations: this is the logo of the 150th anniversary of the unification of Italy. Written in a semi-circle along the upper outer edge around the three flags is the inscription “150° DELL’ UNITÁ D’ITALIA” (150th anniversary of the unification of Italy), with the letters “RI” superimposed on one another off-centre to the right in indication of the issuing country, the Republic of Italy. Beneath the three flags stand the dates “1861 › 2011 › ›”. Centred below the dates is the mintmark “R”, with the letters “ELF INC.”, the initials of the artist Ettore Lorenzo Frapiccini and the first three letters of the Italian name of his profession (incisore) along the bottom edge to the right. The 12 stars of the European Union are depicted on the coin’s outer ring.
20892.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28324.name: 2 Euros (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28324.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28324.obverse_description: Coin features the world in the form of a euro symbol in the centre, showing how the euro has become a true global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolise the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28324.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

38359.name: 2 Euros (Giovanni Pascoli)
38359.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
38359.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin features a portrait of Giovanni Pascoli, a poet from the Romagna region who is a prominent representative of late 19th century Italian literature. The portrait is flanked by the poet’s year of death “1912”, the mintmark “R” and the letters “M.C.C.”, the initials of the designer Maria Carmela Colaneri, on the left and by the year of issue “2012”’ immediately above the monogram of the Italian Republic “RI” on the right. The inscription “G. PASCOLI” forms an arch along the bottom edge of the coin’s inner part below the portrait. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
38359.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

45254.name: 2 Euros (Giuseppe Verdi)
45254.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
45254.obverse_description: The design shows the bust of Giuseppe Verdi in three quarter view facing left; on the left the monogram of the Italian Republic while on the right, R (monogram of the Mint of Rome); both are above the dates of the anniversary. In exergue initials of the Author Maria Carmela Colaneri and "G. VERDI". The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
45254.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

50746.name: 2 Euros (Giovanni Boccaccio)
50746.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
50746.obverse_description: The design shows the head of Giovanni BOCCACCIO in three quarter view facing right, drawn from the fresco by Andrea del Castagno, around 1450 ca. (Florence, Galleria degli Uffizi); around, on the bottom, BOCCACCIO 1313 2013; on the right, superimposed letters R (monogram of the Mint of Rome)/RI (monogram of Italian Republic)/m (monogram of the author Roberto Mauri). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
50746.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

61828.name: 2 Euros (Carabinieri)
61828.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
61828.obverse_description: The design shows a reinterpretation of the sculpture ‘Pattuglia di Carabinieri nella tormenta’ made in 1973 by Antonio Berti; on the right, superimposed letters of the Italian Republic monogram "RI" and year of issue 2014 under it; on the left, 1814; up, letter "R" (monogram of the Mint of Rome); at the bottom of sculpture monogram of the Author Luciana De Simoni "LDS", under it in semicircle legend "CARABINIERI." The coin’s outer ring bears 12 stars of the European Union.
61828.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

64092.name: 2 Euros (Galileo Galilei)
64092.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
64092.obverse_description: The design shows the head of Galileo Galilei from the painting of Justus Sustermans, 1636 (Florence, Galleria degli Uffizi); around, upside, GALILEO GALILEI; on the right, superimposed letters R (monogram of the Mint of Rome)/astronomic telescope/C.M. (monogram of the Author Claudia Momoni); on the left, superimposed letters of the Italian Republic monogram ‘RI’; in exergue 1564-2014. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
64092.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

72035.name: 2 Euros (Expo Milano 2015)
72035.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
72035.obverse_description: In the field a composition symbolizing the fertility of Earth: on a semicircle standing for the terrestrial globe, a seed fed by water and about to germinate; above the Earth, a vine shoot, an olive branch and a sheaf of wheat growing out of a tree trunk; above, archshaped inscription “NUTRIRE IL PIANETA”; on the left, initials of the designer Maria Grazia Urbani “MGU”; on the right, acronym of the Italian Republic “RI” and the letter “R” identifying the Mint of Rome; in the middle, logo of the EXPO MILANO 2015; all around, the twelve stars of the European Union.
72035.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

74627.name: 2 Euros (Dante Alighieri)
74627.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
74627.obverse_description: Dante with an open book in left hand and the Purgatory mountain behind him: a particular from illustrations of "Divina Commedia" by Domenico di Michelino (1417-1491), realized inside "Duomo di Santa Maria del Fiore", Florence; at the center, monogram of Italian Republic “RI”; on the right side, letter “R”, Rome mintage; in basso, “SP”, engraver's initials and dates “1265 2015”, rispectively birth of Dante and issuing year.
74627.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

76638.name: 2 Euros (30 Years of European Union Flag)
76638.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76638.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
76638.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

84714.name: 2 Euros (Plautus)
84714.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
84714.obverse_description: Theatre masks representing two New Comedy characters, a young woman and a slave, from a mosaic of the second century AD (Capitoline Museums, Rome); around, the construction plan of a Roman theatre and ‘RI’, the monogram of the Italian Republic; on the left, ‘R’, and on the right, ‘LDS’, the initials of the author Luciana De Simoni; below, ‘184 A. C.’, ‘2016’ and ‘PLAUTO’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
84714.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

82330.name: 2 Euros (Donatello)
82330.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
82330.obverse_description:
Head of the "David di Donatello", the bronze sculpture from the Museum of the Bargello in Florence. On the left side can be seen the monogram of the Italian Republic "RI" and below two lines of the dates of the anniversary. Above the head the mintmark and at right the letters "CM", initials of the designer Claudia Momoni.
