The outer ring of the coin depicts the twelve stars of the European Union flag.
82330.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

107086.name: 2 Euros (St. Mark's Basilica)
107086.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
107086.obverse_description: Façade of St. Mark ’s Basilica in Venice; in exergue, “1617” and “2017”, the year of completion of the Basilica and that of the coin’s issue respectively, flank the monogram of the Italian Republic “RI”; below, the inscription “SAN MARCO”; on the right “LDS”, initials of the designer Luciana De Simoni; above, “VENEZIA” and the letter “R” identifying the Mint of Rome; around, the twelve stars of the European Union.
107086.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

107085.name: 2 Euros (Titus Livius)
107085.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
107085.obverse_description: Bust of the Roman historian Titus Livius, from a work by Lorenzo Larese Moretti; on the left, “RI”, monogram of the Italian Republic and “C.M.”, initials of the designer Claudia Momoni; on the right, “17” and “2017”, the year of Titus Livius’ death and that of the coin’s issue respectively, and the letter “R”, identifying the Mint of Rome; around, a dot-decorated frame and the inscription “TITO • LIVIO”; around, the twelve stars of the European Union.
107085.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

132892.name: 2 Euros (Italian Republic Constitution)
132892.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
132892.obverse_description: Enrico De Nicola, the temporary head of state and the person who signed the act of promulgation of the Constitution of the Italian Republic on December 27, 1947, and Alcide De Gasperi, the president of the Council, to his right and Umberto Terracini, the president of the Italian Constituent Assembly, to this left. Above the inscription "COSTITUZIONE" and the monogram of the Italian Republic "RI"; below the words "CON SICURA COSCIENZA", the mint mark "R" and the dates "1948 • 2018", respectively the year of the entry into force of the Italian Constitution and the year of issue of the coin; on the left the initials "UP" of the author Uliana Pernazza. On the outer edge, the twelve stars of the European Union.
132892.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

133074.name: 2 Euros (Ministry of Health)
133074.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
133074.obverse_description: An allegorical representation of Health with some symbolic elements of the activities of the Ministry: environment, research, food and medicine. In the left field, the monogram «RI», the acronym of Italian Republic; above «R», identifier of the Mint of Rome; at the base of the female figure, initials of the author Silvia Petrassi; on the top, curved, the words «MINISTRY OF HEALTH», on the left, curved, the dates «1958 - 2018». The 12 stars of the European Union are depicted on the coin’s outer ring.
133074.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

155940.name: 2 Euros (Leonardo da Vinci)
155940.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
155940.obverse_description:
The design shows a detail of the painting ‘Dama con l'ermellino’ (Lady with an Ermine) by
Leonardo da Vinci (Czartoryski Museum in Krakow). On the left, the inscription ‘Leonardo’, the initials of the author
Maria Angela Cassol ‘MAC’ and the logo ‘RI’ acronym of the Italian Republic; on the right, ‘R’, mintmark of the Mint of
Rome and the dates ‘1519-2019’, respectively the year of Leonardo’s death and the year of the coin issuance.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
155940.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

193757.name: 2 Euros (80 years of National Firefighters Corps)
193757.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
193757.obverse_description: The logo of the Italian National Fire Corps in the centre, the monogram of the Italian Republic to the left, the date to the right along with the mintmark. The initials of the designer are below along with an inscription around the centre ring and the 12 stars of Europe in the outer ring
193757.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

200469.name: 2 Euros (Maria Montessori)
200469.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
200469.obverse_description:
A portrait of Maria Montessori in a geometric composition with some didactic elements drawn from her educational system. In the right field, ‘RI’, an acronym of the Italian Republic; on the left, ‘R’, identifying the Mint of Rome; on the right, ‘LDS’, initials of the designer Luciana De Simoni; at the top and bottom, the dates ‘1870’ and ‘2020’, the year of birth of the Italian pedagogue and that of coin’s issue, respectively; around the portrait, the inscription
‘MARIA MONTESSORI’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
200469.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

268894.name: 2 Euros (Health Professions)
268894.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
268894.obverse_description: The design depicts in the centre, a man and a woman in medical clothing with masks, a stethoscope and a medical folder representing doctors and nurses working on the front line in the fight against COVID-19. Above, the word "GRAZIE" followed, on the right, by a heart symbol; on the left, a reproduction of the medical cross; in the centre, "RI", acronym of the Italian Republic; on the right, "R", identifying the Mint of Rome; below, on the left, "CM", initials of the designer Claudia Momoni; in exergue, the year of the coin’s issue "2021".
268894.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

268102.name: 2 Euros (Rome)
268102.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
268102.obverse_description: Detail of Dea Roma, sculpture by Angelo Zanelli inserted in the center of the Altare della Patria, a large votive altar dedicated to the Italian nation set in the Vittoriano complex in Rome. Above, the legend “ROMA CAPITALE”; on the right, the dates “•1871•2021•”, the year of the proclamation of Rome as the Capital of Italy and that of the coin’s issue respectively; on the left, “RI”, acronym of the Italian Republic, and “R”, identifying the Mint of Rome; in exergue, “UP”, initials of the designer Uliana Pernazza; around, the twelve stars of the European Union.
268102.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.
12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

323896.name: 2 Euros (Italian National Police)
323896.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
323896.obverse_description:
Two officers of the “Polizia di Stato” (Italian National Police Force), a man and a woman, stand out in the foreground against a police car. 
Above, the arch-shaped inscription “POLIZIA DI STATO”; on the right “RI”, acronym of the Italian Republic; below, on the left, in the centre and on the right, respectively, “R”, identifying the Mint of Rome, “AM”, initials of the designer Annalisa Masini and the dates “1852 2022”, the year of the foundation of the “Polizia di Stato” and that of the coin’s issue; around, the twelve stars of the European Union.
323896.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

324287.name: 2 Euros (Falcone - Borsellino)
324287.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
324287.obverse_description:
Portraits of the two Italian judges Giovanni Falcone and Paolo Borsellino inspired by a picture of Tony Gentile.

Above, the arch-shaped inscription “FALCONE - BORSELLINO”; below, the dates “1992 2022”, the year of the deaths of both anti-Mafia judges and that of the coin’s issue, respectively, with the acronym of the Italian Republic “RI” in between; on the right, "R", identifying the Mint of Rome; on the left, “VdS”, initials of the designer Valerio de Seta; around, the twelve stars of the European Union.
324287.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

331445.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
331445.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
331445.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
331445.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

353313.name: 2 Euros (Italian Air Force)
353313.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
353313.obverse_description: Depicts the logo designed by the Italian Air Force staff for the centennial celebrations. The design depicts a large stylized numeral “100” crossed by a runway with an antique aircraft, a pioneer propeller biplane, at the lower left and a modern fighter aircraft, presumably an F35, at the upper right. A semicircular line on the right edge of the image links the two elements with the dates 1923 and 2023.
353313.reverse_description:
A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean.

12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

353310.name: 2 Euros (Alessandro Manzoni)
353310.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
353310.obverse_description: Depicts a portrait of Alessandro Manzoni, inspired by the image of the Italian writer on the one hundred thousand lire banknote issued in 1967. The acronym of the Italian Republic (IR) on the left, the years of the writer's death and the issue of the coin below. Mintmark (R) on the right, the engraver's initials (AV) below. The outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
353310.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

401386.name: 2 Euros (Financial Police)
401386.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
401386.obverse_description:
The design depicts, in the centre, a stylized version of the heraldic symbol of Guardia di Finanza Corps: it portrays the concept of security and provides a future vision of the Corps’ role celebrating the past, by combining it with the years to come and challenges they will continue to face. This emblem includes different elements: mountain, sea and the sky, which are the natural environments where Corps operate; the Griffon, a mythological animal which, according to the legend, supervises protection of the Treasury, represented by the chest of the State, and the turreted crown. At the top, in semi-circle, is the inscription ‘GUARDIA DI FINANZA’. Below, ‘RI’, acronym of the Italian Republic and the dates ‘1774-2024’, year of the establishment of the Guardia di Finanza Corps and year of coin’s issue respectively; on the right, ‘R’, identifying the Mint of Rome and ‘MB’, the initials of designer Marta Bonifacio.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
401386.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

410233.name: 2 Euros (Rita Levi-Montalcini)
410233.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
410233.obverse_description: In the foreground, portrait of Rita Levi-Montalcini inspired by a photograph by Manuela Fabbri; in the background, microscope taken from a medal designed by the brother of the Italian scientist Gino Levi-Montalcini, with the base in the shape of a horseshoe as a good luck charm for the award of the Nobel Prize for Medicine, that she received in 1986. Above, arch-shaped, the inscription “RITA LEVI-MONTALCINI”; on the left, “RI”, acronym for the Italian republic, and “R”, identifying the Mint of Rome; in exergue, “2024”, year of the coin’s issue; on the right, “SP”, initials of the designer Silvia Petrassi; around, the twelve stars of the European Union.
410233.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

465028.name: 2 Euros (Voyage of the Amerigo Vespucci Ship)
465028.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
465028.obverse_description: In the centre, the depiction of the Amerigo Vespucci Training Ship which, with sails unfurled, surfs the waves of the sea. Arch-shaped, the inscription “Amerigo Vespucci World Tour 2023-2025”; At the bottom left, “2025”, the year of the coin’s issue and “RI”, acronym of the Italian Republic; on the right, “R”, identifying the Mint of Rome; in exergue “E. FERRETTI”, signature of the designer; around, the twelve stars of the European Union.
465028.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

453332.name: 2 Euros (Jubilee 2025)
453332.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
453332.obverse_description: At the centre, the depiction of the Holy Door located at St. Peter's Basilica. At the top, in an arch, the inscription "IUBILAEUM", while at the bottom, also in an arch to close the Latin inscription, a series of alternating empty and full fish, symbol of Christianity. On the left, "2025", the year the coin was minted and "R", identifying the Mint of Rome; on the right, "RI", acronym of the Italian Republic, and "C.M.", initials of the designer Claudia Momoni. Arch-shaped, the twelve stars of the European Union.
453332.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

565225.name: 2 Euro (Saint Francis of Assisi)
565225.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
565225.obverse_description: At the centre, an image of Saint Francis of Assisi inspired by a 14th-century fresco by Simone Martini located in the Basilica of Saint Francis, Assisi (Umbria, Italy). Around, the inscription "SAN FRANCESCO D'ASSISI 1226-2026", the year of the Saint's death and the year of the coin's issue, respectively. At the bottom right, "RI acronym for the Italian Republic. On the left "R", identifying the Mint of Rome, and at the bottom left "AV", the designer's signature. Arc-shaped, the twelve stars of the European Union.
565225.reverse_description: At right, map of Western Europe with six stars above and beneath connected by vertical stripes. Value at left overlapping it.

564681.name: 2 Euros (Carlo Collodi - Pinocchio)
564681.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
564681.obverse_description: At the centre, two back-to-back depictions of Pinocchio: the live boy striding left and the wooden puppet facing right. Creator's name , birth and death years above. Mint mark in field to left, and issuer's monogram to right. Outer ring with 12 stars.
564681.reverse_description: At right, map of Western Europe with six stars above and beneath connected by vertical stripes. Value at left overlapping it.

63874.name: 2 Euros (Riga – 2014)
63874.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
63874.obverse_description: The central image of the coin shows the skyline of Riga and the historic centre of the city that has been included in the list of the UNESCO World Heritage Sites. At the top of the image, the inscription ‘EIROPAS KULTURAS GALVASPILSETA’ (European capital of culture) and at the bottom the name of the celebrated city and the year of issuance ‘RIGA — 2014’, and underneath the indication of the issuing country ‘LV’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
63874.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

53188.name: 2 Euros
53188.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
53188.obverse_description: The obverse of the coin features the portrait of a Latvian folk-maid in profile, originally depicted on the reverse of the 5 lats silver coin in 1929. It is semi-encircled by the inscriptions LATVIJAS on the left and REPUBLIKA on the right. The coin's outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
53188.reverse_description: The reverse of the coin shows a geographical image of Europe; it also features the numeral 2 and the inscription EURO.

77458.name: 2 Euros (Black Stork)
77458.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
77458.obverse_description: The design shows a black stork (Ciconia nigra) which is considered to be one of the nature conservation flagship species in Europe. In 2005 the Black Stork Protection Plan was adopted in Latvia. At the bottom of the design is the name of the issuing country ‘LATVIJA’ and underneath the year of issuance ‘2015’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
77458.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

76639.name: 2 Euros (30 Years of European Union Flag)
76639.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76639.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
76639.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

69614.name: 2 Euros (Latvian Presidency of the Council of the European Union)
69614.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
69614.obverse_description: An archetypal image of a millstone, the logo of the Latvian Presidency of the Council of the European Union, with the inscriptions LATVIJAS PREZIDENTŪRA ES PADOMĒ (Latvian Presidency of the Council of the European Union) and EU2015.LV above and beneath it respectively.
69614.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

85889.name: 2 Euros (Latvian farming and countryside)
85889.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
85889.obverse_description: The design shows a Latvian brown cow which is one of the symbols of Latvian dairy farming both in folklore and in life. Underneath the design, at the left side there is the inscription of the year of issuance ‘2016’ and at the bottom of the inner circle the name of the issuing country ‘LATVIJA’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
85889.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

95462.name: 2 Euros (Vidzeme)
95462.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
95462.obverse_description: The design shows the coat of arms of the historical region of Latvia — Vidzeme or Livland. At the top, there is the inscription of the issuing country ‘LATVIJA’ and at the bottom the inscription ‘VIDZEME’. At the right is the year of issuance ‘2016’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
95462.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

125266.name: 2 Euros (Kurzeme)
125266.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
125266.obverse_description:
The design shows the coat of arms of the region of Kurzeme. At the top, the name of the issuing country ‘LATVIJA’ and at the bottom the inscription ‘KURZEME’. At the right hand side, the year of issuance ‘2017’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
125266.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

125267.name: 2 Euros (Latgale)
125267.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
125267.obverse_description:
The design shows the coat of arms of the region of Latgale. At the top, the name of the issuing country ‘LATVIJA’ and at the bottom the inscription ‘LATGALE’. At the right hand side, the year of issuance ‘2017’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
125267.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

132943.name: 2 Euros (100th Anniversary of the Baltic States)
132943.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
132943.obverse_description: The coin features the three Baltic sisters (Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania) with braided hair. Above the coats of arms of the three Baltic countries; on the right the year of issue "2018"; on the left the word "LATVIJA". The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
132943.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

143883.name: 2 Euros (Zemgale)
143883.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
143883.obverse_description:
The design shows the coat of arms of the region of Zemgale. At the top, the name of the issuing country ‘LATVIJA’ and at the bottom the inscription ‘ZEMGALE’. At the right hand side, the year of issuance ‘2018’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
143883.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

179193.name: 2 Euros (The Rising Sun)
179193.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
179193.obverse_description:
The motif of a rising sun was very popular at the time of the foundation of the Latvian state. The rising sun symbolised the new country. With the help of artist Ivars Drulle, the sun motif created by artist Ansis Cīrulis and emerging as one of the basic elements of the Coat of Arms of Latvia has reborn into the commemorative coin "The Rising Sun".

The graphic design and plaster model of the national side of the new commemorative coin has been created by artist Ivars Drulle,
179193.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

196830.name: 2 Euros (Latgalian Ceramics)
196830.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
196830.obverse_description:
The coin is dedicated to the Latgalian pottery. The traditional pottery established itself for
substantial periods of time in two districts, Latgale and Kurzeme, but only in Latgale it has survived throughout the times
and passed over to the next generations. The Latgalian pottery is included also in the Latvian Culture Canon.
The design depicts a characteristic piece of Latgalian ceramics – clay candelabrum. It bears an inscription ‘LATGALES
KERAMIKA’ (Latgalian ceramics), the country name ‘LATVIJA’ as well as the year of issuance ‘2020’.
The coins outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
196830.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

267043.name: 2 Euros (De iure recognition)
267043.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
267043.obverse_description: The national side of the commemorative coin bears the inscription "100 LATVIJA DE IURE" as well as the year of issue (2021). At the same time, the inscription "Latvija" also indicates the country of issue.
267043.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

324799.name: 2 Euros (Financial Literacy)
324799.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
324799.obverse_description:
Financial literacy is the important ability to understand and effectively use various financial skills, including personal financial management, budgeting, and investing. Financial literacy is the foundation of our relationship with money, and it is a lifelong journey of learning. The design shows a tree, symbolizing the importance of financial literacy and the knowledge about it. At the bottom is the year of issuance ‘2022’ and underneath the name of the issuing country ‘LATVIJA’.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
324799.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

331196.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
331196.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
331196.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
331196.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. Twelve stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

366879.name: 2 Euros (Sunflower for Ukraine)
366879.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
366879.obverse_description:
The design represents a sunflower, which is one of the national symbols of Ukraine, as Ukraine is one of the biggest sunflower oil producers and exporters worldwide. Since the start of the war in Ukraine, the sunflower has become a global symbol for peace. For the first time sunflowers were used as a peace symbol in the summer of 1996. Then they were planted in a military base near Pervomaysk, approximately 155 miles south of the capital Kyiv. Now sunflowers are used as a symbol of peace and support to Ukraine worldwide. At the top, in semi-circle, is the inscription ‘SLAVA UKRAINAI!’ (Glory to Ukraine) and at the bottom, in semi-circle, is the name of the issuing country ‘LATVIJA’ followed by the year of issuance ‘2023’. The author of the design is the Latvian artist Krišs Salmanis.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
366879.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

441453.name: 2 Euros (Puzuris)
441453.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
441453.obverse_description: The national side of the coin depicts a puzuris that is suspended by a thread. The name of the country (LATVIA) and the year of issue (2024) are also featured on the coin.
441453.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

462615.name: 2 Euros (Sēlija)
462615.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
462615.obverse_description:
The design shows the coat of arms of the region of Sēlija. At the top, the name of the issuing country ‘LATVIJA’ and at the bottom the inscription ‘SĒLIJA’. At the right hand side, the year of issuance ‘2025’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
462615.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

78191.name: 2 Euros (Lithuanian Language)
78191.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
78191.obverse_description: The design shows the word ‘AČIŪ (THANK YOU) - one of the most beautiful words in the Lithuanian language. An original Lithuanian font - created specially on the basis of the Latin font for the occasion of the centenary of the restoration of Lithuanian press - in order to match better the combinations of letters used most in the Lithuanian language, is used in the project. All the letters of the Lithuanian language are depicted on the background of the word ‘AČIŪ’, laid out in a tag cloud. At the bottom is the name of the issuing country ‘LIETUVA’ and underneath the name is the year ‘2015’. At the bottom right, under the word ‘AČIŪ’ is the mint mark of the Lithuanian Mint.
78191.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

76640.name: 2 Euros (30 Years of European Union Flag)
76640.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76640.obverse_description: The European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country, the years ‘1985-2015’, and the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
76640.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

67632.name: 2 Euros
67632.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
67632.obverse_description: The coin features the arms of Lithuania, with the date on the right and the country name below. The coin's outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
67632.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

86935.name: 2 Euros (Baltic Culture)
86935.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
86935.obverse_description: An amber disc, one of the most characteristic symbols of the Baltic culture, decorated with a crosspiece of drilled dots — an ornament symbolising the world’s axis. The country name is at the top flanked by the mint mark and the date. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
86935.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

116893.name: 2 Euros (Vilnius)
116893.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
116893.obverse_description: The old town of Vilnius as the centre of Lithuanian culture represented by the free-standing bell tower of the St. John's Church of Vilnius University, In the background, the Orthodox Church of the Holy Ghost is to the left along with the two towers of the Church of St. Catherin to the right. The country name is to the upper right above the date and mintmark. The twelve stars of the European flag are shown on the outer coin ring.
116893.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

132495.name: 2 Euros (Baltic States)
132495.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
132495.obverse_description: On the national side, the three Baltic States are symbolically represented as a braid. They are united by their history: a common past, present and future. A stylised numeral representing the 100th anniversary and the heraldic signs of all three states are featured as well. The inscription of the country’s name and the year of issue (2018) are depicted in a semi-circle. The mark of the Lithuanian Mint and the mark of the designer are also included. The 12 stars are incorporated in the outer part of the design, set out as they are on the flag of the European Union.
132495.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

140885.name: 2 Euros (Song and Dance Celebration)
140885.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
140885.obverse_description: The national side of the coin features stylised figures of people and birds that are symbolic of the Lithuanian folk dance and song and typical of paper cuttings, a Lithuanian folk art genre. The coin also features the inscription LIETUVA (LITHUANIA), year (2018) and the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union flag.
140885.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

156358.name: 2 Euros (Lithuanian Multipart Songs)
156358.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
156358.obverse_description: The national side of the coin depicts linear motifs, symbolising polyphonic melodies of the unique Lithuanian heritage – folk songs sutartinės. The lines are widening and narrowing again, spinning into a swirl decorated with various miniature geometric and nature figures. The coin features inscriptions LIETUVA (LITHUANIA) and SUTARTINĖS (Lithuanian multipart songs), year (2019) and the mark of the Lithuanian Mint. 12 stars are incorporated in the outer part of the coin, set out as they are on the flag of the European Union.
156358.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

161190.name: 2 Euros (Samogitia)
161190.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
161190.obverse_description: The centre of the national side of the coin features a bear standing on its hind legs, with a chain collar on the neck. It has been depicted in the coat of arms of Samogitia since the 16th century. The bear is situated against a shield topped with a crown, which is held by an armoured soldier (a symbol of courage, sacrifice and patriotism) and a goddess with an anchor (a symbol of hope). Below is a Latin inscription PATRIA UNA (one fatherland).The composition is surrounded by inscriptions LIETUVA (Lithuania) and ŽEMAITIJA (Samogitia), year (2019) and the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union flag.
161190.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

220976.name: 2 Euros (Hill of Crosses)
220976.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
220976.obverse_description: The national side of the coin depicts the Hill of Crosses: fragments of wooden and forged crosses, symbolising Lithuanian cross-crafting and folk culture. Cross-crafting and cross-symbolism in Lithuania are included in the UNESCO Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity. The coin features the inscriptions LIETUVA (LITHUANIA) and KRYŽIŲ KALNAS (THE HILL OF CROSSES), year (2020) and the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint.
220976.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

215429.name: 2 Euros (Aukštaitija)
215429.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
215429.obverse_description:
The design features a coat of arms with an armor-clad knight holding a sword in his right hand. The coat of arms is held by two angels, symbolically exalting and protecting Aukštaitija – one of the most important regions of Lithuania and the cradle of its statehood.
Below is a Latin inscription PATRIAM TUAM MUNDUM EXISTIMA (CONSIDER YOUR HOMELAND TO BE THE WHOLE
WORLD). The design is surrounded by inscriptions: ‘LIETUVA’ (the name of the issuing country), ‘AUKŠTAITIJA’, the year of issuance
‘2020’ and the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
215429.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

282044.name: 2 Euros (Žuvintas Biosphere Reserve)
282044.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
282044.obverse_description:
The design depicts the characteristic surroundings of Žuvintas Biosphere Reserve, which belongs to the World Network of Biosphere Reserves of UNESCO’s Man and the Biosphere Programme: remote islands of a unique bird lake and Lithuania’s largest wetland as well as its distinctive animals.
In the foreground there is a great bittern reaching for a fire-bellied toad floating in the water. In rushes depicted on the right there is an aquatic warbler, above – a flock of common cranes and a mute swan, symbolising the establishment of the Reserve.
The design is surrounded by inscriptions LIETUVA (LITHUANIA), ŽUVINTAS, UNESCO, year (2021) and the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint.
The design is realised by Eglė Ratkutė and Ernestas Žemaitis.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
282044.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

264790.name: 2 Euros (Dzūkija)
264790.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
264790.obverse_description: Depicts a coat of arms of Dzūkija with a motto in Latin "Out of most warring tribe – industrious people" means that knowledge of the world starts from the native land. The composition is surrounded by country and region names, year, and the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union flag.
264790.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean

321225.name: 2 Euros (Basketball in Lithuania)
321225.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
321225.obverse_description: The national side of the coin depicts the contour of the map of Lithuania arranged as a basketball court in the centre, which signifies that basketball has been played in Lithuania already for 100 years. The coin also features the inscriptions LIETUVA (Lithuania), 1922-2022 and the logo of the Lithuanian mint, its manufacturer, arranged in a semi-circle around the centre. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union flag.
321225.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

323472.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
323472.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
323472.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
323472.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. Twelve stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

326489.name: 2 Euros (Suvalkija)
326489.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass centre in copper-nickel ring
326489.obverse_description: The national side of the coin depicts a Taurus on the escutcheon, while the escutcheon is decorated on both sides with silver oak branches with acorns joined at the bottom with a silver ribbon with an inscription VIENYBĖ TEŽYDI (UNITY MAY BLOSSOM). Oak branches symbolize the rich history of the region that goes back to the pagan times of the Lithuanian state. In the past, Taurus was the most widespread animal in this region. The composition is surrounded by an inscription LIETUVA (LITHUANIA) and the year 2022 on the top and an inscription SUVALKIJA and the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint at the bottom. 12 stars are incorporated on the outer part of the coin, set out as they are on the flag of the European Union.
326489.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean

356346.name: 2 Euros (Together with Ukraine)
356346.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
356346.obverse_description: In the center of the national side (obverse) of the coin is a stylized sunflower, the petals of which are like silhouettes of people holding hands, hugging, protecting each other, embodying unity, support, and the strength of being together. The sunflower is composed by artistic means as if the rising sun is the hope of a new beginning, the birds are freedom, hope and courage, it is like people rising above the circumstances, never putting up with the imposed will, it is an allusion to people who sacrificed their lives, flying away from their souls. The composition also includes the inscriptions "Lithuania", "Together with Ukraine", year of issue 2023, the logo of the producer UAB "Lietuvos monetų kalyklos", 12 EU stars arranged in a circle.
356346.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean

431883.name: 2 Euros (Straw Garden Traditions)
431883.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
431883.obverse_description: A stylised straw garden consisting of geometric shapes of varying sizes, one tip of which is pointed upwards (to the sky) and the other downwards (to the underground). The national side also bears the inscription “LIETUVA” (LITHUANIA), the year of issue (2024), the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint and the twelve stars of the EU.
431883.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean

461086.name: 2 Euro (Defence)
461086.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
461086.obverse_description: The national side (the obverse) of the commemorative coin dedicated to defence features the contours of Lithuania. The state borders are surrounded by a stylised spine barrier, symbolising the concept of holistic and whole-of-society defence. The image of the hedgehog also represents the openness and fragility of free societies, which must be defended, to the utmost, against external threats. Lithuania is presented as a hedgehog that remains at the ready to defend itself always. The centre of the obverse bears the inscription “LIETUVA” (LITHUANIA), the year of issue (2025), and the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the EU flag.
461086.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

534953.name: 2 Euros (Lithuania Minor)
534953.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
534953.obverse_description: The national side of the coin depicts the shield of the coat of arms, with a moose holding a spear in its forelegs and a shining lighthouse above it. Kurenkahns are on both sides of the shield, and below it appears a quote by Vydūnas: “Spindulys esmi begalinės šviesos” (A ray is the essence of infinite light). The composition is surrounded by the inscriptions “Lietuva” (Lithuania), “Mažoji Lietuva” (Lithuania Minor), the year of issue (2025), and the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the EU flag.
534953.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

564733.name: 2 Euros (Energy independence)
564733.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
564733.obverse_description: The centre of the national side of the coin (obverse) bears a symbol of energy – the bird of freedom, soaring dynamically and vigorously upward. The lightning motifs symbolise energy and purpose. At the top of the coin, the bird’s head morphs into a stylised version of the Columns of the Gediminas, while the wings illustrate the harmony between internal energy sources and new external connections. “Lietuva” (Lithuania) is inscribed on the left of the composition, and the right bears the year of issue (2026) and the mintmark of the Lithuanian Mint. The coin’s outer ring displays the 12 stars of the EU flag.
564733.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

144.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (1st map)
144.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
144.obverse_description: On the right side, a geometrical portrait of His Royal Highness Grand Duke Henri is accompanied, on the left side, with the lettering "LËTZEBUERG" (Luxembourg)
144.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the fifteen states of the European Union

2192.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Monogramme)
2192.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2192.obverse_description: The effigy of His Royal Highness, Grand-Duke Henri on the left side of the coin facing right with the monogram of Grand-Duke Henri (special letter 'H' topped with a crown) to the right. The 12 stars around the inner ring in a semi-circular form appear at the right of the monogram. The year 2004, surrounded by the mint mark as well as the engraver's initials, and the word LËTZEBUERG are written in circular form at the top of the ring. The words 'HENRI — Grand-Duc de Luxembourg' appear at the bottom of the ring.
2192.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2196.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Henri and Adolphe)
2196.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2196.obverse_description: The effigy of Grand Duke Henri facing right superimposed on the effigy of Grand Duke Adolphe in the centre with a legend above, and their names and dates below. In the outer ring of the coin, the twelve stars of the European Union surrounding the design are placed between the country name, the date, and the latter centred below the effigies and flanked by the letter ‘S’ on the left and the logo of the mint on the right.
2196.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2197.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Guillaume)
2197.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2197.obverse_description: The effigy of Grand-Duke Henri is superimposed on that of Grand-Duke Guillaume in the centre of the coin. The year of issue, 2006, appears below the effigies, flanked by the letter ‘S’ and the mintmark. The word ‘LËTZEBUERG’ appears above the effigies. The 12 stars of the European Union are shown in the outer ring of the coin.
2197.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2212.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Treaty of Rome)
2212.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2212.obverse_description:
The coin shows the Treaty document signed by the six founding countries on a background evoking the paving (designed by Michelangelo) of the Piazza del Campidoglio in Rome, where the signing took place on 25 March 1957. ‘Treaty of Rome 50 years’, ‘EUROPE’ and the name of the issuing country appear in the respective languages of the euro area or in Latin. Thus, the legend differs from country to country, but the image is the same. The Treaty of Rome established the European Economic Community and ultimately led to the introduction of the euro in 1999 and the euro banknotes and coins in 2002. The anniversary was celebrated on 25 March 2007. The euro area countries have marked the occasion by jointly issuing this commemorative coin. Its design was selected following a competition organised by the European mints. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.

At the left, a latent image showing the portrait of Grand Duke Henri I.
2212.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2202.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Palais Grand-Ducal)
2202.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2202.obverse_description: The coin features an effigy of Grand-Duke Henri superimposed on an image of the Grand Ducal Palace. The year of issue, 2007, appears to the left, with the engraver's mark above and the mint mark below. The word ‘LËTZEBUERG’ appears at the base of the design. The 12 stars of the European Union are shown on the outer ring of the coin.
2202.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

6420.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (2nd map)
6420.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
6420.obverse_description: On the right side, a geometrical portrait of His Royal Highness Grand Duke Henri is accompanied, on the left side, with the lettering "LËTZEBUERG" and encircled by the twelve stars of Europe
6420.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the states of the European Union

3732.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Château de Berg)
3732.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
3732.obverse_description: The coin shows Grand-Duke Henri and the ‘Château de Berg’, the official residence. The year of issue, 2008, flanked by the mint mark and the mark of the engraving workshop, is inscribed at the top. The word ‘LËTZEBUERG’ appears at the bottom of the design. The outer ring of the coin depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
3732.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

8111.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Accession of Charlotte)
8111.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
8111.obverse_description: The coin features Grand-Duke Henri and Grand-Duchess Charlotte. To the right of the image, placed vertically, are the word ‘LËTZEBUERG’ and the year ‘2009’, flanked by the mintmaster’s mark and the mint mark. The outer ring of the coin shows the 12 stars of the European Union.
8111.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

5071.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Economic and Monetary Union)
5071.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5071.obverse_description:
Stick figure which merges into the € symbol. Twelve stars of the European Union on outer ring.
A large latent image showing either the main design, a stylized man with the Euro sign (€), or the portrait of Grand Duke Henri I.
5071.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

10115.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Duke Henri)
10115.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
10115.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin depicts, on the left, a portrait of Grand Duke Henri facing towards the right and, immediately adjacent, the Grand Duke’s coat of arms, above which the year of issue, 2010, is featured between mint marks, overlapping slightly into the outer ring. Below the portrait, the name of the issuing country, “LËTZEBUERG”, overlaps slightly into the outer ring. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
10115.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

18687.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Jean de Luxemburg)
18687.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
18687.obverse_description: The coin depicts on the right hand of its inner part the effigy of His Royal Highness, the Grand-Duke Henri, looking to the left, and superimposed on the effigies of the Grand-Duke Jean and the Grand-Duchess Charlotte. The text "LËTZEBUERG" is depicted above the three effigies. The year-date "2011", flanked by the mintmark and the Mintmaster mark, appears above. The name of the Royal Highness is depicted below the respective effigy. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
18687.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28323.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Euro Cash)
28323.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28323.obverse_description:
Coin features the world in the form of a euro symbol in the centre, showing how the euro has become a true global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolise the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union. 
Latent image at center showing either the Euro sign (€) or the portrait of Grand Duke Henri I
28323.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

41672.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Royal Wedding)
41672.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
41672.obverse_description: The coin depicts, to the left of its inner part, the effigy of His Royal Highness the Grand Duke Henri, with the effigy of Prince Guillaume, Hereditary Grand Duke, superimposed on that of Countess Stéphanie to the right. The words “PRËNZENHOCHZAÏT” and “LËTZEBUERG” appear above the year “2012” at the bottom of the inner part of the coin, flanked by the mint mark and the mint master’s initials. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
41672.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

29640.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Death of William IV)
29640.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
29640.obverse_description: The coin depicts, to the left of its inner part, the head of His Royal Highness the Grand Duke Henri in front of that of his great-grandfather, the Grand Duke Guillaume IV, both looking to the right, with their names “HENRI” and “GUILLAUME IV † 1912” below. Above are the year “2012”, flanked to the left and right by the mintmark (Mercury’s wand) and the mintmaster mark (a sailing boat), and the words “GRANDS-DUCS DE LUXEMBOURG”. The city of Luxembourg appears in the background. The 12 stars of the European Union are depicted on the coin’s outer ring.
29640.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

50740.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (National Anthem)
50740.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
50740.obverse_description: The effigy of Grand Duke Henri facing left on the right side of the inner part with musical notation and the text of the national anthem to the left. The text ‘Ons Heemecht’ appears at the top of the inner part along with the country name and the date, which is flanked by the mintmark and the initials of the mint master, appearing at the bottom of the inner part of the coin. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
50740.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

53651.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (175 Years of the Nation)
53651.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
53651.obverse_description: The coin depicts on the right hand side of its inner part the effigy of His Royal Highness, the Grand-Duke Henri, looking to the right, and on the left hand side of its inner part, vertically positioned, the years ‘1839’ and ‘2014’ and the name of the issuing country ‘LËTZEBUERG’. The inscriptions ‘ONOFHÄNGEGKEET’ and ‘175 Joër’ appear at the bottom of the inner part of the coin. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
53651.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

65439.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Golden Jubilee)
65439.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
65439.obverse_description: The coin’s inner part depicts the effigies of Their Royal Highnesses, the Grand-Duke Henri and the Grand-Duke Jean, both looking to the left. It is surmounted by the year ‘1964’ above the effigy of the Grand-Duke Jean and by a crown. At the bottom, the names ‘Jean’ and ‘Henri’ appear below the respective effigy as well as the year ‘2014’. The inscription ‘50e ANNIVERSAIRE DE L’ACCESSION AU TRÔNE DU GRAND-DUC JEAN’ encloses in circular form the upper inner part of the coin. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
65439.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

73857.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Accession)
73857.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
73857.obverse_description: The coin depicts their Royal Highnesses Grand Duke Henri and the Grand Duchess. The year of accession ‘2000’, the issuing country ‘Luxembourg’ and the year mark ‘2015’ appear above them. Below them appears the inscription ‘15e anniversaire de l’accession au trône de S.A.R. le Grand-Duc’ (15th anniversary of the accession to the throne of H.R.H. the Grand Duke). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
73857.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

76912.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (House of Nassau-Weilburg)
76912.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76912.obverse_description: On the left of the coin is the effigy of His Royal Highness Grand Duke Henri, and on the right, in a circular arrangement, and in chronological order of their accession to the throne, the portraits of their Royal Highnesses Grand Dukes Adolphe and Guillaume IV, Grand Duchesses Marie-Adélaïde and Charlotte, and Grand Duke Jean. To the right, also in circular format, is the inscription ‘1890 - Dynastie Nassau-Weilbourg’. The name of the issuing country, ‘Luxembourg’, and the year, ‘2015’, run vertically in the centre of the design.
76912.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

78817.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (European Union Flag)
78817.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
78817.obverse_description:
The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.

Latent image at center showing either the main design, the European flag, or the portrait of Grand Duke Henri I.
78817.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

84907.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (50 Years of Grand Duchess Charlotte Bridge)
84907.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
84907.obverse_description: The design shows the ‘Grand Duchess Charlotte Bridge’, which is the main infrastructure connecting the city centre of Luxembourg to the urban quarter of Kirchberg, the location of the city’s European Union institutions. The French name of the bridge, ‘Pont Grande-Duchesse Charlotte’, is inscribed on the image of the bridge, and the year ‘1966’ appears directly beneath this text. At the top of the design, an image of the Grand Duke Henri of Luxembourg; at its left, the year of issuance ‘2016’. At the bottom appears the name of the issuing country, ‘LUXEMBOURG’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
84907.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

100577.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Volunteer Army)

126563.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (William III)
126563.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring

126563.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

132310.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Constitution of Luxembourg)
132310.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
132310.obverse_description: The coin bears the effigy of His Royal Highness Grand Duke Henri on the left, looking to the right. To the right of the design are the dates "1868-2018" and the words "150 years". Below the effigy of the Grand Duke, the text "Constitution of the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg" and a side section of an open book are shown.
132310.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

147753.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (William I)
147753.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
147753.obverse_description:
The design shows on the right hand the effigy of His Royal Highness, the Grand Duke Henri, looking to the left and on the left hand the effigy of HRH the Grand Duke Guillaume Ist. Between both effigies are depicted vertically the year-dates ‘1772-1843’ as well as the name ‘Guillaume Ier’. At the bottom appears the text
‘LUXEMBOURG’ and the year-date ‘2018’. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
147753.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

157481.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Grand Duchess Charlotte)
157481.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
157481.obverse_description: The effigy of Grand Duke Henri to the left and the effigy of Grand Duchess Charlotte to the right along with the country name at the bottom and the date below, and the inscription Centenaire de l’accession au trône de la Grande-Duchesse Charlotte’ around the effigies around the rim and the 12 stars of Europe in the outer ring
157481.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

180495.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Voting Rights)
180495.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
180495.obverse_description: The design shows on the left hand the effigy of His Royal Highness, the Grand Duke Henri, and a ballot box where a ballot paper is being inserted by a hand to the right and the country name below. At the top, the inscription ‘Centenaire du suffrage universel’ (Centenary of the universal franchise) in a semicircle and the dates ‘1919 - 2019’ below and the second date being the year of issuing. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
180495.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the gathering of the fifteen states of the European Union

243914.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Birth of Prince Charles - hologram version)
243914.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Hologram)

The design depicts at the centre the representation of Prince Charles as well as the effigies of his parents, Their Royal Highnesses the Hereditary Grand Duke Guillaume and the Hereditary Grand Duchess Stephanie. Below these effigies, the text ‘S.A.R. de Prënz Charles’ (His Royal Highness the Prince Charles) is followed by the date of birth ‘10 May 2020’. At the left, the word ‘LËTZEBUERG’ as the designation of the issuing country is depicted vertically. The monogram (letter ‘H’ with a crown) is a representation of the Grand Duke Henri.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
243914.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

277754.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Marriage of Grand Duke Henri - hologram version)
277754.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
277754.obverse_description:
The design depicts the effigies of the Grand Duke Henri and the Grand Duchess Maria Teresa. Below the effigies, two wedding rings are preceded by the wedding date ‘14. FEBRUAR 1981’ and followed by the year-date ‘2021’. The word ‘LETZEBUERG’ designating the issuing country is depicted below.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
277754.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

277753.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Grand Duke Jean - hologram version)
277753.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
277753.obverse_description:
The design depicts on the left hand side the effigy of the Grand Duke Jean and on the right hand side the effigy of the Grand Duke Henri. Close to each effigy, the respective name of the Grand Duke is specified, as well as the year of birth ‘1921’ of the Grand Duke Jean. Below the effigies, an outline of Luxembourg city is represented. At the left, the text ‘GROUSSHERZOGE VU LËTZEBUERG’ designating also the issuing country is depicted in circular form. The year-date ‘2021’ is shown at the bottom right.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
277753.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

258829.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Grand Duke Jean - classic version)
258829.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
258829.obverse_description:
The design depicts on the left hand side the effigy of the Grand Duke Jean and on the right hand side the effigy of the Grand Duke Henri. Close to each effigy, the respective name of the Grand Duke is specified, as well as the year of birth ‘1921’ of the Grand Duke Jean. Below the effigies, an outline of Luxembourg city is represented. At the left, the text ‘GROUSSHERZOGE VU LËTZEBUERG’ designating also the issuing country is depicted in circular form. The year-date ‘2021’ is shown at the bottom right.

331428.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Erasmus Programme)
331428.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
331428.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
331428.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

331472.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (10th Anniversary of the Wedding of the Grand-Ducal couple)
331472.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
331472.obverse_description:
The design depicts the effigies of the Hereditary Grand Duke Guillaume and the Hereditary Grand Duchesse Stéphanie. Close to the effigies, the respective names are depicted in semi-circular form. Two wedding rings appear at the left of the year-date 2022. At the bottom of the design, the word ‘LËTZEBUERG’ designating the issuing country is depicted, as well as the wedding date ‘20.Oktober 2012’. The monogram (letter ‘H’ with a crown) is a representation of the Grand Duke Henri.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag
331472.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.
