360804.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
360804.obverse_description:
The design depicts the effigy of the Grand Duke Henri looking to the right and to the figure ‘25’. Several pictograms of Olympic disciplines are shown close to the effigy. Below the effigy, three intersecting circles are enriching the design. At the bottom right, the text ‘MEMBER VUM INTERNATIONALEN OLYMPESCHE KOMMITEE’ is depicted. The name of the country ‘LËTZEBUERG’ as well as the text ‘GROUSSHERZOG HENRI’ are represented at the left.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.

398220.name: 2 Euros - Henri I (Feierstëppler - classic version)
398220.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
398220.obverse_description:
The design depicts in its centre the representation of the Luxembourg coin issued in 1924 showing a steel industry worker, this representation being integrated in the figure ‘100’ as indication of the anniversary of the first issuance of one of the longest circulating Luxembourg franc coins. The name of the issuing country LÉTZEBUERG as well as the year date 2024 appear at the top of the design.

The bottom of the design depicts the word ‘FEIERSTÉPPLER’, the common and popular designation of this coin. The monogram (letter ‘H’ with a crown) is a representation of Grand Duke Henri. As the producing mint is not yet known, the mint marks might be adapted within the spaceholder.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
398220.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

398222.obverse_description:
The design depicts on the left side the statue of Grand Duke Guillaume II on horseback and on the right side the effigy of Grand Duke Henri looking to the left. The texts ‘GROUSSHERZOGE VU LÉTZEBUERG’ and ‘GUILLAUME II 1792-1849’ are depicted in semicircular form in the upper left part of the design. The year-date ‘2024’ and the figure ‘175’ as indication of the anniversary appear below the effigy of Grand Duke Henri. As the producing mint is not yet known, the mint marks might be adapted within the spaceholder.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
398222.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. 467628.name: 2 Euro - Henri I (Schuman Declaration)
467628.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
467628.obverse_description:
The design depicts at the bottom a hand holding a pencil writing on a piece of paper. The upper part of the pencil ends up in an olive branch, continuing with doves, both being symbols of peace. The doves fly to the horizon showing six stars, representing the six Member States signing later the Treaty establishing the European Coal and Steel Community, for which the Schuman declaration set the basis. The upper part of the design shows the text "DECLARATION SCHUMAN" as well as its date " 09.05.1950 ". Below the number "75" represents the indication of the anniversary of the declaration. The name of the issuing country “LUXEMBOURG” as well as the year of issuance “2025” appear at the right of the design. The monogramme (letter "H" with a crown) is a representation of Grand Duke Henri.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
467628.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

2188.obverse_description: Maltese cross dividing each letter of the country name and the date at the bottom
2188.reverse_description: The face value, next to a map showing the European continent without borders

5072.name: 2 Euros (Economic and Monetary Union)
5072.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5072.obverse_description: Stick figure which merges into the € symbol. Twelve stars of the European Union on outer ring.
5072.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

26196.name: 2 Euros (First elected representatives)
26196.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
26196.obverse_description: The inner part of this coin, the first of a series of five coins to be issued over a period of five years to mark major milestones in Malta’s constitutional history, features a hand inserting a ballot paper into the ballot box to commemorate the election in 1849 of the first Maltese representatives to the Council of Government advising the British Governor of Malta, which had been a British colony since the Treaty of Paris of 1814. The upper right outer edge of the inner part of the coin bears the words “MALTA – First elected representatives 1849”. The 12 stars of the European Union are depicted on the outer ring of the coin.
26196.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28322.name: 2 Euros (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28322.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28322.obverse_description: The world in the form of a euro symbol in the center, showing how the euro has become a global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolize the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28322.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

37037.name: 2 Euros (Majority Representation)
37037.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
37037.obverse_description: The design of the inner part of the coin, which was created by the Maltese artist Gianni Bonnici, shows a jubilant crowd against the background of the Governor’s Palace in Valletta. The words “MALTA – Majority representation 1887” are inscribed along the upper edge of the coin’s inner part, while the year of issue, “2012”, is given at the bottom. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
37037.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

47613.name: 2 Euros (Self-Government)
47613.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
47613.obverse_description: The design shows a map of the Maltese islands and a representation of the population. At the bottom of the design, the year ‘2013’. At the top, in semi-circle, the words ‘MALTA — Self-government 1921’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
47613.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

61125.name: 2 Euros (Independence in 1964)
61125.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
61125.obverse_description: The coin commemorating the Malta Independence constitution of 1964 is the fourth in the series of five coins that commemorate constitutional milestones in Maltese history. By means of the 1964 constitution Malta became an independent nation for the first time after hundreds of years of foreign domination. The national side of the coin shows a detail of the bronze monument commemorating Independence which was designed by the artist Gianni Bonnici in 1989. It shows a young woman, representing Malta, carrying the Maltese flag. At the right, in semi-circle, the inscription ‘MALTA — Independence 1964’ and at the bottom the year of issuance ‘2014’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
61125.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

62582.name: 2 Euros (Malta Police Force)
62582.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
62582.obverse_description: The coin commemorates the 200th anniversary of the Malta Police Force which was set up by means of proclamation XXII of 1814. Thus the Malta Police Force is one of the oldest in Europe. The national side of the coin depicts the badge of the Malta Police Force with the legend ‘200 Years Malta Police Force’ and the dates 1814-2014. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
62582.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

73843.name: 2 Euros (Republic of Malta 1974)
73843.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
73843.obverse_description: The coin is the last in a series of five commemorating Maltese constitutional milestones. Malta was declared a republic on 13 December 1974 following constitutional changes which were agreed upon by a great majority in Malta’s Parliament. The design reproduces a marble tablet affixed to the façade of the Presidential Palace in Valletta to mark Malta’s change from a monarchy to a republic. At the top right, in semi-circle, the inscription ‘MALTA — Republic 1974’. At the bottom, the year ‘2015’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
73843.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

71440.name: 2 Euros (First Flight from Malta)
71440.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
71440.obverse_description: The coin commemorates an important milestone in Maltese aviation history — the 100 years since the first flight from Malta. It was on 13 February 1915 that Captain Kilmer took off from the Grand Harbour on a seaplane that was carried by HMS Ark Royal. The plane landed in the harbour after a 55-minute flight. The coin depicts Captain Kilmer’s seaplane with Senglea Point, a prominent feature in the Grand Harbour, in the background. At the top the inscription ‘FIRST FLIGHT FROM MALTA’ in semi-circle. At the right the years ‘1915-2015’. At the bottom left the inscription ‘100TH ANNIVERSARY’ and at the bottom the initials of the designer ‘NGB’ (Noel Galea Bason). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
71440.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

77307.name: 2 Euros (30 Years of European Union Flag)
77307.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
77307.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in a semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
77307.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

95463.name: 2 Euros (Children and solidarity - Love)
95463.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
95463.obverse_description: This coin is envisaged to commemorate the role of the Malta Community Chest Fund in Society through a programme for secondary school students entitled ‘From Children in Solidarity’. This programme identifies the social role of children in five different areas with the first theme being ‘Solidarity through Love’. This design was created by Ms Sarah Cilia a secondary school student who has expressed this theme through two hands forming a heart shape which displays a representation of the Maltese flag. At the bottom of the design is the inscription ‘MALTA 2016’. Two stylised representations of human bodies are designed on the left wrist and two on the right one. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
95463.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

84920.name: 2 Euros (Ġgantija Temples)
84920.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
84920.obverse_description: The Ġgantija Temples situated on the Island of Gozo. Ġgantija is a megalithic temple complex dating to the Neolithic ages. It is one of the world's oldest free-standing structures, as well as one of the oldest religious structures. Built approximately in the 36th century BC, Ġgantija pre-dates Stonehenge and the Egyptian Pyramids. At the top right the inscription ‘ĠGANTIJA TEMPLES’ and underneath the years ‘3600-3200 BC’. At the bottom left the name of the country of issuance ‘MALTA’ and underneath the year of issuance ‘2016’, flanked by the mint master mark and the mintmark. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
84920.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

113393.name: 2 Euros (Ħaġar Qim Temples)
113393.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
113393.obverse_description: The coin depicts the Ħaġar Qim Temples on thecrest of a ridge overlooking the south-west coast of Malta about two kilometres away from the village of Qrendi. At the top right the inscription ‘Ħaġar Qim TEMPLES’ and underneath the years ‘3600-3200 BC’. At the bottom left the name of the issuing country ‘MALTA’ and underneath the issuing date ‘2017’.
113393.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

127889.name: 2 Euros (The Peace)
127889.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
127889.obverse_description: The coin’s theme centres on the concept of solidarity and peace. The design was created by a school student and depicts two children holding the Maltese flag with the dove of peace flying over them. At the bottom is the name of the issuing country ‘Malta’ and at the right side is the year of issuance ‘2017’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
127889.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

151381.name: 2 Euros (Cultural Heritage)
151381.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
151381.obverse_description: The design was created by a school student and depicts a variety of themes intrinsic to Maltese heritage including: an allusion to the prehistoric temples, which are the world’s oldest free-standing structures, a church dome and spire, which is a characteristic feature of the Maltese town and village skyline and a representation of a traditional Maltese boat carrying a Maltese flag. The name of the issuing country ‘Malta’ is at the top of the inner circle of the coin and the year ‘2018’ at the bottom. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag
151381.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

132944.name: 2 Euros (Mnajdra Temples)
132944.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
132944.obverse_description: The coin depicts the megalithic temples of Menaidra. At the top right are the words "MNAJDRA", "TEMPLES" and "3600-2500 BC", indicating the years of the founding of the Temples. The country of issue "MALTA" is shown at the bottom left and, below, the year of issue "2018". The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
132944.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

182718.name: 2 Euros (Nature and Environment)
182718.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
182718.obverse_description:
The design was created by a school student and depicts a fruit tree and a stylised representation
of the sun. At the bottom left is the name of the issuing country ‘MALTA’ and underneath the year of issuance ‘2019’.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
182718.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

167679.name: 2 Euros (TA’ ĦAĠRAT Temples)
167679.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
167679.obverse_description:
The coin depicts the prehistoric temples of TA’ ĦAĠRAT. At the top right the inscriptions ‘TA’ ĦAĠRAT’, ‘TEMPLES’ and the years ‘3600-3200 BC’ one under the other. At the bottom left the name of the country of issuance ‘MALTA’ and underneath the year of issuance ‘2017’. At the bottom right the initials ‘NGB’ of the artist Noel Galea Bason.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
167679.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

241752.name: 2 Euros (Games)
241752.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
241752.obverse_description: The design depicts a melange of traditional games popular with Maltese children. These include marbles, spinning tops and traditional kite-making and flying. Depicted is also a Maltese version of hop-scotch which is known locally as ‘passju’. Three bees flying in a circle allude to a popular chant sung by Maltese children during play. Included in the design is the name of the issuing country, ‘Malta’, and the year of issue, ‘2020’. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
241752.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

215435.name: 2 Euros (Skorba Temples)
215435.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
215435.obverse_description:
The design features Skorba, a prehistoric temple located near the hamlet of Zebbiegh, in the
northwest of Malta. The complex consists of two temples built side by side. These were built on a much older settlement
remains of which were discovered outside the temple complex. Skorba lacks the monumentality of the other temples on
the Maltese islands. Nevertheless, the site is of utmost importance having enabled archaeologists to set Malta’s prehistoric
cultural sequence and establish that the islands were first inhabited at around 5 000 BC. The inscription ‘SKORBA
TEMPLES 3600-2500 BC’ appears at the top of the design. At the bottom are the name of the issuing country ‘MALTA’
and the year of issuance ‘2020’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
215435.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

300648.name: 2 Euros - (Heroes of the Pandemic)
300648.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
300648.obverse_description:
The design is created by Maria Anna Frisone and depicts two medical professional bracing themselves up to face the unknown. It is meant to extoll the services of these selfless individuals who have been on the forefront of the fight against the COVID-19 pandemic. At the top, in semi-circle, is the inscription ‘HEROES OF THE PANDEMIC’. At the bottom, in semi-circle, is the inscription ‘MALTA – 2021’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
300648.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

309264.name: 2 Euros (Tarxien Temples)
309264.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
309264.obverse_description:
The design depicts a detail of the prehistoric structure. At the top is the inscription ‘TARXIEN TEMPLES 3600-2500 BC’. At the bottom is the name of the issuing country ‘MALTA’ and underneath it is the year of issuance ‘2021’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
309264.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

319136.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
319136.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
319136.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
319136.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. Twelve stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

346677.name: 2 Euros (Hypogeum of Ħal-Saflieni)
346677.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
346677.obverse_description:
The design represents a detail of the prehistoric structure. At the top is the name of the issuing country 'MALTA' and below is the year of issue '2022'. At the bottom is the inscription 'ĦAL-SAFLIENI HYPOGEUM' 4000 – 2500 BC'.
The outer ring of the coin represents the 12 stars of the European flag.
346677.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

346667.name: 2 Euros (Council Resolution on Women, Peace and Security)
346667.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
346667.obverse_description:
The design shows three female faces. From the top left to the bottom left are the inscriptions ‘WOMEN’, ‘PEACE’, ‘SECURITY’, the year of issuance ‘2022’ and the name of the issuing country ‘MALTA’. At the centre, underneath the faces, are the inscriptions ‘UNSCR’ and ‘1325’.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
346667.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

369514.name: 2 Euros (The arrival of the French in Malta)
369514.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
369514.obverse_description:
The design depicts a personification of the French Republic as depicted on the official letterheads of the period. At the left-hand side is the inscription ‘225th ANNIVERSARY’ followed from the top to the right, in semi-circle, by the inscription ‘ARRIVAL OF THE FRENCH IN MALTA’. At the bottom is the year of issuance ‘2023’. The inscriptions ‘Liberté’ (Freedom) and ‘Egalité’ (Equality) are at the left and right side of the image, respectively. At the top left of the inscription ‘Egalité’ are the years ‘1798-2023’.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
369514.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

369187.name: 2 Euros (Nicolaus Copernicus)
369187.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
369187.obverse_description:
The design depicts a profile portrait of Copernicus, together with a stylized heliocentric representation of the universe as proposed by Copernicus. At the top left is the inscription ‘NICOLAUS COPERNICUS 1473 – 1543’ and at the bottom left is the name of the issuing country ‘MALTA’ followed by the year of issuance ‘2023’. Also at the bottom is the signature of the artist Daniela Fusco ‘FUSCO’.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
369187.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

429284.name: 2 Euros (Iċ-Ċittadella)
429284.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
429284.obverse_description: View of the Citadel of Gozo. Name of the citadel above, country name and denomination below.
429284.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

572557.name: 2 Euros (Academy of Athens)
572557.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
572557.obverse_description:
Facade of the Academy of Athens.
The outer ring of the coin represents the twelve stars of the European flag.
572557.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

572553.name: 2 Euros (Exodus of Messolonghi)
572553.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
572553.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

429280.name: 2 Euros (Maltese Honey Bee)
429280.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
429280.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

470466.name: 2 Euro (Mdina)
470466.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
470466.obverse_description: A Cityscape view of the ancient city of Mdina.
470466.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

470460.name: 2 Euro (The Maltese Ox)
470460.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
470460.obverse_description: The Maltese Ox grazing on a field.
470460.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

555125.name: 2 Euros (Valletta)
555125.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
555125.obverse_description: A cityscape view of Valletta, the Capital City of Malta since 1571. The city itself has been inscribed in the UNESCO World Heritage list since 1980, whilst it is the southernmost European Capital, and the smallest Capital in the European Union. Named after the grandmaster Jean De Valette, who laid the foundations of this planned city in 1566.
555125.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

555127.name: 2 Euros (Il-Kelb Tal-Fenek)
555127.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
555127.obverse_description: Il-Kelb tal-Fenek, is the only pure Maltese species of dog, which was traditionally used for hunting. On the coin, the dog can be seen standing with head left, with full posture, as if waiting for the next game falling from the sky.
555127.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

211.name: 2 Euros - Rainier III
211.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
211.obverse_description: The portrait of HSH Prince Rainier III is encircled by the inscription "MONACO" and the twelve stars of Europe
211.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the 15 states of the European Union

5035.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (1st map)
5035.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5035.obverse_description: Stars, state name, prince of Monaco facing right, year

5036.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Grace Kelly)
5036.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5036.obverse_description: The coin features an effigy of Princess Grace, to the right of which appear the word ‘MONACO’, the mint mark, the year ‘2007’ and the engraver’s mark. The name of the artist, ‘R.B.BARON’, is shown under the Princess’s hair. The 12 stars of the European Union are depicted on the outer ring of the coin.
5036.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

15757.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (2nd map)
15757.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
15757.obverse_description: The bust of Albert II facing right with the country name above and the date below
15757.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

24307.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Royal Wedding)
24307.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
24307.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows, at the centre, the effigies of Prince Albert and Princess Charlene. At the bottom, the name of the issuing country ‘MONACO’ and the year of issuance ‘2011’. The mint mark and the mint engravers mark appear before and after ‘MONACO 2011’, respectively. The 12 stars of the European Union are depicted on the coin’s outer ring.
24307.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

35946.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Lucien Grimaldi I)
35946.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
35946.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows a portrait of Lucien I Grimaldi, in profile, facing to the left. The inscription “1512 SOUVERAINETE DE MONACO 2012” forms an arch around the portrait along the outer edge of the inner part of the coin, flanked by two ornamental diamonds, as well as by the horn of plenty (mintmark of Monnaie de Paris) and the “fleurette” (hallmark of the engraving workshop) to the left and right, respectively, at the bottom of the portrait. The founder’s name, “Lucien Ier”, is given above the mintmark. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
35946.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

49385.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Admission to UN)
49385.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
49385.obverse_description: The coin depicts a dove with an olive branch, with in the middle of the arc, on the left side the cornucopia, and on the right side the punch, as the workshop Paris Mint mark. At the top, in semi-circle, the indication of the issuing country ‘MONACO’ and at the bottom, in semi-circle, the inscription ‘1993 ADMISSION À L'ONU 2013’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
49385.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

77312.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Foundation of the Fortress)
77312.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
77312.obverse_description: The design shows a tower on the top of the rock. At the top the name of the issuing country ‘MONACO’ flanked by the Paris mint mark and the mint master mark. At the bottom, the inscription ‘FONDATION DE LA FORTERESSE’ (Foundation of the fortress), flanked by the years ‘1215’ and ‘2015’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
77312.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

87722.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Foundation of Monte Carlo)
87722.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
87722.obverse_description: The design shows CHARLES III within the background MONTE CARLO. At the top is the name of the issuing country ‘MONACO’ flanked by the mint mark and the mint master mark. At the bottom, in semi-circle from left to right is the inscription ‘1866 CHARLES III FONDE MONTE CARLO 2016’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
87722.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

127894.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Prince's Company of Carabiniers)
127894.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
127894.obverse_description:
The design shows a CARABINIER and in the background the Palace of Monaco. At the top is the inscription ‘MONACO’ flanked by the mintmark and the mint master mark. At the bottom are the years ‘1817-2017’ and underneath the inscription ‘CARABINIERS DU PRINCE’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
127894.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

142707.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (250th Anniversary of the birth of François-Joseph Bosio)
142707.composition: Bimetallic: nickel clad nickel brass centre in copper-nickel ring
142707.obverse_description: The design shows Mr. Bosio and a statue as the background image, the country name at the top of the image in a semi-circle. At the bottom, in a semi-circle as well, there are two inscriptions: ‘FRANÇOIS JOSEPH BOSIO’ and underneath ‘1768 – SCULPTEUR - 2018’. At the left and right sides of these two inscriptions, there are the mintmark and the mint master mark. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
142707.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

169095.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Prince Honoré V)
169095.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
169095.obverse_description:
The effigy of Prince Honoré V facing with the inscription ‘HONORÉ V’ to the left and the country name to the right with the words ‘1819 — Avènement — 2019’ in a semicircle at the bottom with the 
12 stars of the European flag in the outer ring
169095.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the gathering of the fifteen states of the European Union

215229.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Prince Honoré III)
215229.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
215229.obverse_description:
The design shows the effigy of Prince Honoré III. At the left is the inscription ‘HONORÉ III’ and
at the right the name of the issuing country ‘MONACO’. At the bottom, in semi-circle, is the inscription
‘1720 – Naissance – 2020’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
215229.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the gathering of the fifteen states of the European Union

303851.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (10th anniversary of the Royal Wedding)
303851.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
303851.obverse_description:
The design shows the profile portraits of Prince Albert II and Princess Charlene. At the top, the name of the issuing country ‘MONACO’ appears. At the bottom, appears in a semi-circle the inscription ‘2011 MARIAGE PRINCIER 2021’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
303851.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

340051.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Prince Albert I of Monaco)

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.

483867.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

472551.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (County of Carladès)

526353.name: 2 Euros - Albert II (Marquisate of Baux)
526353.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
526353.obverse_description: The ruins of the Château des Baux de Provence, with the sun and the Monaco coat of arms above.

152.name: 2 Euros - Beatrix (1st map)
152.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
152.obverse_description: An uncluttered portrait of Queen Beatrix is accompanied, in the right side, facing left with a vertically written inscription above the date
152.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the fifteen states of the European Union

2207.name: 2 Euros - Beatrix (Treaty of Rome)
2207.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2207.obverse_description: The coin shows the Treaty document signed by the six founding countries on a background evoking the paving (designed by Michelangelo) of the Piazza del Campidoglio in Rome, where the signing took place on 25 March 1957. ‘Treaty of Rome 50 years’, ‘EUROPE’, and the name of the issuing country appear in the respective languages of the euro area or in Latin. Thus, the legend differs from country to country, but the image is the same. The Treaty of Rome established the European Economic Community and ultimately led to the introduction of the euro in 1999 and the euro banknotes and coins in 2002. The anniversary was celebrated on 25 March 2007. The euro area countries have marked the occasion by jointly issuing this commemorative coin. Its design was selected following a competition organized by the European mints. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
2207.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

6481.name: 2 Euros - Beatrix (2nd map)
6481.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
6481.obverse_description: An uncluttered portrait of Queen Beatrix, accompanied on the right side with the vertically written inscription "BEATRIX KONINGIN DER NEDERLANDEN" (Beatrix queen of the Netherlands) and encircled by the twelve stars of Europe
6481.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the fifteen states of the European Union

5073.name: 2 Euros - Beatrix (Economic and Monetary Union)
5073.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5073.obverse_description: The coin shows a stick figure which merges into the € symbol. It seeks to convey the idea of the single currency and, by extension, the Economic and Monetary Union (EMU) being the latest step in Europe’s long history of trade and economic integration. The coin is issued by each euro area country and shows the name of the country as well as the legend ‘EMU 1999-2009’ in the respective languag (s). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
5073.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

19021.name: 2 Euros - Beatrix (Erasmus)
19021.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
19021.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows Erasmus writing his book on the right and the effigy of Queen Beatrix to the left. The two images are separated by the vertical inscription "Beatrix Koningin der Nederlanden". The year "2011", the Mint master’s mark and the mintmark are positioned horizontally near the center of the coin. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
19021.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

318981.name: 2 Euros - Beatrix (Erasmus - Pattern)
318981.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
318981.obverse_description: Whole image is larger than in the issued version. The inner part of the coin shows Erasmus writing his book on the right and the effigy of Queen Beatrix to the left. The two images are separated by the vertical inscription "Beatrix Koningin der Nederlanden". The year "2011", the Mint master’s mark and the mintmark are positioned horizontally near the center of the coin. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
318981.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

28327.name: 2 Euros - Beatrix (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28327.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28327.obverse_description: The world with a euro symbol in the centre showing how the euro has become a global currency over the last ten years with the surrounding elements symbolizing the importance of the euro to the ordinary people (represented by the family group), to the financial group (represented by the Eurotower), to trade (represented by a ship), to industry (represented by a factory), and energy, research, and development (represented by two wind turbines). The designer's initials are between the ship and the Eurotower and the country name at the top and the dates "2002-2012" at the bottom. The outer ring depicts the 12 stars of Europe.
28327.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

42418.name: 2 Euros - Beatrix (Investiture of King Willem-Alexander)
42418.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
42418.obverse_description: In the foreground the effigy of Queen Beatrix and on the background, partly covered by the effigy of the Queen, the effigy of the Prince of Orange. The circumscription around both effigies reads WILLEM-ALEXANDER PRINS VAN ORANJE (crown symbol) BEATRIX KONINGIN DER NEDERLANDEN (mintmark) 28 JANUARI 2013 (mint master mark). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
42418.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

50013.name: 2 Euros - Willem-Alexander (200 Years of the Kingdom)
50013.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
50013.obverse_description: The inner part of the design shows a continuing ribbon that starts from the outside depicting the effigy of King Willem-Alexander and continues to depict the effigies of his six predecessors: Queen Beatrix, Queen Juliana, Queen Wilhelmina, King Willem III, King Willem II and King Willem I. The effigies are surrounded by the inscription ‘Willem-Alexander Koning der Nederlanden’ followed by the Royal Dutch mintmark, the mint master mark, the year 2013, the initials of the designer, and at the left side of the effigies the inscription ‘200 Jaar Koninkrijk’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
50013.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

54432.name: 2 Euros - Willem-Alexander (200 Years of the Kingdom; Coloured)
54432.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
54432.obverse_description: The inner part of the design shows a continuing orange ribbon that starts from the outside depicting the effigy of orange colored King Willem-Alexander and continues to depict the effigies of his six predecessors: Queen Beatrix, Queen Juliana, Queen Wilhelmina, King Willem III, King Willem II and King Willem I. The effigies are surrounded by the inscription ‘Willem-Alexander Koning der Nederlanden’ followed by the Royal Dutch mintmark, the mint master mark, the year 2013, the initials of the designer and at the left side of the effigies the inscription ‘200 jaar Koninkrijk’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union coloured in orange.
54432.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

59483.name: 2 Euros - Willem-Alexander (Accession)
59483.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
59483.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin depicts the effigies of both the King Willem-Alexander and the former Queen Beatrix. At the left, in a semi-circle, the inscription ‘WILLEM-ALEXANDER KONING DER NEDERLANDEN’ and at the right, in a semi-circle, the inscription ‘BEATRIX PRINSES DER NEDERLANDEN’. Between the two inscriptions, at the top, the crown symbol and at the bottom the mint master mark, the mint mark and in between the year of issuance ‘2014.’ The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
59483.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

59563.name: 2 Euros - Willem-Alexander (Accession; Coloured)
59563.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
59563.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin depicts the effigies of both the King Willem-Alexander and the former Queen Beatrix. At the left, in semi-circle, the inscription ‘WILLEM-ALEXANDER KONING DER NEDERLANDEN’ and at the right, in semi-circle, the inscription ‘BEATRIX PRINSES DER NEDERLANDEN’. Between the two inscriptions, at the top, the coloured crown symbol and at the bottom the mint master mark, the mint mark and in between the year of issuance ‘2014’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
59563.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

54960.name: 2 Euros - Willem-Alexander (2nd map)
54960.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
54960.obverse_description: Right effigy of the Dutch king Willem-Alexander with the inscriptions "Willem-Alexander Koning der Nederlanden" (Willem-Alexander King of the Netherlands) and the year surrounded by the twelve stars of the European Union.
54960.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders

76641.name: 2 Euros - (30 Years of European Union Flag)
76641.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76641.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in a semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years 1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
76641.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

131803.obverse_description: Portrait of King Willem-Alexander facing right, surrounded by 12 orange coloured stars in the outer ring.
131803.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

377860.name: 2 Euros - Willem-Alexander (The Hague yellow star; Coloured)
377860.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass centre in copper-nickel ring

164656.name: 2 Euros - Willem-Alexander (Maastricht red star; Coloured)
164656.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass centre in copper-nickel ring
164656.obverse_description:
Right effigy of the king of the Netherlands Willem-Alexander with the inscriptions "Willem-Alexander Koning der Nederlanden" (Willem-Alexander king of the Netherlands) and the year.

The outer ring of the coin represents the twelve stars of the European flag. The stars are colored red.
164656.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

331447.name: 2 Euros - Willem-Alexander (Erasmus Program)
331447.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
331447.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus program: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolized by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolized by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary, comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
331447.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

160.obverse_description: The third royal seal of 1144 of Afonso Henriques, the first king of Portugal, is encircled by alternately the seven country's castles and the five coats of arms, which appear an the Portuguese flag, and the letters of the word "PORTUGAL", themselves encircled by the twelve stars of Europe
160.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the gathering of the fifteen states of the European Union

Thus, the legend differs from country to country, but the image is the same. The Treaty of Rome established the European Economic Community and ultimately led to the introduction of the euro in 1999 and the euro banknotes and coins in 2002. The anniversary was celebrated on 25 March 2007. The euro area countries have marked the occasion by jointly issuing this commemorative coin. Its design was selected following a competition organized by the European mints. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
2206.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

2204.name: 2 Euros (Council of the European Union)
2204.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
2204.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin features a cork oak (quercus suber), the arms of Portugal below the lower branches to the left of the trunk, while the country name written over three lines, appears on the right. Inscribed in the form of an arc along the edge of the inner part of the coin below the tree are the artist’s signature (I. Vilar), the year of issue (2007), the mintmark (INCM) and the legend “PRESIDÊNCIA DO CONSELHO DA UE”. The outer ring of the coin bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
2204.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

3911.name: 2 Euros (Human Rights)
3911.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
3911.obverse_description: The coin shows the Portuguese coat of arms at the top, with the name of the issuing country (‘PORTUGAL’) and the year ‘2008’ just below. The lower half of the coin features a geometric design and the legend ‘60 ANOS DA DECLARAÇÃO UNIVERSAL DOS DIREITOS HUMANOS’, followed by the inscription ‘Esc. J. Duarte INCM’ in very small characters. The outer ring of the coin shows the 12 stars of the European Union.
3911.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

9897.name: 2 Euros (2nd map)
9897.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
9897.obverse_description: The third royal seal of 1144 of Afonso Henriques, the first king of Portugal, is encircled by alternately the seven country's castles and the five coats of arms, which appear on the Portuguese flag, and the letters of the word "PORTUGAL", all encircled by the twelve stars of Europe
9897.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders

5074.obverse_description: The coin shows a stick figure which merges into the € symbol. 7639.name: 2 Euros (Lusophony Games)
7639.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
7639.obverse_description: A gymnast swirling a long ribbon and the Portuguese shield at the top together with the name of the issuing country. At the bottom, the legend ‘2.os JOGOS DA LUSOFONIA LISBOA’ is inscribed between the initials ‘INCM’ on the left and the artist's name ‘J. AURÉLIO’ on the right. The year ‘2009’ appears above the gymnast. The coin's outer ring shows the 12 stars of the European Union on a background of concentric circular lines.
7639.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

15315.name: 2 Euros (Portuguese Republic)
15315.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
15315.obverse_description: The coin commemorates the 100th anniversary of the end of the constitutional monarchy of King Manuel II and the establishment of the Portuguese Republic following the revolution of 5 October 1910. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
15315.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

22490.name: 2 Euros (Fernão Mendes Pinto)
22490.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
22490.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin commemorating the 500th anniversary of the birth of Fernão Mendes Pinto, a Portuguese explorer whose book on his discoveries and adventures in the seas and countries of Asia was published posthumously under title “Peregrinação” (Pilgrimage), depicts a sailing ship floating on a sea formed by a series of wave-shaped references to Portugal, Lisbon, his book and some of the destinations of his travels, With the country designation “Portugal” centered beneath them. His name flanked by the dates “1511” and “2011” form a semicircle around the upper edge of the inner part. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
22490.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders

28329.name: 2 Euros (10 Years of Euro Cash)
28329.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
28329.obverse_description: Coin features the world in the form of a euro symbol in the centre, showing how the euro has become a true global player over the last ten years. The surrounding elements symbolise the importance of the euro to ordinary people (represented by a family group), to the financial world (the Eurotower), to trade (a ship), to industry (a factory), and to the energy sector and research and development (two wind turbines). The designer’s initials, “A.H.”, can be found (if you look very carefully) between the ship and the Eurotower. Along the upper and lower edges of the inner part of the coin are, respectively, the country of issue and the years “2002 – 2012”. The coin will be issued by all euro area countries. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
28329.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

34225.name: 2 Euros (Guimarães)
34225.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
34225.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin depicts a stylised image of Afonso Henriques, the first King of Portugal, together with his sword and the Castle of Guimarães, which is regarded as the birthplace of Portugal as an independent nation in the 12th century. To the left of the image is the Portuguese shield above the word “PORTUGAL”. To the lower right is the inscription “GUIMARÃES 2012” with the logo of Guimarães 2012 European Capital of Culture. Along the lower edge of the inner part are the mintmark “INCM” and the name of the designer, “JOSÉ DE GUIMARÃES”. The outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
34225.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders

42726.name: 2 Euros (Porto Clérigos Tower)
42726.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
42726.obverse_description: The tower is depicted as it can be seen by the people on the street when standing close to it, side by side with the typical Oporto skyline as seen from Douro River south bank. At the top, in a semi-circle, the inscription ‘250 ANOS TORRE DOS CLÉRIGOS — 2013’. At the bottom right, the Portuguese coat of arms and underneath the indication of the issuing country ‘PORTUGAL’. At the left, the mint mark and the name of the artist ‘INCM — HUGO MACIEL’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
42726.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

66314.name: 2 Euros (Family Farming)
66314.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
66314.obverse_description: Tools typically used in traditional agriculture in the centre, together with farming products: a chicken in the centre, surrounded by pumpkins, a basket of potatoes, and other vegetables and flowers. On the left side, in a semi-circle, the subject of the commemoration ‘AGRICULTURA FAMILIAR’ (Family Farming) and on the right side, in a semi-circle, the name of the country name followed by the date. At the bottom left the mintmark. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
66314.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

59474.name: 2 Euros (Carnation Revolution)
59474.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
59474.obverse_description: The two curves represent the general shape of a carnation, the flower symbolizing the movement, which was also the origin of the revolution’s name. The name of the issuing country ‘PORTUGAL’ and the Coat of Arms are inscribed on the top of the flower. The center of the image shows the date of the event ‘25 DE ABRIL’ (25th April) and at the bottom is written the number of years past since the revolution ‘40 ANOS’ (40 years) and the year of issuance ‘2014’. The shape of the letters and numbers is inspired by those used in posters and other political information supports 40 years ago, as a symbol of the euphoric period lived right after the event. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
59474.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

74264.name: 2 Euros (Timor)
74264.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
74264.obverse_description: Figure representing a XVI Century Portuguese typical sailing boat (Nau Portuguesa), representing the arrival of the Portuguese navigators to the island, and a local house thatched roof top, including the typical wood sculptures, permanent memories of myths and legends. The one depicted on the coin represents the history of the first inhabitants, who arrived by boat from other parts of the Asian continent and the importance of the horse to travel around the steep mountains which cover most of the island. At the top right, the year ‘1515’ and the name of the issuing country ‘PORTUGAL’. At the bottom left, the inscription ‘TIMOR’ and the year ‘2015’. At the bottom, the signature of the artist Fernando Fonseca.)
74264.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

71656.name: 2 Euros (Portuguese Red Cross)
71656.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
71656.obverse_description: The design depicts a visual composition based on the well-known cross, symbol of the organisation, replicated several times to represent the expansion of the humanitarian action both in Portugal and abroad. The contour of a hand in the background symbolises the different kinds of help given by the organisation to the people, mostly medical, but also and among others: cooperating, constructing and supporting. At the left side, in semicircle, the inscription ‘CRUZ VERMELHA PORTUGUESA’. At the top, the Coat of Arms and the name of the issuing country ‘PORTUGAL’ and at the bottom the years ‘1865’ and ‘2015’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
71656.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

77133.name: 2 Euros (30 Years of European Union Flag)
77133.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
77133.obverse_description: The designs show the European Union flag as a symbol that unites people and cultures with shared visions and ideals for a better common future. Twelve stars that morph into human figures are embracing the birth of a new Europe. At the top right, in semi-circle, are the issuing country and the years ‘1985-2015’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist (Georgios Stamatopoulos). The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
77133.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

87329.name: 2 Euros (25th of April bridge)
87329.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
87329.obverse_description: The design depicts the image of the bridge. At the top right is the inscription ‘PORTUGAL’. At the bottom right are the inscriptions ‘PONTE’, ‘25 DE ABRIL’, ‘1966’, and ‘2016’, one below the other. At the bottom left is the mintmark ‘INCM’ and at the bottom center is the name of the designer ‘JOSÉ AURÉLIO’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
87329.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

84061.name: 2 Euros (Portuguese Olympic Team for Rio 2016)
84061.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
84061.obverse_description: The design shows an image based on the well-known artwork ‘Heart of Viana’, signed by the author Joana Vasconcelos, which was inspired by traditional jewelry from the north of Portugal (the area around the city of Viana do Castelo). It symbolizes the Portuguese people’s support for the national team on the occasion of the Olympics. At the left and the right, in a semi-circle, are the inscriptions ‘JOANA VASCONCELOS’ and ‘EQUIPA OLÍMPICA DE PORTUGAL 2016’, respectively. At the bottom is the mintmark ‘INCM’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
84061.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

105040.name: 2 Euros (150 Years of Public Security)
105040.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
105040.obverse_description: The design shows human figures and buildings, symbolic representations of citizens and cities, where public security is mainly assured, and the simplified police symbol. The legends include the year ‘1867’ and the year of issue ‘2017’, the country of issue ‘PORTUGAL’, the subject of commemoration (‘SEGURANÇA PÚBLICA’ – Public Security), the three main issues related to citizenship (‘DIREITOS’, ‘LIBERDADES’ e ‘GARANTIAS’ – Rights, Freedoms, and Warranties), and the author’s name JOSÉ DE GUIMARÃES. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
105040.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

105042.name: 2 Euros (150 Years of Public Security; Coloured)
105042.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
105042.obverse_description:
The design shows coloured human figures and buildings, symbolic representations of citizens and cities, where the public security is mainly assured and the simplified police symbol. The legends include the year ‘1867’ and the year of issue ‘2017’, the country of issue ‘PORTUGAL’, the subject of commemoration (‘SEGURANÇA PÚBLICA’ – Public Security), the three main issues related to citizenship (‘DIREITOS’, ‘LIBERDADES’ e ‘GARANTIAS’ – Rights, Freedoms and Warranties), and the author’s name JOSÉ DE GUIMARÃES.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
105042.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

117220.name: 2 Euros (Raul Brandão)
117220.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
117220.obverse_description: The design shows the face of Raul Brandão. At the left side is the inscription RAUL BRANDÃO and underneath the years ‘1867’ and ‘2017’. At the bottom right is the inscription of the country of issuance ‘PORTUGAL’ and at the top right is the name of the author ‘LUIS FILIPE DE ABREU’ followed by the mintmark ‘INCM’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
117220.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

133675.name: 2 Euros (Botanical Garden of Ajuda)
133675.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
133675.obverse_description: Canary dragon tree (Dracaena draco) in the Ajuda Botanical Garden (Jardim Botânico da Ajuda) in Lisbon. Above the inscription "250 ANOS JARDIM BOTANICO DA AJUDA" and below the country name "PORTUGAL" and the year "2018".
133675.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

133674.name: 2 Euros (Imprensa Nacional - National Mint)
133674.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
133674.obverse_description: Eight lines of Portuguese text in the centre divided by horizontal lines and the 12 stars of Europe in the outer ring
133674.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

157844.name: 2 Euros (500th anniversary of Magellan Circumnavigation)
157844.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
157844.obverse_description:
The Spanish expedition to the East Indies began in 1519 and ended in 1522, resulting in the
first circumnavigation of the Earth.
The design depicts the effigy of Fernão De Magalhães. At the right side, in semicircle, is the inscription ‘CIRCUM
NAVEGAÇÃO’ (circumnavigation) and underneath the inscription ‘1519 FERNÃO DE MAGALHÃES’. At the left side, in
semicircle, is the year of issuance ‘2019’ and the name of the issuing country ‘PORTUGAL’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
157844.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

157845.name: 2 Euros (600th Anniversary of the discovery of the Madeira archipelago)
157845.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
157845.obverse_description:
The event, known as the first Portuguese territorial discovery, marked a historic milestone in
the consolidation of Portugal as a maritime power and gave rise to the Portuguese Age of Discoveries (1418-1522).
The design depicts the Madeira Archipelago and the island of Porto Santo. In semicircle are the inscriptions ‘600 anos do
Descobrimento da Madeira e de Porto Santo’ and ‘PORTUGAL 2019’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
157845.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

227198.name: 2 Euros (730 years of the University of Coimbra)
227198.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
227198.obverse_description:
The design depicts the tower of University of Coimbra and a visual composition of triangles representing the roofs of the university of Coimbra, the highest is of the Joanina Library within the lettering ‘UNIVERSIDADE DE COIMBRA 730 ANOS PORTUGAL 2020’ (English – UNIVERSITY OF COIMBRA 730 YEARS PORTUGAL 2020).
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
227198.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

197919.name: 2 Euros (75th anniversary of United Nations)
197919.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
197919.obverse_description:
The design depicts the UN logo. At the top is the year of issuance ‘2020’, the name of the
issuing country ‘PORTUGAL’ and the inscription ‘UN 75 YEARS’. Under the words ‘UN’ and ‘YEARS’ is their translation in
Portuguese ‘ONU’ and ‘ANOS’. At the left side is the name of the mint ‘CASA DA MOEDA’ and at the right side is the
engraver’s name ‘ANDRÉ LETRIA’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
197919.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

258832.name: 2 Euros (Presidency European Union)
258832.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
258832.obverse_description:
The design depicts the representation of the link between the capital of Portugal and the others European capitals and thereby draw a map, within the lettering ‘PRESIDÊNCIA DO CONSELHO DA UNIÃO EUROPEIA PORTUGAL 2021’ (English – PRESIDENCY OF THE COUNCIL OF THE EUROPEAN UNION PORTUGAL 2021).
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
258832.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

274850.name: 2 Euros (Tokyo Olympic Games)
274850.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
274850.obverse_description: Depicts the official logo of the Olympic Committee of Portugal surrounded by the inscription "Portugal at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics". Engraver's name on the left and mint name on the right. Around the outer ring the 12 stars of the European Union.
274850.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

321378.name: 2 Euros (Crossing of the South Atlantic by plane)
321378.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
321378.obverse_description:
This crossing was achieved by using only internal means of navigation: a modified sextant and a course corrector. The design shows the representation of the Lusitânia seaplane, one of the three Fairey III biplanes that were used by the commander and pilot Sacadura Cabral and the navigator Gago Coutinho, accomplish the flight between Lisboa and Rio de Janeiro. The edge inscription reads ‘TRAVESSIA DO ATLÂNTICO SUL’. Below the plane, the inscription ‘PORTUGAL 1922-2022’. The Mintmark will be ‘CASA DA MOEDA’, the Portuguese name of the Mint.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
321378.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

327887.name: 2 Euros (Erasmus Programme)
327887.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
327887.obverse_description:
The design is a mix of two major elements of the Erasmus programme: the original intellectual inspiration, Erasmus himself, and the allegory of its influence over Europe. The first one is symbolised by one of the most known depiction of Erasmus. The second one is symbolised by a beam of links going across the coin from a beacon to another, representing the numerous intellectual and human exchanges between the European students. As a reference to Europe, some of these links form other stars, born from the synergy between the countries. The figure 35, for 35th anniversary comes out from the stars in a contemporary graphical style.
The outer ring depicts the twelve stars of the European flag.
327887.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin.
