219.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the gathering of the fifteen states of the European Union.

5080.name: 2 Euros - John Paul II (State anniversary)
5080.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5080.obverse_description: The inner part shows a schematic representation of the perimeter walls of the Vatican City with St Peter's Basilica in the foreground. Also in the inner part are the inscriptions '75 o ANNO DELLO STATO' and '1929-2004' as well as, in smaller letters, the name of the designer 'VEROI' and the initials of the engraver 'L.D.S. INC.'. The outer part of the coin features the twelve stars of the European Union and the inscription 'CITTA' DEL VATICANO'.
5080.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

227.name: 2 Euros - Sede Vacante
227.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
227.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin shows the coat of arms of Cardinal Chamberlain, the interim leader of the Vatican City State, with the emblem of the Apostolic Chamber in the upper part of the design. The words ‘SEDE VACANTE’ form a quasi-semicircle along the upper left edge of the inner part. The year of mintage, ‘MMV’, appears in Roman numerals along the right-hand edge of the inner part. Between the coat of arms and the year of mintage is the mintmark ‘R’. The designer's initial and surname, ‘D. LONGO’, appear on the lower left-hand side along the edge of the inner part. The engraver's initials appear on the lower right-hand side of the design along the edge of the inner part: ‘MCC inc’. Twelve stars in a quasi-semicircle are shown on the upper half of the outer ring. On the lower part of the outer ring the words ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’ form a semicircle.
227.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

5081.name: 2 Euros - Benedict XVI (20th World Youth Day)
5081.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5081.obverse_description: Depicted in the centre of the coin is the Cathedral of Cologne with a comet passing by above. The words ‘XX GIORNATA MONDIALE DELLA GIOVENTÙ’ are inscribed along the upper edge of the central part, separated by the tail of the comet and two of the cathedral’s spires, one of which extends into the coin’s outer ring. Twelve stars are depicted in a semicircle along the upper part of the outer ring, separated at the top by the year of issue (2005) and the mintmark ‘R’. Below the central image of the cathedral, the words ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’ form a semicircle in the outer ring.
5081.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

5082.name: 2 Euros - Benedict XVI (Swiss Guard)
5082.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5082.obverse_description: The coin features a Swiss guard taking an oath of loyalty to the Pope. The inscription ‘GUARDIA SVIZZERA PONTIFICIA’ forms a semi-circle around the guard, while under the flag appears the name of the issuing state, ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’. ‘1506’ appears on the left side, above the signature of the engraver ‘O. ROSSI’ along the flagpole. ‘2006’ appears on the right side, above the mint mark ‘R’. The 12 stars of the European Union are depicted on the outer ring.
5082.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

235.name: 2 Euros - Benedict XVI (1st map)

235.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the fifteen states of the European Union

3910.name: 2 Euros - Benedict XVI (80th Birthday)
3910.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
3910.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin features the profile of His Holiness Benedict XVI facing the left. The legend ‘BENEDICTI XVI P.M. AETATIS ANNO LXXX CITTA' DEL VATICANO’ is engraved along the outer edge of the inner part of the coin. To the left of the effigy are the mintmark, ‘R’, the year of issue, ‘2007’, and the initials of the engraver, ‘M.C.C. INC.’, while the artist’s name, ‘LONGO’, is inscribed on the right. The outer ring of the coin bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
3910.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

5083.name: 2 Euros - Benedict XVI (Saint Paul the Apostle)
5083.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
5083.obverse_description: The coin depicts the conversion of Paul on the road to Damascus, which is visible in the background. Dazzled by a light from the sky, he falls from his rearing horse. To the left of the image is an inscription denoting the issuing country ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’, and to the right the legend ‘ANNO SANCTO PAULO DICATO’, as well as the year ‘2008’, the mint mark ‘R’ and the artist’s name ‘VEROI’. The initials of the engraver ‘L.D.S. INC.’, Luciana De Simoni, appear beneath the image. The outer ring of the coin shows the 12 stars of the European Union.
5083.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

10001.name: 2 Euros - Benedict XVI (2nd map)
10001.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
10001.obverse_description: The three-quarter portrait of His Holiness Pope Benedict XVI is surrounded by the inscription "CITTA' DEL VATICANO" and the date on top and on the side, on his shoulders the inscriptions "D.L." the name of the designer Daniela Longo and "M.C.C INC." the name of Maria Carmela Colaneri the engraver. Around the piece near the listel the twelve stars of the European flag.
10001.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

14914.name: 2 Euros - Benedict XVI (International Year of Astronomy)
14914.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
14914.obverse_description: This coin was issued on the occasion of the International Year of Astronomy. The inner part of the coin depicts an allegory of the birth of the stars and planets together with several astronomical instruments. The design is inspired by Michelangelo's frescoes in the Sistine Chapel. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
14914.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

15840.name: 2 Euros - Benedict XVI (Year for Priests)
15840.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
15840.obverse_description: The coin commemorates the Year for Priests, proclaimed by Pope Benedict XVI to run from of 19 June 2009 to 19 June 2010, that was concluded with an international gathering of priests attended by the pope. The coin features a shepherd pulling a lamb from a lion’s mouth. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
15840.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

24658.name: 2 Euros - Benedict XVI (26th World Youth Day)
24658.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
24658.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin features a number of young people and flags, together with the mintmark “R” and the year “2011”, surrounded, along the upper edge, by the Roman numerals “XXVI” and the letters “G.M.G.”, the abbreviation of Giornata Mondiale della Gioventù (World Youth Day), and by the name of the issuing country “CITTA’ DEL VATICANO” along the lower edge. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
24658.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

38505.name: 2 Euros - Benedict XVI (7th World Meeting of Families)
38505.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
38505.obverse_description: The inner part of the coin features a family, with the Cathedral of Milan, the host city of the Seventh World Meeting of Families, in the background. The event is held every three years to highlight the family as a common human heritage, reflecting the fact that the family is universal and that it contributes to the human element of life everywhere in the world. Inscribed around the family scene are the names of the event and of the Vatican City in Italian and the year “2012”. The mintmark “R” appears between the boy and his mother. The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European Union.
38505.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

46032.name: 2 Euros - Sede Vacante
46032.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
46032.obverse_description: The design features the coat of arms of the Cardinal Camerlengo and above it the symbol of the Apostolic Camera, two small crosses, the words ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’ at the left and ‘SEDE VACANTE MMXIII’ at the right. The coin’s outer ring bears the twelve stars of the European Union.
46032.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

50603.name: 2 Euros - Francis (28th World Youth Day)
50603.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
50603.obverse_description: The design features the most known monument of Rio de Janeiro, the ‘Christ of Corcovado’, and some young people around the statue. At the top, in semi-circle, the inscription ‘XXVIII G.M.G. CITTÀ DEL VATICANO RIO 2013’. At the right, the mintmark ‘R’ and the name of the artist ‘P DANIELE’. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
50603.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

65941.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Berlin Wall)
65941.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
65941.obverse_description: The design depicts some bricks of the partially collapsed Berlin Wall in the foreground, with the wording ‘XXV ANNIVERSARIO DEL CROLLO DEL MURO DI BERLINO 1989 2014’ (25th anniversary of the fall of the Berlin Wall 1989 2014) written within the bricks. An olive branch can be seen in the centre, in a gap between the bricks and a piece of barbed wire, and the Brandenburg Gate appears in the background. At the top, the words ‘CITTA’ DEL VATICANO’ are inscribed. The coin’s outer ring bears the 12 stars of the European Union.
65941.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

58574.name: 2 Euros - Francis
58574.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
58574.obverse_description: The right effigy of Pope Francis surrounded by the inscriptions "CITA' DEL VATICANO" (Vatican City), the letter "R" from the Rome mint, the year, the name "P. DANIELE" by Patrizio Daniel, the designer, on the pope's left shoulder, and on his right shoulder the name of the coin's engraver "MCC" Maria Carmela Colaneri.
58574.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders

76911.name: 2 Euros - Francis (8th World Meeting of Families)
76911.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
76911.obverse_description: The design depicts two families which ideally embrace the whole Earth. At the top, the year of issuance ‘2015’. At the left side, the name of the artist ‘C. Principe’. The mint mark R appears on the arm of one family member at the right. The design is surrounded by the inscription ‘VIII INCONTRO MONDIALE DELLE FAMIGLIE’ (The VIII World Meeting of Families), from the left to the right in semi-circle and ‘CITTA' DEL VATICANO’ at the bottom.
76911.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

87772.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Vatican Gendarmerie)
87772.composition: Bimetallic: copper-nickel clad nickel centre in nickel brass ring
87772.obverse_description: Vatican Guard in front of the Dome of St. Peter’s Basilica. At the top, in semi-circle is the inscription "CORPO DELLA GENDARMERIA 1816-2016". At the bottom, in semi-circle is the name of the issuing country "CITTA' DEL VATICANO". The mint mark "R" appears at the left of the design and the name of the designer "D. Longo" appears at the right.
87772.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

94537.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Jubilee of Mercy)
94537.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
94537.obverse_description:
The national face features St Martin of Tours sharing his cloak with a poor man. The design is surrounded by the inscriptions 'GIUBILEO DELLA MISERICORDIA' at the left and 'CITTÀ DEL VATICANO' at the right, both in semi-circle. At the right there is as well the year of issuance '2016' and at the bottom the mint mark 'R' and the name of the artist 'M. CRISCIOTTI'.
The coin's outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
94537.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

114887.name: 2 Euros - Francis (St. Peter and St. Paul)
114887.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
114887.obverse_description:
The design features Saint Peter and Saint Paul and their symbols, respectively the keys and the sword. At the top is the inscription ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’, in semi-circle. At the bottom, in semi-circle is the inscription ‘1950o DEL MARTIRIO DEI SANTI PIETRO E PAOLO’. At the bottom left is the year of issuance ‘2017’ and at the bottom right is the mint mark ‘R’. Between them is the name of the artist ‘G. TITOTTO’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
114887.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

124385.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Fatima apparitions)
124385.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
124385.obverse_description:
The design features the three young shepherds to whom Mary appeared, on the background the Sanctuary of Fatima. At the top is the inscription 'CITTÀ DEL VATICANO' in semi-circle and right under is the year '1917'. Underneath the year, from left to right, is the inscription 'FATIMA 2017'. At the left side is the mintmark 'R' and at the bottom the name of the designer 'O.ROSSI'.
The coin's outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
124385.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

105616.name: 2 Euros - Francis
105616.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
105616.obverse_description:
The designs feature the coat of arms of the Sovereign of the Vatican City State, Pope Francis. The mintmark 'R' appears at the bottom
left and the year of issuance '2017' at the bottom right.
The coins' outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
105616.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

150440.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Padre Pio)
150440.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
150440.obverse_description:
The design features the right profile of a portrait of Padre Pio. At the top, from left to right, in semi-circle is the inscription of the issuing country ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’. At the bottom is the inscription ‘Padre Pio’ and at its right the mint mark ‘R’. At the left hand of the design is the year ‘1968’ and at the right is the year of issuance ‘2018’. At the bottom left is the name of the artist ‘P.DANIELE’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
150440.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

141147.name: 2 Euros - Francis (European Year of Cultural Heritage)
141147.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
141147.obverse_description:
The design features the Statue of Laocoön and His Sons, also called the Laocoön Group, a fundamental work of the world sculpture, foundational for the Vatican Museums. At the center bottom, the inscription of the issuing country ‘Città del VATICANO’. From left to right, in semi-circle, the inscription ‘ANNO EUROPEO DEL PATRIMONIO CULTURALE’. At the top right the year of issuance ‘2018’ and the mintmark ‘R’. At the bottom right the name of the artist ‘D. LONGO’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
141147.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

179823.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Sistine Chapel)
179823.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
179823.obverse_description:
The design features the Last Judgment in the Sistine Chapel. At the left, in semi-circle is the
inscription of the issuing country ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’. At the right, in semi-circle, are the inscriptions ‘CAPPELLA
SISTINA — FINE DEI RESTAURI’ and ‘1994-2019’. At the right side is the mintmark ‘R’ and at the bottom the name of the
artist ‘D. LONGO’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
179823.reverse_description: A map of the European continent is represented on a dynamic canvas composed of the twelve stars of the European Union.

167210.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Foundation of the Vatican City State)
167210.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
167210.obverse_description:
The design features a portrait of Pope Pius XI (Sovereign of the State in 1929) and the Lateran in Rome. At the top, from left to right, in semi-circle is the inscription of the issuing country ‘STATO DELLA CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’. At the bottom are the years ‘1929’ and ‘2019’ and underneath the name of the artist ‘FUSCO’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
167210.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

214516.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Birth of John Paul II)
214516.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
214516.obverse_description:
The design features Pope John Paul II, the home where He was born and the Basilica near His
home in Wadowice, Poland. At the top left, in semi-circle is the inscription ‘PAPA GIOVANNI PAOLO II’. At the right are
the years ‘1920 2020’, and the mintmark ‘R’. At the bottom, in semi-circle, is the name of the issuing country ‘CITTA’ DEL
VATICANO’. Under the image of the Basilica are the artist’s name ‘G. TITOTTO’ and the engraver’s initials ‘M.A.C. INC’
 (Maria Angela Cassol, engraver). 
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag
214516.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

236691.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Raphael Sanzio)
236691.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
236691.obverse_description:
The design features a portrait of Raphael and two angels. At the right, in semi-circle, is the
inscription ‘RAFFAELLO SANZIO’. Above the image of the two angels are the years ‘1520’ and ‘2020’ and underneath it is
the name of the issuing country ‘CITTA’ DEL VATICANO’. At the bottom is the mintmark ‘R’ and the artist’s name ‘D.
LONGO’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
236691.reverse_description: A map, next to the face value, shows the European continent without borders.

297633.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Caravaggio)
297633.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
297633.obverse_description:
The design features a boy with a basket of fruits, painted by Caravaggio. At the top, in semicircle, is the name of the issuing country ‘CITTA’ DEL VATICANO’. At the left is the mintmark ‘R’. At the bottom is the inscription ‘CARAVAGGIO’ and underneath are the years ‘1571-2021’. At the bottom right are the initials of the artist Chiara Principe, ‘CP’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
297633.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

307109.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Dante Alighieri)
307109.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
307109.obverse_description:
The design features a portrait of Dante and Palazzo Vecchio in Florence. At the top right, in semi-circle, is the name of the issuing country ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’. At the bottom is the inscription ‘Dante’ and underneath are the years ‘1321-2021’ and the name of the artist ‘P. DANIELE’.
The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
307109.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

335772.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Mother Teresa of Calcutta)
335772.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni. Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni)
335772.obverse_description:
The design features a portrait of Mother Teresa with a child. At the top, in semi-circle, is the inscription ‘MADRE TERESA DI CALCUTTA’ and at the bottom is the name of the issuing country ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’. At the right of the portrait is the mintmark ‘R’ and underneath it are the years ‘1997’ and ‘2022’.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
335772.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

335775.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Paul VI)
335775.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni. Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni)
335775.obverse_description: On the coin we see the Pope smiling and opening his arms wide with the gesture he often used when greeting the crowds. He wears the cassock tied at the waist with a fascia bearing the coat of arms of his pontificate embroidered on the bottom, his cape and cloak.
335775.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

365224.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Alessandro Manzoni)
365224.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
365224.obverse_description:
The design features Manzoni’s portrait, a pen and the beginning of his masterpiece, ‘I Promessi Sposi’. At the top, from left to right, in semi-circle, is the inscription ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’. At the bottom is the inscription ‘Alessandro Manzoni’ and underneath it the dates ‘1873◦2023’. At the left is the mintmark ‘R’.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
365224.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

365229.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Pietro Perugino)
365229.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
365229.obverse_description:
The design features Perugino’s portrait and a particular of the Baptism, fresco in the Sistine Chapel in the Vatican. At the top right, in semi-circle, is the inscription ‘CITTÀ DEL VATICANO’. At the bottom is the inscription ‘PERUGINO’ and underneath it the dates ‘1523◦2023’. At the left is the mintmark ‘R’.

The coin’s outer ring depicts the 12 stars of the European flag.
365229.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

421091.name: 2 Euros - Francis (St. Thomas Aquinas)
421091.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
421091.obverse_description: Bust of Doctor Angelicus Thomas Aquinas
421091.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

431862.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Guglielmo Marconi)
431862.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
431862.obverse_description:
Portrait of Marconi in center, Pope Pius XI at the right, and future Pius XII at the left, above a radio wave. 
Twelve stars of the European flag surround the center on the outer ring.
431862.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

476025.name: 2 Euros - Sede Vacante
476025.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring
476025.obverse_description: The coat of arms of Cardinal Kevin Farrell, Acting Head of State of Vatican City State, bearing the emblem of the Apostolic Chamber at the top, is surrounded by the inscription "SEDE VACANTE" (the seat being vacant) and the date in Roman numerals "MMXXV" (2025), followed by the inscription "CITTA' DEL VATICANO" (Vatican City). The outer ring of the coin features the twelve stars of the European flag.
476025.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

565516.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Michelangelo Buonarroti)
565516.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
565516.obverse_description: The dome of St. Peter's Basilica in the Vatican, designed by Michelangelo. Below it is a reference to the structure of the Sistine Chapel ceiling, frescoed by him. On the left is the artist's handwritten signature, "Michelagniolo," reproduced from original documents.
565516.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.

565521.name: 2 Euros - Francis (Holy Year)
565521.composition: Bimetallic: nickel brass clad nickel centre in copper-nickel ring (Core: 75% Cu, 20% Zn, 5% Ni Ring: 75% Cu, 25% Ni)
565521.obverse_description: Pope Francis opening with his hands the Holy Door of St. Peter's Basilica.
565521.reverse_description: A geographical map of Western Europe spans the outer ring and inner core on the right side of the coin. The inscription 2 EURO is superimposed over the map of Europe, with the numeral “2” located in an open field representing the eastern Atlantic Ocean. 12 stars are located on the right side of the outer ring, with six stars atop the map of Europe and six stars below it; six vertical stripes cut across the inner core of the coin, visually connecting the upper and lower star segments.
